# Thread: What's up light fam!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Asad Choudhry — 2017-06-07T21:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215577>

Hi,

New to the forum. Looking forward to being part of the change this world needs so badly. The best part is that instead of focusing in on social issues if we deal with biology first a lot of social ills can be resolved. I been reading Dr. K's blog for like a year or more, but in the last 3 months I have decided that despite the breadth of material I will read all of it including all the citations , already its helped me build a more complete picture of what is going on in our mind/body's. Don't be discouraged if it doesn't sink it, it eventually does sink in. This is coming from a math and physics undergrad and MD so be patient and keep at it. It takes a while.

I sucked at medicine don't get me wrong I studied hard and got the 99% percentile on my mcats and had very solid board scores, but the medicine that I was taught was dead/lifeless, and the practice of it was even worse. You are a glorified secretary at this point, I'm terrible at multi-tasking always have been and the physician of today is supposed to mainly be good at multi-tasking.

Not good at asking questions,
Not good at thinking
Not good at pondering interdisciplanary knowledge connections
Not good at talking and deeply connecting with patients interpersonally.

I quit residency partially because the system was environmentally / and emotionally toxic but also because I wasn't good enough. I would never be good at killing , boxing , and packaging my patients out the door in 15 minutes, while making sure everything I did was up to date with the gold standard guidelines, making sure I didn't forget any screenings/vaccinations they had coming up, typing and staring at a screen for 90% of my patient encounter, and then typing and staring at a screen after the encounter.

It was stupid no offense to practicing physicians you are stronger willed than I am so I have a lot of respect, but it really was stupid. They make it so doctors can't think deeply about problems, its probably setup that way on purpose. Thinking slowly doesn't make money.

### caroline — 2017-06-08T01:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215595>

Hi Asad and welcome. That is a heartbreaking story about what our wonderful Doctors are expected to do instead of what they wanted to do - Heal.

What do you do now?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-08T02:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215598>

Welcome. It was good to meet you face to face in Vermont. Love your avatar.

### Asad Choudhry — 2017-06-08T06:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215610>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=215595)
>
> Hi Asad and welcome. That is a heartbreaking story about what our wonderful Doctors are expected to do instead of what they wanted to do - Heal.
>
> What do you do now?

Thanks Caroline,

I'm working with three startups based out of SF but I think I'm making a change pretty soon. My body is requiring it if I want to have any semblance of a quality of life lol.
Its would be a heartbreaking story if I wasnt already starting to understand the deeper principles of water I had gotten into Gilbert ling and gerald pollacks work and really taught me the whole edifice of modern research in medicine is in trouble if this is true. I am happy I gained the knowledge I gained and happy to drop the useless components of that knowledge as I reconnect with the art of healing.

### Asad Choudhry — 2017-06-08T06:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215611>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=215598)
>
> Welcome. It was good to meet you face to face in Vermont. Love your avatar.

Thanks Dr. K I am glad I made the trek to meet you I knew I had to, thanks for taking the time to answer my questions, jumped in the lake twice haha.

### caroline — 2017-06-08T09:33:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215612>

Have you read Andrew Marino's book? going somewhere: truth about a life in science

### Asad Choudhry — 2017-06-09T03:11:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215655>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=215612)
>
> Have you read Andrew Marino's book? going somewhere: truth about a life in science

No I havent yet Caroline , its been ordered along with couple other Dr. K books, i need john ott's stuff and nick lanes stuff as well. The rest I had from before.

### ObstreperousPolarBear — 2018-07-02T05:13:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-244735>

Always good to see other docs finding this work. Especially interested to see how those of you with math/physics backgrounds handle the material. I feel like I am drinking from the firehose again. Couldn't agree more re: how medicine is flawed and getting more flawed. I struggled with the same issues you mentioned in residency.

I'm curious what your next career move would be... obviously, out of SF is only a partial answer.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-02T16:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-244752>

The more time I spend around academics, I note that when a message offends people in positions of power, one often observes unconventional tactics used to demean the work or person, which evade the thorny issues of engaging in the argument. Academia is devolving. The idea that "science is settled" and that opposing views are met with personal attacks & censorship, rather than arguing ideas on their merit, is the antithesis of science.

# Thread: Getting naked in nature, freeing yourself and healing

**Subforum:** The Cave

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-naked-in-nature-freeing-yourself-and-healing.19616/post-215711>

Personally, it's a must that I spend time in nature naked on a daily basis. I know that this is hard for many to do, based on their societal beliefs that they have been conditioned with. I'm thankful that I never was one to follow the herd and basically have marched to the beat of my own drum my entire life. Getting naked in Nature was easy for me and now, it's just so natural to be one with it and I won't go a day without it . Please enjoy this young man's TED Talk!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-naked-in-nature-freeing-yourself-and-healing.19616/post-215726>

Going to a "clothing optional" beach worked for me. It was difficult at first .....but necessary!

but I soon discovered that being topless on any beach, in a somewhat secluded area, worked wonders .....I didn't really care any more about what others saw or thought.
I needed to do it for me and my hormones and my well being.

We now always take a cold shower and then sit on our deck and expose the necessary bits!

The first time I told my partner to get his "junk"in the sun was a Kodak moment to be sure!

it is very freeing Rose!

all i can say was from that first moment in my hammock naked in the sun..... not to be dramatic - changed my life - but in a way that's not quite obvious to casual observer... i do feel so much better now - and i did graduate to outside the hammock...

someone commented to me a couple of days ago - someone who i hadn't seen in over a year that I seemed so much happier... that it looked like I had lost weight (not really) - they almost didn't recognize me... (wow really) but in good way

Barefoot just comes so naturally to me now, just like looking at the morning sun to start my hormone panel for the day. I love the feeling of feeling unconfined by clothing and shoes and walls. I just feel very strong, happy and calm, at peace.

I think about how many women could make their transition through menopause like this, just by changing their environment and being true to Nature. They won't find this in any kind of pill, or supplement......

### Technosmith — 2017-06-11T13:34:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-215803>

Hi folks,

Not sure if Jack responds directly to these posts so I will direct this question to the masses but hope that potentially he will step in and comment alongside other well-informed members of the forum.

Do we know what Jack's current perspective is on raw food? It would seem that raw food is much more electron dense, better for the microbiome, and natural but Traditional Chinese Medicine suggests that raw food is too 'Yin' and can unbalance the body. Some alternative practitioners feel thsi is a key aspect of dietary recommendations.

Jack, do you have a scientific explanation to 'debunk' this concept or is it potentially a valid recommendation in relation to healing?

Finally, does he still feel that vegetables are not that important in the diet?

Fully appreciate that the environment is the key over-riding factor but would be good to get his current take on these questions?

Thanks,

Phil

I love my raw wild oysters..... they are my medicine!
I eat most part of my food raw. I love it. Sometime i do want a steak cooked rare, or some lightly cooked seafood (non fatty stuff), but honestly I have to say, I have my best energy and mood when I eat raw animal foods!
I think the viruses and bacteria in the food are benefiting me...
Also, it tastes better
And get less nnEMF  (except if you cook with gas, but I do not own a gas stove)
and making a meal get way faster = saving time

But if I feel like doing a fish soup, or a cooked mussel dish I do!

I do not eat much veggies at all. I do love my fresh picked nettle smoothie or wild edibles tho

up north you need more.. when you have lots of nnEMF you need more....

and what to cook also differs... Seafood has most benefits eaten raw

### drezy — 2017-06-12T18:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-215970>

Jack really tries to lay out the facts so that we can make our conclusions. Once in a while I'm sure it gets tiring and he belts it out in no uncertain terms.

> If your redox is good......you can eat shit on a shingle.

From: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...estion-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/#post-214013](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/#post-214013)

Maybe demystify your perception of Traditional Chinese Medicine a bit. Think them as just early biohackers.

Why do you ever have to biohack? It's because you've become uncoupled.

I trust but verify Jack's comments. In the past if I ate one piece of bread I would get stomach aches, the craps while even gaining weight, and really bad skin breakouts for days.

I'm all "What the what?!? Crazy doc up in here telling me 'shit on a shingle'? I'll prove him wrong!"

4 light, water, & magnetism months later I find the biggest and most shitty GMO burger with all kinds of toppings with the least Ying and yang you've ever seen with GMO wheat coated onion rings stacked in there too. I threw in Large onion rings on the side too. To make it the shittiest of "shingles" I had a 16oz cheap beer afterward. That beer with this meal would be a crippler and guarantee me I'd be working from the crapper pretty much all day the next day.

Results:
My nicely coupled and more diverse micro biome laughed it right off. Like a costume party they even got to pretend for one night they were Redneck microbiota. HooooWeee! It was lolz for them! I also lost 2 lbs when normally would have gained 3-4 assumedly in inflammation.

What changed? My environment/coupling. (In the same period I cut workouts and any supplementation to really try to break doc's theory)

DHA, have a very long half life, little goes long way.
Test what you have then make decision.
Too much of a good stuff in one place depresses another good stuff in another place.
Optimal is the word.
From those that did the testing and posted here there was one girl that had, have low DHA.

DHEA, the desired limits that I have seen and posted should have added caveat.
Monitor your estrogens and then decide, if not often at least from time to time.

hahaha you actually are the funniest motherfucker on this forum...

Agree with everything you just said though... People put food in their mouth, have a reaction, and blame the food. Makes logical sense. But why is the reaction occurring, is it the food or are your mitochondria struggling because of something else, like nnEMF?

This last week, after I watched the Wild Alaska BBC one show with the bears, and hearing they eat 40kg of fish and 40kg of fruits and grass and all kinds of sugary shit a day, I've decided I want to be a bear cause they are cool as fuck.

I've been eating rice, bread, cakes, Biscuits, anything I can shove down my gob that tastes good - and plenty of meat and seafood.

I've waited for a bad reaction, to the sugar, but nothing... It has been really really liberating, and it has finally unlocked me (FULLY - I was already half way there) from the chains of the Food Guru bullshit. I have kept the seafood in take up to at least once a day, but apart from that - Ketosis or Carb diet doesn't seem to matter for my energy anymore... In fact, I feel better with the carbs right now, probably because it's summer.

For someone with diagnosed with Crohn's, being told you can't eat "bread, sugar, gluten etc. etc.", it's absolutely amazing to get to this point and truly not give a fuck about eating McDonalds or Sugar anymore, and not being the guy in the office who has to say no to a biscuit to stay in Ketosis...

Bravo Dr Kruse, Bra- fucking-vo.

### kris90 — 2017-07-28T14:26:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-220291>

Definitely finding the same. When you can increase your quantum yield, food doesn't matter. When you've got the DC electric current running strong throughout your body, you can do whatever you want, and cool shit happens.

I treat myself a lot in the summer. I don't crave foods per se, probably because my B-endorphin is through the roof with all the sun I've been getting, but I definitely have been pigging out lately. As long as I get lots of sun, ground, CT and some seafood, those electrons just move so damn fast, and it's impossible for me to put on fat. I've dropped to 9% bodyfat (may even being approaching 8% now) from 10-11% around spring, and I've been having a lot of fun with my eating and food intake. I'm not even really hungry, but summer is all about pigouts with family and friends.

When you can use sunlight to convert food electrons into useable energy, you don't have a requirement to store fat. It's like Jack says, calories only matter when you are leptin resistant, because you lose your ability to handle food electrons.

### drezy — 2017-07-28T16:33:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-220300>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=220290)
>
> hahaha you actually are the funniest motherfucker on this forum...

Wow. That is a high honor for me. The ability to make someone in another place and at another time laugh is invaluable to me. Especially given the immensity of the firehose we are all in the process of drinking from here. It can be daunting.

I think laughter/humor may be a way to actually accelerate change in a mind like a catalyst. Kings kept a jester around often to serve as a cathartic prop for subjects that otherwise might revolt, but also be informed himself in a less threatening way. Also I think you mentioned you're married. If your spouse does something annoying you can either have a big fight about it or simply poke a little fun of it. The latter technique can lead to peaceful change.

I even think women with their thinner myelin have groomed men not just to be provider/protectors but also comedians since during laughing gals let their guard down. It's like the first intoxicant. I've gone at least a day without being raunchy around here so think about that thing hanging between our legs. We've all been to that party where either out of drunken volition or prank, some guy's shorts get pulled down. It slays the whole room with laughter. I suggest that even the most comedically challenged guy has himself a mini comedian kit right there. There's gotta be a a quantum tie in there somewhere...

Hey, I'm really glad to hear that you have come such a long way in your health and see the results of your higher redox and decoupled from beliefs that would have kept you ill. For the record I've since kept it epi-paleo, but like you, love knowing at social events I don't really have to pay attention to whatever is being served.

### Sean Waters — 2017-07-28T16:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-220302>

For the record I've since kept it epi-paleo, but like you, love knowing at social events I don't really have to pay attention to whatever is being served.

Honestly a lot of your posts actually make me laugh out loud... which is really hard to do on the subject of disease and being optimal. Science isn't associated with humour, naturally. But like you I think it is so important... The more optimal I am, the more I laugh... that's one thing I've noticed was a big change from getting better.

I'm not married but nearly at the 1 year mark with my partner who I adore, mainly because we spend all of our time together laughing - and when we don't, one of us isn't feeling so well - and so we try and get each other better to get back to that place again.

Laughter in relationships is absolutely pivotal, which based on the above, means being optimal is absolutely pivotal - and the only way it can truly last. I think Dopamine is the quantum tie, whenever I get out of an Ice Bath I am literally giggling like a 10 year old and making jokes with my girlfriend, full of energy and flirting like you do in the "honey moon stage" of a relationship. Which is why I always CT and get AM sun, because it directly affects my relationship. 1 year later, we are still in the honey moon period, purely because of Dopamine.

Yeah I think epi paleo is more important the more uncoupled you are, and I do bring home the DHA everyday... but once you are in the high Redox zone... like Phelp's, who apparently ate 10 pizzas a day during training for the Olympics, or Wim Hof who eats spaghetti and drinks beer after his ice baths, I think it matters not what fuel comes in... if you are a Ferrari, you are a Ferrari.

> What we found was that when we were doing CT regularly, any food binges resulted in more bodyheat - Leptin sensitivity at work burning off excess fuel.

Exactly. The excess energy is released as heat when our cell has reached its capacity for energy requirements. It's the exact opposite of conventional wisdom that excess energy is stored as fat.

Turns out, body fat is a "backup" system humans use to re-capture lost energy that could not be used inside the cell, so fat gain occurs as energy flow slows, and if you keep electron tunneling speeds high, you can eat very little food and maintain an energy balance (tightly coupled), or you can uncouple and release excess energy as heat. That's why I stopped counting calories, carbs, and focus on doing things that keep me tightly coupled: 1) sunlight, 2) CT, 3) Fasting/high fat diet and 4) the right kind of exercise. You do that, you eat whatever you want, when you want, and life becomes more fun.

# Thread: allergy to cinnamaldehyde and other spices
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergy-to-cinnamaldehyde-and-other-spices.19630/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### Sunny1966 — 2017-06-12T13:46:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergy-to-cinnamaldehyde-and-other-spices.19630/post-215903>

Last week I have been diagnosed with a cinnamaldehyde allergy and a fragrance mix 1 allergy by a skin prick test. In my puberty I had already been told to avoid spicy meals, chocolate, pork and a lot more foods, but as years went by I forgot about it more or less and my alergies got much better, until I was affected by hormonal birth control based on synthetic progesterone in an IUD. The last few years I noticed that many spices give me a hormonal reaction and I still had to be careful with spices. Synthetic progesterone in birth control is derived from testosterone and testosterone is derived from cinnamaldehyde. What is the relationship with light, water and magnetism to spices and what can I do to improve my allergy to spices?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-12T14:46:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergy-to-cinnamaldehyde-and-other-spices.19630/post-215909>

You have a redox problem........sunlight build redox fastest because it rebuilds the sea inside of a cell by allowing mitochondria to make water.

### JanSz — 2017-06-12T14:59:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergy-to-cinnamaldehyde-and-other-spices.19630/post-215911>

> [Sunny1966 said:](/goto/post?id=215903)
>
> Last week I have been diagnosed with a cinnamaldehyde allergy and a fragrance mix 1 allergy by a skin prick test. In my puberty I had already been told to avoid spicy meals, chocolate, pork and a lot more foods, but as years went by I forgot about it more or less and my alergies got much better, until I was affected by hormonal birth control based on synthetic progesterone in an IUD. The last few years I noticed that many spices give me a hormonal reaction and I still had to be careful with spices. Synthetic progesterone in birth control is derived from testosterone and testosterone is derived from cinnamaldehyde. What is the relationship with light, water and magnetism to spices and what can I do to improve my allergy to spices?

At your present location take 2-3 months of intensive skin sun conditioning.
Take two months vacations real near equator.
Go there with a bag of cinnamaldehyde.
On second month of vacation eat cinnamaldehyde.
See how that affect you.
If you get no reaction, you know where you want to spend the rest of your life.

\---------------------------------

cinnamaldehyde
Cinnamaldehyde is an organic compound with the formula C6H5CH=CHCHO. Occurring naturally as predominately the trans isomer, it gives cinnamon its flavor and odor.

...........

Thanks Jack, sun feels indeed healing to me. I will do my best to get as much sun as possible. Two months before the skin prick test I was not allowed to get sun exposure otherwise the results would not be accurate, so it makes a lot of sense to heal with sunshine. I did not have all these weird allergies for many years, until I got this hormonal IUD. I have read somewhere (in the chapter on Melasma I guess) that you wrote we cannot take any supplements or herbs in order to heal. I guess for me it will include spices on my food as well.

Thanks for the advice Jan! Unfortunately there is no possibility for me to spend two months near the equator, I will spend as much time outside as possible and go to the beach for a swim as much as possible. I will spend 3 weeks in the south of Italy in July . I am not sure I am willing to take the risk for a bag of cinnamaldehyde. It is about everywhere and difficult to avoid. I started to eat organic food last year only to find out now that cinnamaldehyde is allowed to spray as a fungicide on most of the organic vegetables and fruits. It also is in most personal care products. So I am sure I will get a dose of Cinnamaldehyde anyway every day and therefore it might be the cause of my slow healing process.

### JanSz — 2017-06-12T23:41:38+0300

Do not supplement with vit D.
Eat sulfur containing food, onions, garlic...
Spend max possible amount of time on the sun, exposing max skin, breasts, inside mouth, inside vj, anus.
Do not shower right after sun exposure.
On end of the summer test your vit D.
You should have:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
Twice that is ok too.

Likely you can't get there in Holland.
Add to your sun exposure
twice daily 16 minute exposure to Red Sperti
while almost touching the lamp.

In September 2016 I was measured at the endocrinologist and again I was 1.76 cm. I have always been 1.74 and was measured during prenancies etc. So this was a real surprise that I had actually grown taller at my age. In December 2015 and September 2016 my hormones were a complete mess. I secrete very high progesterone in my 24 hour urine test, very high cortisol, too high DHEA, too high protein in my urine, SHBG and IGF1 too high. I developed a severe frozen shoulder in December 2015 and it cleared after I stopped eating pork and tomatoes in November 2016 and started to apply topical magnesium. I feel much better now with my hormones, but havent tested anything, only I started to get a rash around my ankles in February, so I got the skin prick test and I think it is a useful piece of my healing puzzle. # Thread: coconut habanero/chipotle cream sauce for fish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Coldsnow — 2017-06-13T02:09:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/post-216023>

This is bomb for a fish tacos - I don't specify amounts because intuition I think is crucial
in the kitchen and creates the real magic. (oh I hope this fits into the Epi paleo RX; I think habanero's do)

\-coconut cream (native forest is best)
\- habanero diced thin - go easy and take seeds out
\- chipotle - de seeded
\- ginger (lots)
\- lime juice and skin
\- turmeric - diced
\- sea salt
\- bone broth powder

put in little bullet blender and mix till creamy - great for little dip with chips or
on pescado. Great mayo alternative

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-13T02:35:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/post-216029>

Sounds quite good. I am going to build it Friday

### caroline — 2017-06-13T03:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/post-216035>

sounds great - thanks for posting Coldsnow.

I have found that I can use my garlic press with fresh numeric ......

bone broth powder = gelatine?

### Coldsnow — 2017-06-13T22:29:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/post-216098>

great idea with garlic press; yes indeed

the bone broth powder firms it up a bit kind of like throwing file in a gumbo
but on the healthy spectrum

### freeheartbk — 2017-06-13T19:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-pregnenolone.19636/post-216084>

Hi all! I have chronic lyme and chronic active EBV, my hormones are totally trashed. Saliva test about a year ago showed very low cortisol throughout the day (as well as low estrogen and even lower progesterone) so I started taking hydrocortisone, which has helped a bit with energy.

I recently had other hormones checked as well and the results were terrible: DHEA-s at 16.5, Pregnenolone is 19 and Testosterone is less than 3. I just ordered Pure Encapsulations DHEA and Pregnenolone and am very excited to start taking them, but am a bit concerned about combining with the hydrocortisone. How would you suggested I titrate the dosages and possibly wean off the HC while going up on the DHEA and Pregnenolone?

### CatherineRaye — 2018-09-15T02:18:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-what-says-you-toxic-necessary-evil.19637/post-251008>

We are 100% non-vax, and perfectly happy and comfortable with it. Before I was pregnant with our oldest, a vaccine developer's relative warned me off, so I started researching. He told her never to let anyone she cared about get Gardasil, so she said she didn't even know why she was telling me this because I was too old to get the shot, but repeated his warning to me. That set me off researching.

Our oldest caught whooping cough at about 7 months old. It was scary, but basically we just kept her comfortable and I nursed her non-stop, which I have always nursed them on demand until they choose to wean, anyhow. She was a lot better after about a week, and the residual cough gone within another two weeks. She had also had H1N1 about 4 months before that, and she has never caught another flu since.

We use nosodes, and are perfectly comfortable with that, and have had no problems. People in North America and the UK tend to think they are strange, but in France and Switzerland, they are considered totally normal. You get them from the corner pharmacist, who is trained in pharmaceuticals, herbs, essential oils, and homeopathy. Especially in Switzerland, because pharmacists are so highly trained (and holistically), when one is sick, they usually go to the pharmacist, and only go to a medical doctor if the pharmacist isn't able to assist them first.

My big thing is protecting them from people who are vaxxed and shedding while they are really small.

We have the occasional little bumps in the road, but overall, they are far healthier than most other children. We also do not have wifi or cell phones or a microwave or a (not-so) "smart" meter. We feed them as healthy as possible, but allow them junk once in a while. With the exception of the first year or so of our oldest life, we have only lived in places without fluoride in the water. We homeschool, so they have unlimited outside time to play. All of these are factors, but I feel that vaccines are a big factor in damaging children's developing immune system. Even ignoring all the toxins in the shots, it isn't natural to expose someone to an illness by injection. That is my big ol' vaccine rant.

Regardless, I think the most important thing is for mothers and fathers to exercise their rights to make their own choices for their families.

So had to find my own ways to heal myself. I had hyper and hypo symptoms. It was the wors time of my life. I have healed alot since but still need more healing. When I do get these migraines which resently started back up because for a while I was doing ok until menop and with having only one ovary and that is when other things started to change. Today I took one aleve ( which at first increases the pain) what help the most is an ice pack and sleep. I missed up my leptin rx because all I craved was milk/ ice cream , the thought of protein or even veggies made me nauseaous. Okay going to shut down for the night.

### kris90 — 2017-06-16T20:19:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thank-you-dr-kruse-and-kris90.19639/post-216361>

So the CT is important because it lowers leptin by releasing fatty acids from adipose tissue. Basically, forces fat burning, because electrons from fat will release more heat/light. CT can help get DHA into your cell membranes so you can assimilate light better in your eye. The more light you can assimilate, the less mass you require to make energy (think E=mc2). But you also have to eat seafood in order to provide the DHA. Don't use fish oil pills, get it directly from food.

>
> Thank you Kris

Yes, CT can definitely reduce your carb cravings as you transition into a fat burner. Being a fat burner destroys hunger, because whether you eat or not, your body is constantly tapping into an energy source: fat. Either from your diet (when you eat) or from your adipose tissue. That's how you lose fat and destroy hunger simultaneously by feeding your body itself lol.

As for morning, 4:30 is quite early. Where do you live? Is the sun out when you wake up? If not, I'd recommend not eating until the sun is up, even if you wake up before sunrise.

### kris90 — 2017-06-16T21:44:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thank-you-dr-kruse-and-kris90.19639/post-216369>

Ok, so yes, that is a good sign that you aren't hungry when you wake up since the sun is not up. I wouldn't stress over eating within 30 mins of rising, I would moreso just eat as soon as the sun is out, and make sure you get morning sun shining into your eyes prior to eating.

Also, personally, in winter, because I'm up long before the sun, I do CT first thing in the morning. And actually, anyday I know it will be rainy or cloudy and I won't actually see the sunrise, I hop in the shower and do CT. That's what I did today, because it was rainy and dark out this morning.

That's good, keep it up. Thanks, yes we are expecting a boy! Very excited about having a son. I have a daughter now and it's great.

### drezy — 2017-06-16T22:44:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thank-you-dr-kruse-and-kris90.19639/post-216380>

@[kris90](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19157/)
Congratulations! Having a little man in the house is going to be fun.

You are one of many people who confirm my low regard for "reference levels" and how they are established in the first place.

@[Billybats](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20191/) kris is certainly your wingman here and doing a great job ushering you into the group.

> but could be water weight but not going to analyze it.

Yeah, don't over analyze at this point. "Use the force" and go by how you feel and your energy levels.

The carb craving is part habit and often larger part environment. As you quell the habit and become more aware of your environment and it's effects it will become easier.

The effects on men and women can differ a little in speediness due to many factors. Women sometimes have to be more patient than us testosterone fueled oxen. Stick with it though, I think it'll payoff. My own wife is almost a decade older than me . She has been doing CT for 3 sessions at ~59F 3 times a week. She complained that she has nothing near my weight loss, but does admit she has:

-   wayyyyyy better sleep
-   more mental clarity
-   higher libido (Thanks Jack!)
-   All the while under pressure of one of the biggest professional projects she's ever taken on. She said "I should be falling apart by now."

Whatever the case, you've got a really great setup with kris keeping an eye on you and some of the changes your mentioned seemed to come easily enough. My money is on you @[Billybats](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20191/) .

This.

I think Jack uses the BAB as a strategy to manage hunger and re-yolk the peripheral clocks (gut) with the central clock (brain). Your main focus should be on AM sunlight, and just try to get something into you that is high in protein, and some fats. Don't overthink it. Your instincts are what is most important, and AM sunlight = release dopamine so you can increase signal-to-noise ratio and think clearer. Follow your instincts.

As for potatoes, I don't see a problem, but I would cook them in lots of coconut oil or butter. I always add CO/butter to all my carbs. Carbs are much less energy efficient than fats, so when I eat them, I just "fatten" them up with saturated fats. It's like upgrading to a higher-grade fuel.

# Thread: CSF leaks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JohnnyAZ — 2017-06-14T06:43:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-216131>

I just completed EMF 2 and I got lost at the CSF leak area. It mentioned you can get a leak from sinus surgery (had it twice) but I did not understand how it connected to EMF. I searched the forum for more info on CSF leak and found some info but am still a little lost. Can anyone point me in the right direction?

Also listened to both EMF webinars and the Mito webinar twice.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-14T18:43:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-216166>

Leaking CSF means less water around the brain and water is a natural Faraday cage......less water means less protection from nnEMF

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-14T18:52:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-216168>

Most Faraday cages are made of mu metal but biology uses water to do it. This is why CSF has ions in it. Water with ions in it becomes a conductive plasma to electric forces/fields. Faraday cages, also known as Faraday shields, are enclosures of varying sizes and shapes that are made up of highly conducting materials. When an electric field is applied to the exterior of the cage, electrons in the conducting material are displaced so that one side of the cage becomes negatively charged, whereas the opposite side of the cage becomes positively charged. This induced electric polarization cancels out the external electric field almost entirely inside the conductors and, to a large extent, also in the nonconducting parts inside the enclosure, protecting its interior from electromagnetic waves.

### JohnnyAZ — 2017-06-14T18:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-216170>

Got it! Thanks. I am all caught up...for now.

### Jack Thompson — 2019-12-14T05:57:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-283550>

> [JohnnyAZ said:](/goto/post?id=216170)
>
> Got it! Thanks. I am all caught up...for now.

I had bad CSF leak in 2017. Had multiple blood patches and two major spinal surgeries at Duke before I got fixed. I then experienced rebound high pressure which has dissipated but I still have some symptoms like horrible tinnitus which is 100% from the leak, not Nn EMF. Is there anything I can do to help?

### Pogo — 2017-06-15T05:05:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-focus-on-grammar-and-spelling-over-content.19645/post-216226>

Within the narrow confines of social media, that is fair enough. Particularly so, on Twitter with the minimal message length.

Even on Facebook, the dearth of formatting lends itself to shorter, snappier content. But entirely forgoing use of the <Enter> key is just plain lazy. Exposing the lack of readability or legibility of content, is not "focusing on grammar and spelling".

In blog postings, articles, books etc, there are no excuses for egregious grammatical errors. Language exists for the purpose of communication. Failing to use it properly leads to miscommunication, inconsistency and ambiguity.

Ive always been afraid to fight, since i was a kid... The other day, i went to the night club with my brother, who got really drunk and some guys knew him and hated him because of some girl issue, so they hit him in the face really hard and i took him to the bathroom to help him clean and everything, i was so scared,,,, but then, when we were about to leave the place, those 3 guys came again and hit him and my brother fell to the ground like a brick wall, so something woke up inside of me,... i just jumped on the hitting guy and smashed his face with all of my fury... but i may have hit him 5 or 6 times and his siblings kicked my face and my stomach and i fell to the floor also, but the security guards took us all out of the club... Sometimes we don’t choose our path. It chooses us. Kristian Olaf Bernhard Birkeland was a Norwegian scientist. He is best remembered as the person whose theories of atmospheric electric currents elucidated the nature of the aurora borealis. He understood the sun better than most scientists, in my opinion. His genius was buried in his work on ammonia and linked to electric arcs. In order to fund his research on the aurorae, he invented the electromagnetic cannon and the of fixing nitrogen from the air. Modern science, especially cosmology and astrophysics have no idea how brilliant either of these pursuits were. The fortunate accidents for many scientists and clinicians that came after him. Some of them have realized that buried in his work come some amazing perspective of how stars and mitochondria do things in similar fashion. Birkeland was nominated for the Nobel Prize seven times. He never won it once. That is another scientific tragedy. Today, science still has no idea how brilliant this man was. He was driven to the grave by his own work. He avoided nature and became unable to sleep and used barbituates to try to continue on. He was unsuccessful, like many with sleep disorders. The ultimate irony, is his work contained the answer to his sleeplessness. He never found that Rx. People who benefited from his work have and have decided to carry to fruition. Birkeland proposed in 1908 in his book The Norwegian Aurora Polaris Expedition 1902–1903 that polar electric currents, today referred to as auroral electrojets, were connected to a system of currents that flowed along geomagnetic field lines into and away from the polar region. Such field-aligned currents are known today as Birkeland currents in his honor. Those currents, I believe hold the entire key to the universe and how biology of living things connects to it. His hidden brilliance is hidden in his work on the Birkeland-Eyde process. This process was buried in science because it was not economically exploitable. Fritz Haber perfected the process, and brought in the era of nitrogen based fertilizer. His method of creation turned out to be quite economical and profitable. His work had many unintended consequences and ultimate have led us to corporations like Monsanto and glyphosate. Birkeland used an electrical arc that was easily formed between two coaxial electrodes, and through the use of a strong magnetic field (pinch). The electric plasma, when created in this way, was spread out into a thin disc of plasma. This plasma disc is seen in star creation as well. The plasma temperature in the Birkeland disc was in excess of 3000°C. Air, from the Earth's atmosphere, was blown through this arc, causing some of the nitrogen to react with oxygen forming nitric oxide. By carefully controlling the energy of the electric arc and the velocity of the air stream, yields of up to 4% nitric oxide were obtained. What people still fail to realize is this is the process that is critical to the nitrogen cycle on this planet for everything that uses chlorophyll as a pigment to collect light and make a DC electric current from water. The electric arc is so strong that is can break the triple bond of Nitrogen. The irony is that a leaf uses a 3D cage around a magnesium ion to create an electric arc from water using sunlight. This process is critical to the creation of the entire food web on Earth. The process is extremely energy intensive and this is why it was not economical, profitable, and buried by scientists and businessman, but little do they know that this science is critical to understand life for a mitochondriac. The colony of mitochondrion in our tissues harness and reverse the process that occurs in a leaf to make water from a mitochondria and bury massive electric arc charge within it. This makes the water made by a mitochondria and electric plasma. This water is not similar to your tap water at all. This water is one of the greatest secrets of life. Birkeland used a nearby hydroelectric power station for the electricity as this process demanded about 15 MWh/Ton of nitric acid. Living things use the power of the sun which is significantly greater and cheap. In fact, it is free. The rate of energy capture by plant and animal photosynthesis is immense by hemoglobin and chlorophyll. Living things get 100 trillion watts (1 trillion watts = 1 terra watt (TW)). This power is ten times the current power consumption of the human race. We are designed to be wirelessly connected to that power station in chronic fashion. Perhaps these facts are why Big Pharma would like to bury the sun in our modern world??? They have to protect the franchise from the real science of nature because its energy is free when one knows how to use it. The same reaction, Birkeland found is carried out by lightning, providing a natural source for converting atmospheric nitrogen to soluble nitrates. Nobody seems to connect the fibers that weave nature's quilt. Some of us however, observe better than we see reality in textbooks. Genius always strives to answer questions the ordinary forgot to pose. Being in medicine today and believing that "knowing cellular biochemistry and not being able to connecting it to anything else in nature, like sunlight, is akin to buying a guitar that Prince once owned and then expecting that you could play Purple Rain as he did in life............That is the absurdity of modern science today. Nobody observes science from this perspective today who teaches clinicians. Why would any patient expect their doctor to "know" this perspective? Instead the paradigm creates the meme than any alternative perspective on things is quackery. Your perspective on people comes from the cage you ARE currently held captive in. Wise folks understand there no facts, only "present interpretations" of the data collected and it is subject to how it was obtained. This is why one person's version of "crazy" is another person's reality. This makes intuition and insanity relative. It depends on "who" has "who" locked in 'what cage'. So why am I taking this moment to teach you a bit about Birkeland? Once you learn about his work you'll begin to be able to clearly question what is at the fundamental core of life. Light, water, and magnetism. Why is the direction and flow of time central to human experience, but why isn’t it central to physics? The answer is buried in Birkeland's currents. > phenomena as atmospheric electromagnetic activity 19 , air ionization 20 , and extremely low
> frequency waves.
>
> (related footnotes)
>
> 19 Atmospheric electromagnetic (EM) activity: The Human body communicates internally by EM and
> electrochemical impulses. The EM field displayed in Kirlian photographs, the effectiveness of acupuncture,
> and the body's physical responses to various types of EM radiation (X-rays, infrared radiation, visible light
> spectra, etc.) are all examples of human sensitivity to EM forces and fields. Atmospheric EM activity is
> regularly altered by such phenomena as sunspot eruptions and gravitational stresses which distort the
> Earth's magnetic field. Under varying external EM conditions, humans are more or less disposed to the
> consideration of new ideas. MindWar should be timed accordingly. Per Dr. LJ. Ravitz:
>
> Electromagnetic field constructs add fuel to the assumption unifying living matter
> harmoniously with the operations of nature, the expression of an electromagnetic field no less
> than non-living systems; and that as points on spectrums, these two entities may at last take their
> positions in the organization of the universe in a way both explicable and rational ... A tenable
> theory has been provided for emergence of the nervous system, developing not from functional
> demands, but instead deriving as a result of dynamic forces imposed on cell groups by the total
> field pattern. Living matter on has a definition of state based on relativity field physics, through
> which it has been possible to detect a measurable property of total state functions. (Ravitz, State-
> Function, Including Hypnotic States" in Journal of American Society of Psychosomatic
> Dentistry and Medicine, Vol. 17, No. 4, 1970.)
>
> 20 Ionization of the air: An abundance of negative condensation nuclei ("air ions") in ingested air
> enhances alertness and exhilaration, while an excess of positive ions enhances drowsiness and depression.
> Calculation of the ionic balance of a target audience's atmospheric environment will be correspondingly
> useful. Again this is a naturally-occurring condition - caused by such varying agents as solar ultraviolet
> light, lightning, and rapidly-moving water - rather than one which most be artificially created. (Detonation
> of nuclear weapons, however, will alter atmospheric ionization levels.) Cf. Soyke, Fred and Edmonds, Alan,
> The Ion Effect. New York: E.P. Dutton, 1977.
>
> 21 Extremely Low Frequency (ELF) waves: ELF waves up to 100 Hz are once more naturally occurring,
> but they can also be produced artificially (such as for the Navy's Project Sanguine for submarine
> communication). ELF-waves are not normally noticed by the unaided senses, yet their resonant effect upon
> the human body has been connected to both physiological disorders and emotional distortion. Infrasound
> vibration (up to 20 Hz) can subliminally influence brain activity to align itself to delta, theta, alpha, or beta
> wave patterns, inclining an audience toward everything from alertness to passivity. Infrasound could be
> used tactically, as ELF-waves endure for great distances; and it could be used in conjunction with media
> broadcasts as well. See Playfair, Guy L. and Hill, Scott, The Cycles of Heaven. New York: St. Martin's
> Press, 1978, pages 130-140.

**Pantothenic acid**, also called **vitamin B5** (a [B vitamin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/B_vitamin)), is a [water-soluble](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solubility) [vitamin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vitamin). Pantothenic acid is an [essential nutrient](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_nutrient). Animals require pantothenic acid in order to synthesize [coenzyme-A](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coenzyme-A) (CoA), as well as to synthesize and metabolize [proteins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protein), [carbohydrates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carbohydrate), and [fats](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fat). The [anion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anion) is called **pantothenate**.
Pantothenic acid is the [amide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amide) between [pantoic acid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantoic_acid) and [β-alanine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beta-Alanine).
\-------------------

2\. A craniosacral therapist once made the claim of an immune system reset after 3 days of continuous grounding.

4\. Being around children all day, like a teacher or daycare worker, but in a natural setting. When I am coaching flag football I get a boost just being around the kids. Consequently I cease to worry about external issues Their energy and mindset is very stress relieving.
5\. Days w/o wearing glasses. In my estimation there are 2 main reasons I feel better when doing this. #1 Obviously more UV/IR into eyes and periorbital tissue. #2 My glasses are titanium... no Bueno for obvious reasons. I've ordered a new pair on line that are solely plastic (so I was told).

### Danny — 2017-06-20T19:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/give-me-your-top-50-bio-hacks-youd-like-to-see-for-a-project-i-am-doing.19668/post-216645>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=216641)
>
> What is bad about titanium frames - I have them and they are so nice and light.

Even though titanium is not as magnetic as other metals it still acts like an antenna for nnEMF. On a humid day in a bad nnEMF environment my head will get really tight behind my ears. Take of my glasses and it resolves. Put them back on and it goes tight again.

For those of you who trust Google, Apple, FB, MSFT, and Cisco read the patent. For those of you who don't trust them here is my take home: All they need to do is just place a modulator on your premises and voila: entrainment and control of the entire population. So over their 50-65 years of life they'll pay these companies for the right to become expendable. Very good business model for profit if you ask me. ### Jack Kruse — 2017-10-13T01:08:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/give-me-your-top-50-bio-hacks-youd-like-to-see-for-a-project-i-am-doing.19668/post-225705>

A neurologist finds peace and happiness in the feeling of constant acceleration in NATURE under the sun adjacent to the ocean to stop being an asshole. I know a few neurologists/neurosurgeons who need a pair of skates.............or need to buy a farm and do what they want on that farm........Would you take on this hack on if you could? Can you afford not do it? People with big lifestyles don't realize just how exxpensive poor health or a poor atitude is until they realize they are that person. Being sick is the most expensive thing I know of in life truthfully. Would you stop your life abruptly and reinvent yourself to build your dopamine bank account up by exercising the receptor cells in many of your senses to rebuild your neocortex to become a new person????? Sounds crazy, doesn't it? Can humans, using light, balance, touch, hearing, and taste, incorporate motion to expand consciousness by improving mitochondrial functioning? When you learn about how mitochondria work in different systems the "hacks" you can come up with are amazing. As your mitochondria make more water and CO2 they create a pure fuel source of protons that can "seed" your neocortical mitochondria to fuel conscious expansion because mitochondrial heteroplasmy lowers. Nature is simple if we just get out of our own way and get back to the basics. You have to see the sunlight and exercise the senses to reconnect with your natural instincts................This will be a large discussion for my member event coming this December and January because I've taken on this hack on now.............How I will be doing it is very different, but I expect this one to be epic ride of learning and discovery.

"Having spent decades as an affluent but ‘typical, institutionalized, educated Western man’, the neurologist John Kitchin MD radically reassessed his life after finding that he was losing his eyesight and had grown unsatisfied in his work. Emerging from a bout of hopelessness with the realisation that all he wanted to do was ‘the basic things and skate’, he gave up his practice. Now a minor celebrity on San Diego’s Pacific Beach boardwalk, Kitchin – or Slomo, as he’s become known – practices an idiosyncratic, seemingly slow-motion style of inline skating that doubles as meditation. A charming and light-hearted vision of what can happen when you actually do what you want to, Slomo (2013) won dozens of awards upon its release, including Best Short Documentary at the SXSW Film Festival." [https://aeon.co/videos/a-neurologis...iness-in-the-feeling-of-constant-acceleration](https://aeon.co/videos/a-neurologist-finds-peace-and-happiness-in-the-feeling-of-constant-acceleration)

### peter jesop — 2017-09-08T15:43:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-songs.19669/post-223160>

Fur die german members ein bischen deutsch:
@[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) @[Queen of Elves](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18728/) @[Theka](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17862/) @[Butters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9350/) @[Verena1028](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19511/)

1...2...3...4...5...6...7...8...9 und aus

Alle warten auf das Licht
fürchtet euch, fürchtet euch nicht
die Sonne scheint mir aus den Augen
sie wird heut nacht nicht untergehen

... und die Welt zählt laut bis zehn

eins... hier kommt die Sonne
zwei... hier kommt die Sonne
drei... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
vier... hier kommt die Sonne

Die Sonne scheint mir aus den händen
kann verbrennen kann euch blenden
wenn sie aus den Fäusten bricht
legt sich heiss auf das Gesicht

Sie wird heut Nacht nicht untergehen
und die Welt zählt laut bis zehn

eins... hier kommt die Sonne
zwei... hier kommt die Sonne
drei... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
vier... hier kommt die Sonne

fünf... hier kommt die Sonne
sechs... hier kommt die Sonne
sieben... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
acht... hier kommt die Sonne

Die Sonne scheint mir aus den Händen
kann verbrennen, kann dich blenden
Wenn sie aus den Fäusten bricht
legt sich heiss auf dein Gesicht
legt sich schmerzend auf die Brust
das Gleichgewicht wird zum Verlusst

läßt dich hard zu Boden gehen
und die Welt zählt laut bis zehn

eins... hier kommt die Sonne
zwei... hier kommt die Sonne
drei... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
vier... und wird nie vom Himmel fallen

fünf... hier kommt die Sonne
sechs... hier kommt die Sonne
sieben... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
acht... hier kommt die Sonne

1...2...3...4...5...6...7...8...9 and out

All waiting for the light
have fear, fear not
the sun shines out of my eyes
she will not go down tonight

... and the world counts loud to ten

one... here comes the sun
two... here comes the sun
three... she's the brightest star of all
four... here comes the sun

the sun shines out of my hands
can burn, can blind you
if she breaks out of my fists
lie hot on your face

she would not go down to night
and the world counts loud to ten

one... here comes the sun
two... here comes the sun
three... she's the brightest star of all
four... here comes the sun

five... here comes the sun
six... here comes the sun
seven... she is the brightest star of all
eight... here comes the sun

the sun shines out of my hands
can burn, can blind you
when she breaks out of my fists
lie hot on your face
lays painful on your chest
the balance come to loss

lets you go down hard
and the world counts loud to ten

one... here comes the sun
two... here comes the sun
three... she's the brightest star of all
four... and will never fall from heaven

five... here comes the sun
six... here comes the sun
seven... she is the brightest star of all
eight... here comes the sun

This song was originally going to be used as heavyweight champion Vitali Klitschko’s entrance music, but was never used. The demo name of the song was Klitschko. Even without the use as Klitschko’s entrance music, the song still references boxing through the verses and the counting to ten, as a referee does for a potential KO. @[Victor Mifsud](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19152/)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-21T03:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-eggs.19670/post-216675>

Suck it in.

Chase it with some slices of raw bacon.

....

.

### Stardust — 2017-07-02T23:28:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-eggs.19670/post-217812>

I was buying eggs from the market for a long time. Not to long ago I switched to buying them from my local butcher. I ended up finding out the butcher who told me the eggs were pastured raised was incorrect. Apparently they were from the Ag Department at our local JC. This is the same JC I drive by with animals who have a massive cell tower 5 feet away from them. Ended up purchasing from my local farmers market and haven't had any problems eating them raw since.

### Inger — 2017-07-04T16:08:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-eggs.19670/post-217936>

That is cool! I have realized the quality of food is pretty important!

I get my organic pastured eggs from a neighbor where I walk by when i go to the forest where I live.. I can see the hens outside eating bugs and grass.. they have pretty much space too, also often just moving free in the forest... So I trust my source  No issues with these eggs either, I often make a raw eggs smoothie now in summer, with raw cacao... yummy!

# Thread: Frankie's optimal journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Francesca Sathan — 2017-06-21T15:19:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216707>

I've been building up the courage to start this journal and to finally write down my health history. I guess I've assumed an ostrich like "head in the sand" position in relation to my health over the last few years as I've been too busy juggling work and young children. However, a trip to my GP in December has opened up a Pandora's box of problems which has given me the wake up call I needed. So, deep breath......here goes.

I'm a 47 year old woman of Italian descent, living in the UK. I have two children 6 & 9 years old. Here is a summary of my medical history in order of diagnosis:

10 yrs - fall resulting in recurrent, intermittent back pain
22 yrs - Anemia
24 yrs - PCOS -
took Dianette
25 yrs - 2 herniated discs - L4/L5/SI
29 yrs - Discetomy (both discs)
32 yrs - High Blood Pressure - taken off Dianette
36 yrs - 2 miscarriages
38 yrs - Birth of my son. Gestational diabetes controlled with food. Induced and my son was born with cord wrap and jaundice. I had a post partum hemorrhage. Awful birth - amazing son!
40 yrs - Birth of my daughter. Gestational diabetes controlled with metformin. Induced but much easier birth. Equally amazing daughter.
40 - 47 yrs - Always tired. Overweight. Constant headaches. Constipation. Anemia.
47 years (Pandora's box) - Diverculitis, Gallstones, High Blood Pressure (taking Ramapril), loss of hearing in right ear. Discovered Dr. Jack Kruse.

So this is the year when I decided to turn my health around. I look at my mum who needed to take a small suitcase of medication on holiday with her on Sunday and I know I need to sort my health out now. I owe it to my children to make sure I'm fit and healthy till I'm an old lady.

The first steps in my journey:
1) Acutely aware that I was spending all day in the office or in the car, I have changed my working conditions so that I work from home two days a week. I work sitting in the garden taking in as much sunlight as possible. I've stopped wearing my sunglasses although need prescription glasses to work on my laptop.

2) I take my dog and sometimes a child out for a walk at 6am and walk barefoot in the park. I gaze at the sun and meditate for 5 minutes. Today I cycled to school with the children and we're spending as much time outdoors and barefoot as possible.

3) I'm switching off the wi-fi at night and when we're not using it. I'm wearing blue-blockers once the sun goes down to watch the TV.

4) I'm going to a yoga class once a week and doing yoga at home when I can.

Next, I plan to look into structured water. Although I have been eating paleo on and off for the last two years, I need to revisit what I'm eating. I haven't eaten meat since 25 yrs old so it's quite restricting and expensive to always eat seafood. I really need to move to the seaside!

Well that's me. It's been therapeutic writing this and hope to report progress in my next installment.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-21T16:33:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216719>

welcome. tell us more about Mom and grandmother

### Francesca Sathan — 2017-06-21T16:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216721>

Thank you Jack for replying. My mum is 75. I suppose she had a similar history to me when she was younger. Anemic, 3 miscarriages and then 3 children. She worked the night shift at local hospital for over 20 years as well as looking after us three. She was depressed and on medication for it in her 40s and then went onto have a hysterectomy after a prolapse and then bladder surgery. She now has osteoporosis and has broken her leg/hip twice, degenerative spine with small fractures. I think she takes calcium? Also high blood pressure and a heart murmur. She's been overweight since my brother (child no 3) was born. My maternal grandmother also had osteoporosis and had kyphosis when she was older. She died after a stroke in her 70s but I don't know much more. My dad's family are much healthier with my paternal grandmother living to the ripe old age of 96. But I have a sneaking suspicion that it's my maternal family which will have a bearing on my own health?

### JanSz — 2017-06-21T17:16:24+0300

Yes we are inheriting most from mother.

Do you happen to know your haplotype?
Have you dome 23and me test?

/

### Francesca Sathan — 2017-06-21T19:04:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216735>

Hiya. No i haven't done this test so don't know my haplotype I'm afraid.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-22T02:21:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216782>

Yes.....youre maternal side is where you get your mitochondria from and her story tells a ton about you

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-22T02:22:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216783>

a 23andme test is only 69 bucks. Might be wise to get it.

### JanSz — 2017-06-22T02:27:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216787>

We are always inheriting our haplotype (and mitochondria) form mother.
Over time, over number of generations mitochondria adjusts to location it finds itself in.
At this time it is mostly about latitude.
So if your mother and number of your grand-mothers stayed in approximately same latitude, that is also your optimal latitude.

I did my analysis here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-73#post-216580](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-73#post-216580)

it turns out that over last 10000 years my grandmothers traveled mostly E-W, and very little NS.
And my current location is one degree south of where my my grandmother was 10000 years ago.

Well I've bitten the bullet and have ordered a test. £99 in the UK but hopefully, it will be a wise investment. Where can I find basic information about haloptypes and latitude?

### JanSz — 2017-06-22T15:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216827>

> [Francesca Sathan said:](/goto/post?id=216822)
>
> Well I've bitten the bullet and have ordered a test. £99 in the UK but hopefully, it will be a wise investment. Where can I find basic information about haloptypes and latitude?

We all started near Lake Victoria.
Location [African Great Lakes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/African_Great_Lakes)
Coordinates

[1°S 33°E](https://tools.wmflabs.org/geohack/geohack.php?pagename=Lake_Victoria&params=1_S_33_E_type:waterbody_scale:3000000)
.
Then 120000-150000 years ago we started migration, populating the word.
One line went south, all other North.

# Thread: Incarcerated 50 year old with high anxiety
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incarcerated-50-year-old-with-high-anxiety.19672/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### the lovely brenda — 2017-06-21T19:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incarcerated-50-year-old-with-high-anxiety.19672/post-216738>

My 53 year old brother is incarcerated for life without parole and has been there for 4 years. He had an adverse reaction to adderall while drunk with his girlfriend of 8 years. They were both alcoholics and had a fight. She was screaming at him and he has a psychosis episode due to the mixture of adderall and alcohol and stabbed her to death as a way to shut her up. She was the director of the battered women's shelter for the local county and he was an electrical engineer. Because of her position and this heinous crime of passion, women from all over who never met him called for him to never see the outside world again.

He is locked in a 6x9 foot cell and gets a bit of sunlight every few weekends first thing in the AM or in the evening after 6PM. This only occurs a few months out of the year because we live in Wisconsin. This has been going on for the past 3 years. The first year he went 9 months without seeing the light of day.

He is college educated and has always been an amazing family man. He is having issues with anxiety and is surrounded by extremely loud thugs in a maximum security prison who have little social skills to function in society. He has stopped eating more than once per day when the guards bring him a meal. He has lost his drive to leave his bed and he has requested that our family stop seeing him. When he knows he has a visitation coming up he has panic attacks and can not handle it.

I am wondering if this is a Vitamin D and lack of sunlight issue? I will be contacting the prison to request some labs to be taken on him and I would like some recommendations. He has been begging for anti anxiety medications but I worry about the side effects. It is difficult to see a psychiatrist in his maximum security prison and he has been waiting around two weeks for an appointment. He attempted suicide during the second year of incarceration and ended up in a coma.

I have sent him breathing exercise hand outs but he wants a magical pill or to be left alone to sleep. He has a long time to sit and think about this. His girlfriend was the love of his life and he misses her along with our family and his freedom.

Any recommendations will be helpful.

Thanks!

### drezy — 2017-06-22T00:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incarcerated-50-year-old-with-high-anxiety.19672/post-216774>

It puts a new spin on Sheriff Joe Arpaio's tent city.

> Prior to the election of Sheriff [Joe Arpaio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joe_Arpaio) in 1993, the prisoner population in [Maricopa County](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maricopa_County) Jail, [Arizona](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arizona), the 4th largest jail system in the world,[\[39\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tent_city#cite_note-39) exceeded the maximum number of inmates allowed in its facilities. Prisoners were routinely released from custody prior to completing their sentence due to the overcrowding. In a study conducted in 1993 it was estimated that construction of a new facility would cost approximately $70,000,000.\[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed)_\] Sheriff Arpaio, concerned about the cost of a new facility and reasoning that military tents were good enough for the men and women of the U.S. armed forces who fought in the [Gulf War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gulf_War), ordered that a tent jail be constructed using inmate labor.[\[40\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tent_city#cite_note-40) It consisted of [Korean War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Korean_War)\-era tents donated by the [United States armed forces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_armed_forces), and a 50 ft (15.4 m) [observation tower](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Observation_tower) with a vacancy sign mounted on the front. The final cost of the project was approximately $100,000 and it is capable of housing over 2,400 inmates. [wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tent_city#Maricopa_County_Jail_modification)

### Brent Patrick — 2017-06-25T11:41:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incarcerated-50-year-old-with-high-anxiety.19672/post-217048>

Yes I work as a correctional Officer in a Maximum Security Correctional Facility , your best bet for your Brother is to have his security classification graded to an acceptable level where he is given more options to where he can be placed within the Gaol and hopefully be transferred to a prison farm.This all comes down to mental health and behaviour of Inmate.
Prison Farm is a positive solution given by the members above as your Brother will have access to Sunlight ,magnetism (bare earth) and copious amounts of Water. The seafood may be an issue as Dr kruse mentioned above and is right on the money "**When you lose control of your environment based upon your choices you are subject to their choices and those outcomes."JK
**
You mention Brenda
"I am wondering if this is a Vitamin D and lack of sunlight issue?"
"He has a long time to sit and think about this. His girlfriend was the love of his life and he misses her along with our family and his freedom".

This is a problem ,but ultimately the issue for your Brother is it could be that he hasn't come to terms with his situation and the loss of his loved one and wanting to commit suicide is testimony to this, I see this very often in inmates I deal with. Taking ownership and accepting of the situation he is in , is how he is going to crawl out of the dark hole he currently in. Medication is going to turn him into a Zombie, if he has a unsympathetic Psych he will be drugged up to his eyeballs so he will not be problematic for staff within the correctional Centre. This is a really bad situation to be in as Dr kruse mentioned "**make humans docile and more controllable' JK
**
Best solution is for him to make appointments to see support staff such as Welfare ,Chaplain (if Religious) and Psychologist to express his concerns especially regarding Yard Time (Sun).Inmates who survive in Gaol are those who are proactive and Vocal until they are takin notice of my staff. You must remember a Inmate is just one of thousands in a Gaol .Your Brother needs to make noise but in a smart way.
The Sun is a major problem I have personally seen dozens of inmates over the years take to their arm with a razor blade due to lack of Sun and confined to a Small cell it is a terrible cycle to get into and this is what you are seeing with your brother.

In Australia we have Official visitors come to Correctional Centres to listen to Issues Inmates and Staff may have ,this is a Government body that works for the Attorney General's Dept . An Official Visitor will then report back to this Dept with anything they they deem to be irregular. Attorney General's Dept is separate too Corrective Services so it is in nature their to show Transparency and to report that the running of Gaols and Inmates are being looked after in a humane environment.

So Brenda you need to start doing research as too who the government Body is in your state that overseers Correctional Facilities, dont give up it wont be easy and the key is for you too follow the advice on this Forum and Dr Kruse's work as you being full of inner fire is what is going to help bring your brother out of his dark hole .Once he see's you and others caring about him and the effort you are making he will hopefully start to make an effort himself.
I have seen this happen many times an inmate with no hope turn full circle and deal with the hand given to him.
Yes Drezy Inmates can take on work ,,,and the smart ones always take outside work gardening maintenance etc

You said Brenda

"It is difficult to see a psychiatrist in his maximum security prison and he has been waiting around two weeks for an appointment." unfortunately the wheels turn slow in a Correctional Facility,, Your Brother cannot give up

JanSZ "**He must be aware of things that influence him and have to assign priority."** Very true as Gaol influences come in good and bad packages your Brother has to work out which is good which is not!

ok so I am reading EMF 4 and listening to the PPP webinar and I have a question. If I am understanding this correctly carbs can disrupt the body's utilization of the PPP and we should be aiming to become fat burners and activate the PPP. However I don't know how the creatine phosphagen system (CP) interacts with the PPP. What I mean is this. If I am a person just now adopting epi-paleo and dealing with some health issues, should I capitalize on the CP while also nourishing the PPP or does the CP shut off the PPP the same way carbs do? If I should be utilizing CP what do I take to counteract the problems with depleted methyl groups? Or should I just ignore the CP and focus on the PPP?

Side notes: I am a person who works out regularly and used to supplement creatine. I have minor fibromyalgia type symptoms in my past medical history.

# Thread: The emerging role of Nrf2 in mitochondrial function
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-emerging-role-of-nrf2-in-mitochondrial-function.19678/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-06-22T13:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-emerging-role-of-nrf2-in-mitochondrial-function.19678/post-216814>

[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0891584915002129](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0891584915002129)

Abstract

The transcription factor NF-E2 p45-related factor 2 (Nrf2; gene name _NFE2L2_) allows adaptation and survival under conditions of stress by regulating the gene expression of diverse networks of cytoprotective proteins, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and detoxification enzymes as well as proteins that assist in the repair or removal of damaged macromolecules. Nrf2 has a crucial role in the maintenance of cellular redox homeostasis by regulating the biosynthesis, utilization, and regeneration of glutathione, thioredoxin, and NADPH and by controlling the production of reactive oxygen species by mitochondria and NADPH oxidase. Under homeostatic conditions, Nrf2 affects the mitochondrial membrane potential, fatty acid oxidation, availability of substrates (NADH and FADH2/succinate) for respiration, and ATP synthesis. Under conditions of stress or growth factor stimulation, activation of Nrf2 counteracts the increased reactive oxygen species production in mitochondria via transcriptional upregulation of uncoupling protein 3 and influences mitochondrial biogenesis by maintaining the levels of nuclear respiratory factor 1 and peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor γ coactivator 1α, as well as by promoting purine nucleotide biosynthesis. Pharmacological Nrf2 activators, such as the naturally occurring isothiocyanate sulforaphane, inhibit oxidant-mediated opening of the mitochondrial permeability transition pore and mitochondrial swelling. Curiously, a synthetic 1,4-diphenyl-1,2,3-triazole compound, originally designed as an Nrf2 activator, was found to promote mitophagy, thereby contributing to the overall mitochondrial homeostasis. Thus, Nrf2 is a prominent player in supporting the structural and functional integrity of the mitochondria, and this role is particularly crucial under conditions of stress.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-22T15:39:08+0300

Which is why I haul my kids everywhere and they don't ride the bus... one of them has braces and I really hope there is no titanium in them because the junior college he goes to has 2 cell towers... he's great about drinking water though - he carries a gallon bottle around with him - otherwise: headaches...

It was so funny last week, someone came to our house and said she installed flux on her computer and switched to incandescent lights and cured her migraines - in front of my husband - who thinks we're all nuts - he just walked away... one's family is the hardest to help because they fight everything you say -

I **thought** I laid things out fairly easily but family has a way of disregarding what you say. @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) asked me to write a bit about this and this is just a prelude once I gather my thoughts.

after the Jack consult my mother has been asking me questions.
