### dusty doris — 2017-06-30T21:08:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contradicting-info-for-carbs.19707/post-217637>

I have been wondering about UVA as well. I typically go outside naked at sunrise, which is currently about 5:20AM. I spend about an hour outside (till about 6:30). I wasn't previously aware of the UVA importance, just the concept of "morning light". So, I was curious if there was any UVA during that timeline and if I need to extend it or start it later in order to get the most benefits.

So, today I ran an experiment. I used the UV meter that Scompy mentioned in that article. It says it detects both UVA and UVB. It doesn't differentiate between the two, just measures those spectrums.

[https://www.amazon.com/Educational-...UTF8&qid=1485276935&sr=8-2&keywords=UVB+meter](https://www.amazon.com/Educational-Innovations-Atmospheric-Light-Meter/dp/B008MB1ZU8/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1485276935&sr=8-2&keywords=UVB+meter)

I also heard that you can approximate the UVA by taking going about halfway between sunrise and the start time of UBV according to the dminder app.

Here are my notes from this morning.

Location: Phoenix, AZ
Sunrise: 5:21AM
Dminder Vitamin D: 7:58AM
Estimated UVA Time: ~6:40AM

Using the meter, I got no UV readings from 5:15AM until 6:30AM. At 6:35 I started getting readings. I wrote down the degrees elevation that dminder gave me as well. The UV index on my meter hit 1 a few minutes before dminder did, but it was pretty close. I'm not sure what exactly the UV Index means on the meter, I'm assuming its just count of total UV, rather than specifically UVB as dminder said that I wouldn't have Vitamin D potential until well after the UVI was above 0.

Readings:
6:30AM - 0
6:35AM - 4 mW/m2, 0 UV index, 13 degrees sun
6:40AM - 8 mW, 0 UV index, 14 degrees
6:45AM - 14m, 1 UV index, 15 degrees
6:50AM - 18m, 1UVI, 16 deg
6:55AM - 25m, 1UVI, 16 deg
7:00AM - 30m, 1UVI 17 deg
7:30AM - 55m, 2UVI, 25 deg
8:00AM - 55m, 2UVI, 32 deg

So, according to the meter, it looks like I started getting some UVA at about 6:35AM, which is 5 minutes before the estimated UVA (based on the half way point between sunrise and UVB). So, in my location, right now, it appears that is a decent way to estimate UVA.

Another interesting note, is that I was told UVA penetrates windows, but not UVB. However, using that meter today, I was showing 0 UV through the window this morning. Even pointing it directly at the sun. Perhaps its just not sensitive enough or the windows can only block UVA up to a certain amount, then more leaks in. But, when I opened the window I was able to read some UV inside the house although not as much as being outside.

Based on this, I am now going to try to extend my session until about 7AM. Or at the least, I will always open the windows and door in my kitchen (which faces the sun) to allow some UVA to enter the home while I am cooking the family eggs and bacon.

# Thread: Why is Jack such a vile arrogant SOB?

### Paul Keenan — 2017-06-30T16:28:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-jack-such-a-vile-arrogant-sob.19715/post-217596>

_'Change comes fastest to those people who are capable of unlearning and relearning. People who do not listen to feedback, will not be able to pivot based on changing circumstances, because they refuse to acknowledge reality. This is tied to a low dopamine state. Moreover, because they think they already have all the answers, they are not open to receiving feedback or contemplating advice designed to help them'.

'I look for and seek people who are honest about their limitations, and who are willing to put in the hard work needed to find out the answers in their environment to what they don't know, rather than someone who is happy remaining confidently ignorant'.

'Until you ALTER your thinking, you will always recycle your experiences...............I don't waste my energy or knowledge on people who “cry” for help but don't really want to hear the truth. Your intelligence is a powerful force that will change people's way of thinking, but only when they're ready. Paul is not. Most out there in the world are conditioned “pharmaceutical or food guru sheep” that have been led for years that masking the disease is the way forward not disease prevention or the promise of a disease reversal. I am not emotional about the truth's nature has revealed'_

How dare you. I have only just read this, I am furious. Ignorant? ABOUT WHAT? I know we need the sun. I know all about mitochondria and ATP. I have personally spoken, and exchanged letters with brilliant biologist John McLaren-Howard who developed the Mitochondrial function tests in the UK, which Dr Myhill and other use for CFS. I have constantly sought answers. Not moving due to debt means one does not want to learn? They want to remain ignorant and sick? What hogwash. Pure bullshit from a megalomaniac.

My thinking has got me from being completely bed ridden to walking 2200 meters this year with no NHS help at all. You have no clue you arrogant idiot. I have worked tireless to be where I am now. I could not feed myself at one point. No doctor has helped me, I have done everything.

Not everyone can implement everything Jack recommends. Jack presumes somebody is ignorant or deluded if they can not up and move to a sunnier location. As if everyone in the UK wants to live there. Or everyone comes to this forum not knowing anything about the sun.

My recommendation is to drop the arrogance and stop presuming people other than yourself do not know anything. To reply like this to somebody such as myself who has been through hell is absolutely vile, very arrogant, dogmatic and lack empathy.

Yes ultimately a change of environment is ideal, but many have to cope the best they can within their environment. Bear this is mind.

### kris90 — 2017-06-30T17:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-jack-such-a-vile-arrogant-sob.19715/post-217603>

Jack is not telling you what HE thinks you should do, he is simply dropping a TRUTH bomb on you from an biological evolutionary perspective.

You can shoot the messenger, or you can accept the hard truth, and open your eyes to reality. Money is a fictional, materialistic, meaningless item that is in no way tied to our evolutionary biology or ability to thrive. Compare yourself to someone living in an off-grid ancient tribe at the equator. You have something in common: you are both broke. What is the difference? The tribe member has their health, happiness and freedom, but you do not.

Money allows a higher power to lower your dopamine, so it can control you. The fact that you think you need money to thrive in this world just proves that "THE SYSTEM" has got you wrapped around its fat, greedy finger.

The sooner you are ready to face the reality, the sooner you will be able to unlock the gate to optimal. We are all trying to help you uncover the "blindfold". There is nothing else we can offer you. You'll be ready when you're ready.

I need to move to a sunnier location and I have several excuses for not already doing so. In other words, I'm not at the point where I put moving as my number 1 priority. ### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-01T05:07:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-jack-such-a-vile-arrogant-sob.19715/post-217676>

> [Paul Keenan said:](/goto/post?id=217596)
>
> _'Change comes fastest to those people who are capable of unlearning and relearning. People who do not listen to feedback, will not be able to pivot based on changing circumstances, because they refuse to acknowledge reality. This is tied to a low dopamine state. Moreover, because they think they already have all the answers, they are not open to receiving feedback or contemplating advice designed to help them'.
>
> 'I look for and seek people who are honest about their limitations, and who are willing to put in the hard work needed to find out the answers in their environment to what they don't know, rather than someone who is happy remaining confidently ignorant'.
>
> 'Until you ALTER your thinking, you will always recycle your experiences...............I don't waste my energy or knowledge on people who “cry” for help but don't really want to hear the truth. Your intelligence is a powerful force that will change people's way of thinking, but only when they're ready. Paul is not. Most out there in the world are conditioned “pharmaceutical or food guru sheep” that have been led for years that masking the disease is the way forward not disease prevention or the promise of a disease reversal. I am not emotional about the truth's nature has revealed'_
>
> How dare you. I have only just read this, I am furious. Ignorant? ABOUT WHAT? I know we need the sun. I know all about mitochondria and ATP. I have personally spoken, and exchanged letters with brilliant biologist John McLaren-Howard who developed the Mitochondrial function tests in the UK, which Dr Myhill and other use for CFS. I have constantly sought answers. Not moving due to debt means one does not want to learn? They want to remain ignorant and sick? What hogwash. Pure bullshit from a megalomaniac.
>
> My thinking has got me from being completely bed ridden to walking 2200 meters this year with no NHS help at all. You have no clue you arrogant idiot. I have worked tireless to be where I am now. I could not feed myself at one point. No doctor has helped me, I have done everything.
>
> Not everyone can implement everything Jack recommends. Jack presumes somebody is ignorant or deluded if they can not up and move to a sunnier location. As if everyone in the UK wants to live there. Or everyone comes to this forum not knowing anything about the sun.
>
> My recommendation is to drop the arrogance and stop presuming people other than yourself do not know anything. To reply like this to somebody such as myself who has been through hell is absolutely vile, very arrogant, dogmatic and lack empathy.
>
> Yes ultimately a change of environment is ideal, but many have to cope the best they can within their environment. Bear this is mind.

**WHAT DOES A LOW DOPAMINE STATE DUE TO A BRAIN:** IT BLINDS THEM TO FUNDAMENTAL TRENDS CAUSING THEIR DEMISE PRESENT ALL AROUND THEM.

**AM I ARROGANT OR HAVE YOU MISSED MY MODUS OPERUNDI?** Arrogance is in everything I do but I cannot be hurt by it because I am so curious. Without curiosity arrogance becomes pathologic. Blurry concepts influence our attention to focus on what matters and ignore what does not. A postulated clarity influences the development of arrogance because curiosity is absent. Simply minding one's own business is more offensive than being intrusive. It is in my gestures, the harshness of my voice, in the glow of my gaze, in my sinewy, tormented face. I have a disdain for stupidity and I was given this streak by evolution for a reason. Evolution chooses between an honest arrogance and a hypercritical humility. It is built into a cells design in my opinion. Arrogance is not rude to a ripe mind. It is a short cut through bullshit to the truth. "Real compassion kicks butt and takes names and is not pleasant on certain days. If you are not ready for this FIRE, than find a new-age, sweetness and light, perpetually smiling teacher and learn to relabel your ego with spiritual sounding terms. But, stay away from those who practice REAL COMPASSION, because they will fry your ass, my friend." -Ken Wilber

Listen critics are everywhere. People think they know you from your social media critics/skeptics. I just laugh at this notion. It just proves how shallow their thinking is by the mention of it. If social media was so right we'd all just find soulmates on it with no fail..........Here is my take: How much easier it is to be critical than to be correct? **_I'd rather be right than liked._** The pleasure of criticizing takes away from us the pleasure of being moved by some very fine things in nature. Remember when people tell you something’s wrong or doesn’t work for them, they are almost always right. When they tell you exactly what they think is wrong and how to fix it, they are almost always wrong. GB was told for 4 yrs to move and she refused = low dopamine = enjoy your life with your choices.

### Rakhee — 2017-06-30T17:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.19717/post-217612>

Hi Everyone,

I am a new member here and I am still going through the reading material.
I am glad to come across this blog and the forum. Trying to incorporate the lifestyle changes and to assimilate the information slowly.

Currently doing:
Blue light blockers after sunset
face dunks in icy water
cold showers
swimming in outdoor pool during sunset for about an hour to 1 hr 30 mins (grounding + sunset)
Seaweed dulce and bladderwrack (with care as I have Hashimoto's, taking just a little everyday)
RO water
Coconut oil/ghee use in food

Trying to add:
Morning sun
More grounding
To add more seafood in my diet ( I have started to take more but need even more I guess, I was a mostly vegetarian person so taking it slowly)

About the morning sun, I live at the border of a state park, mostly surrounded by dense and tall trees. Though that probably is good for us as I guess we would have lower nnEMF, however morning sun/setting sun is a bit hard to get as I can only see it peeking through the trees if I try/after around 7. Is that enough morning light?

Also, I am planning to make a earthing sheet/strap. Please let me know your experiences.

Thank you so much.

### Rakhee — 2017-07-01T15:02:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.19717/post-217708>

I guess I should put up more information about myself here. I am 38, two kids.
Have been not very well for about 8-10 years now. Have Hashimoto's, taking synthroid and a little T3. Had lyme disease that was diagnosed a 8 months ago, after treatment I do feel better. Before treatment getting out of the bed was a tough chore every morning, now only once in a while. I have IBD, not confirmed by tests yet, probably leaky gut too. My worst problem is that my body is super sensitive and reacts badly to food/time I eat in the evening/whatever else I do. It retains a lot of water and my mood goes so awry that I feel like I am a monster even though I am and try to be a good person at other times. I also get very lethargic and fatigued.
Allopathic medine has helped me little but has not helped me much with quality of my life.

After using blue blockers at night my sleep is better and thankfully there are seldom any nights anymore that kept me awake till 3-4 AM.
Hoping to interact and get ideas/help from Dr. Kruse (Thank you again !) and other knowledgeable and motivated people here.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.19717/post-217771>

Thank you ScottishEmma !

I used a earthing strap and some speaker wire I had lying around to test for the first time on how I feel. Kept it on all night. Liked it, I think it helped me a little with my breathing (Asthma). No major aha or oomph moment yet. Planning to keep it on every might in hope to benefit.

Thank you all !

### Meg M — 2017-07-02T20:14:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.19717/post-217794>

Welcome Rakhee! As a fellow person with Hashimoto's, I wanted to share how I've improved over the past year.

In addition to light, water and magnetism/minding my circadian cycles, I've been using a laser on my thyroid. Starting in January I've been trying to emulate this LLLT/Hashimoto's study: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20662037](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20662037) using the B-Cure Laser ([http://gd-energies.com](http://gd-energies.com/)) that I bought on eBay. The B-Cure is 808nm wavelength and cost about $550 USD. I have used the laser on my thyroid twice a week for 20 seconds. Then, in late February, I got my Quantlet and have been using it daily, just on my arm as usual (usually P30 or P60). Between these two devices, added to my LWM practices, I have been able to reduce my thyroid medication dose three times since December, and will likely reduce again based on my most recent labs. For the first time ever, my TPO thyroid antibodies are within normal range at 33 (reference range is 0-34)! hsCRP is down to 2.3 (it started around 14). I have more info on my Journal. [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/my-inconvenient-truth-megs-journal.18127/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/my-inconvenient-truth-megs-journal.18127/) Hope this is helpful, you are on the right track!

### Rakhee — 2017-07-13T20:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.19717/post-218718>

Today sun-gazing for 25 minutes at 7 AM. Been CTing in tub for last few days.

Drinking cold water before the breakfast is fully digested gives me heartburn. Also as per Ayurveda and Chinese medicine drinking cold water while the food is being digested is not considered good as it slows down digestion as it lowers the temp in stomach. I tried drinking it a couple of days and it gave me heartburn after a while.

Is there an alternative option for drinking cold water before CT. I feel fine if I CT without drinking cold water. My vagus nerve didn't care I guess.

Any pointers appreciated.

# Thread: Emma's Journal of Amazingness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/>

### ScottishEmma — 2017-06-30T17:43:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-217614>

Figured it was about time I started a journal.

I'm Emma, 38 from Glasgow, Scotland. Being from Scotland I'm naturally hilarious and I swear a lot!

I'm a stay at home mum to two kids, aged 4 and 2 <-------- crazy times!

I grew up in the countryside outside Glasgow and currently live in an amazing ecohome in the country.

Population density within square mile: 15

Haplotype H (Northern European)

My Mother and Grandmother were from East coast of Scotland. Both indoor workers all their life. My gran had Alzheimer's before she died age 75. My Mum is 64 and doesn't give a shit about health preferring instead to watch TV all the time. Both had/have excessively used alcohol. My mum suffered unreal hormonal swings and heavy menstruation.

As for me, I'm pretty amazing. I've been on a health journey since 2007 starting with raw vegan then doing what all raw vegans do and discovering paleo. I've messed around with ketogenic and bulletproof diets but I'd say my diet is pretty dialled in.

I eat local and organic as much as possible. Far from a SUKD as you can get.

I'd say my only 'issues' that I would like help with are my hormonal imbalances around middle of my cycle. I get the rage for a few days and right before my period I get intrusive thoughts and feel anxious.

Other than that, I think I'm in great health physically and mentally.

We're moving to Spain in September to get closer to the sun as there is fuck all sun here. Seriously. Zero.

I'm currently learning about the EMF stuff having just bought a cornet meter.

Having been freaked out by the reading on my iphone I'm working out how to biohack having a phone.

I've just come off all social media and I LOVE IT!!

Also getting into the CT starting with face dunks which I've been doing a couple of weeks now. Not ready for cold baths yet - our home in Spain in November has an outdoor pool so YAY!

I'm not an official member of the site yet. I'm reticent to spend much more time online - particularly as I'm now having to use my husband's computer plugged into the Ethernet because I demanded we kill WIFI in the house

Also, I just spent $1000 on a vaginal kung-fu course (yes, really) so I'm buttering up my husband (not like that) before diving into another online investment. But I've no doubt it's on the cards because when I get a bee in my bonnet (is that a saying outside Scotland?) I totally go ALL IN.

So yeah, let's see how amazing it can get!

### drezy — 2017-07-01T02:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-217667>

Oh man vaginas AND kungfu?!?! That'd be the end of me. I've been mildly addicted to the V for decades. They seem to have a dramatic effect on my blood flow, reasoning skills (especially financial), and attention span. I've been so under the influence of the V I've purchased amazingly expensive dinners and at my absolute lowest point found myself asking a gal to tell me more about her cats' personalities. It was that moment when I realized that I was shamelessly addicted. Though I open up here about it, I suspect that my wife already has an inkling...

I think you've got the right idea. You're getting the test mentality under your belt for sure and you can let the science trickle in over time. If it's down to sounding smart or being healthier I'll always choose the latter. Since your husband may think you're crazy already try to get him to get some light, either directly or through a tan-through, right on his nuts at least on weekends. I laughed when I first heard this advice. I'm not laughing now.

I'll stay tuned and good luck.

> vaginal kung - fu??? awesome! please spill your guts!

Thanks Caroline, our home is rented. We've been here almost two years. It is a renovated farmhouse with lots of light made of stone, glass and lots of wood using plenty of natural materials including local sheep wool for insulation and rock cut from the local hills and a wormery for compost toilet. The walls are made from a peculiar material, I've no idea what it is but I know that if it gets wet it crumbles! It's in a few acres with its own orchard and lots of wild flowers and fruits growing. Absolutely amazing. I'll be sad to leave it but the one thing it doesn't have much of is sunlight due to its location.

Vaginal kung-fu has been on my radar since having the kids. The teacher is all about the Taoist philosophy and strengthening the vaginal muscles to exude overall health to the system. It's interesting, that's for sure!

Thanks for the welcome everyone!

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-04T18:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-217948>

So I'm half a dioptre improved in one eye. Optician was impressed! That's in two months by the way!!

Kids are now on DAY 5 without the TV and it's going amazing, if a little more exhausting. This is exactly what I wanted - no TV!

I'm taking my cornet over to my Mum's this weekend to demonstrate why I don't want my son on her Kindle fire. She may well not like it but care factor zero. I want her to see what's really going on when she gives it to him... #mumissues

On a side note, I came up with a hack for my husband. He's on the phone A LOT for work and I've persuaded him to put his phone on divert to our home landline during the day - WIN!!

### drezy — 2017-07-04T19:18:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-217955>

Think about it. You're changing into who you are supposed to be with some minor changes. That perspective is both fun and kinda creepy in some ways. Your potential was getting diminished by something you didn't see.

I did the same with my mom months ago. I might as well have been explaining it all to my resident roadrunner. After one Jack consult my mom is now regularly saying things like "You know Jack says...." I think to myself "Yeah, the same things I've been telling you for months", but just smile and say " Why do you think that is?" and let her explain it to me. My mom had to get pretty beat down before her ears could open though.

> I've persuaded him to put his phone on divert to our home landline during the day - WIN!!

Nice.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-07T09:01:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-218145>

Mum guilt alert: I vacuumed yesterday and as usual my kids went to the vacuum to get warm as they normally pull up their jumpers beside the hot air coming out - I know, gross!

It hit me and I grabbed the cornet to test. My god. It was up at 40uT where they were sitting and they've been doing that about 75% of the time the vacuum has been out.

I've told them not to do it anymore. I don't want to start freaking them out about electrical items. Lochie (my son) is already a bit weirded out by the foil over the oven

ORRRRR I'm going to have to give up hovering

I'm on Day 2 without chocolate and the kids are on Day 8 without TV <-------- this makes me super happy!

I only eat artisan chocolate but I do eat it every day. Every day. The last time I tried to give up I went 10 days and my life was so fucking depressing  But yeah, I realise it's not super amazing for me to be eating it as much as I do.

### caroline — 2017-07-07T09:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-218147>

I think good chocolate is absolutely necessary ......that is my story and I am sticking to it!

I vote to give up hovering! I hate being inside! Cooking everything we eat from scratch is taking way too much time.

BTW Amazing Emma - I think we are sisters from another mother. I am like a dog with a bone over almost everything. I never, ever take no for an answer!

### Brent Patrick — 2017-07-07T09:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-218149>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=217988)
>
> Brent - I am wondering how your higher dopamine/inner light affects your day job?

Well where do I start ,basically it has improved it for the better I work in a mental health Pod within the Gaol so all of my inmates have some form of mental illness crimes range from Stalking Intimidation to Murder and everything in between
I must say I am more relaxed and decision making has improved ten fold not that it was ever lacking but the increase in seeing situations from a different level is a bonus.

I have noticed my workload has increased due too inmates approaching me rather than going to another Officer for interaction.

As far as managing Inmates I am strict but I am fare and directions I give to these Inmates has been very receptive on there part, an Officer who can De -escalate a potentially bad situation arising within a Gaol is worth there weight in gold.
So having an inmate listen and see reason with what you say is going to make your day easier (less paper work) this is what I have noticed.

My energy levels have increased with Dr kruse's Protocols ,I go into the yard with the Inmates to get Morning Sun I cannot take my shoes off for obvious reasons but I back -up on too a concrete wall and place my palms on the wall and make like the sphinx for that wireless connection.
At first the inmates looked at me strange as it is vary rare to have an officer in a yard with Inmates but over several weeks I am now just part of the furniture, and will often say "getting your vitamin D chief" (Chief is gaol slang for prison officer)
I have been in this Mental health Pod 3 months now and have majority of the Inmates now basking in the sun like a lizard (middle of winter 10c celsius) and getting there CT in for the day .Before I arrived in this Pod it was very rare for mental health Inmates to use the yard they would stay all day under blue light inside the common area of the Pod

The psychologist of this unit has noticed this behaviour and said she has noticed cognitive improvement with these particular Inmates ,,naturally I referred her to Jack kruse site

Sometimes I feel like Jack Nicholson in one flew over the cuckoo's nest at work , Overall I have been very happy with the hacks I have implemented from Dr kruse and all the other members on the Forum. My vision is now panoramic once I allowed the light in.

> I have been in this Mental health Pod 3 months now and have majority of the Inmates now basking in the sun like a lizard (middle of winter 10c celsius) and getting there CT in for the day .Before I arrived in this Pod it was very rare for mental health Inmates to use the yard they would stay all day under blue light inside the common area of the Pod

There it is. The subtle art of influence. Nice job leading by example Brent. This helps me realize that any influence I've had on people has been from them seeing me repeat what are simply habits for me now, not by spouting science. People can ignore their nitric oxide release only for so long before habituating to it. It took me a while to realize why Doc always keeps saying "We're designed to be addicted to the sun."

The psychologist noticing says a lot too.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-08T08:17:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-218215>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=217614)
>
> I'd say my only 'issues' that I would like help with are my hormonal imbalances around middle of my cycle. I get the rage for a few days and right before my period I get intrusive thoughts and feel anxious.

Emma, I have had similar issues and have resolved them using Fertility Awareness Method. In short, no more drugs/hormones/implants. It is a method of body awareness through understanding your own cycle with monitoring of temperature, cervical mucus and other markers. I was on the pill from my teens until my 30s. I tried the nonhormonal IUD with bad results, so I eventually found this. It has been a major game changer for me in understanding my own body, feminine power and wisdom. I'm new to this site so I can't give any links or references from Dr. Kruse relevant to menstruation, if any, to the bullshit that is medical menstrual control. I'm guessing if you found vaginal kung-fu you're receptive to this kind of approach. I haven't tried that yet, but I'm familiar and have started a practice with a yoni egg. I had issues with emotions and physical pains that disappeared after I stopped trying to manage my cycles medically. Natural healthy bodily cycles do not need to be medically managed, and in fact do better when left alone and honored. I think that is definitely in the spirit of this community. I'd love to chat more with you if you're interested.

@[ValerieBee](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20271/) Thank you so much, I love this post! Yes, I came off the pill about 11 years ago, which coincided with me discovering I didn't actually like my then husband very much! I invested in a Daysy about three months ago and it has been a total game changer. So worth the investment! It takes my temperature every morning and gives me the green light if I'm good to go. Having had two babies I'm absolutely keen not to have any more so we needed to do something and any type of hormone fuckery was not on the agenda for me - and the husband wasn't keen on a vasectomy, understandably. For the record I don't think vasectomies are a good idea anyway...

So yeah, thanks for that. Really, my cycle is a bit unpredictable at times with regards to what day I'll experience rage-like PMT but usually it's anything between day 20 and day 25. I really hate to be so quick to anger during these days. Any advice on that would be great!!

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-08T15:24:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-218241>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=218216)
>
> I came off the pill about 11 years ago, which coincided with me discovering I didn't actually like my then husband very much! I invested in a Daysy about three months ago and it has been a total game changer. So worth the investment! It takes my temperature every morning and gives me the green light if I'm good to go. Having had two babies I'm absolutely keen not to have any more so we needed to do something and any type of hormone fuckery was not on the agenda for me - and the husband wasn't keen on a vasectomy, understandably. For the record I don't think vasectomies are a good idea anyway...
>
> So yeah, thanks for that. Really, my cycle is a bit unpredictable at times with regards to what day I'll experience rage-like PMT but usually it's anything between day 20 and day 25. I really hate to be so quick to anger during these days. Any advice on that would be great!!

The first thing that comes to mind is to suggest giving yourself a break during those times. Like actual rest, seclusion and pampering. Since I've been more attuned with my cycle and the female energy I can absolutely tell now when I am about 24 hrs from my period--I am crazy irritable and just want to be left alone. It turns out the stereotypes about PMS are really distortions of honoring the female body and wisdom, as we try to ignore that with things like the Pill and attempt to fit feminine energy into our modern (dare I say patriarchal) world. I have no doubt you understand I'm speaking on a spiritually energetic level and not male-bashing here. So when we are "cranky" or "irritable" or all the other colorful words people use to describe a woman's need for space to herself during menstruation, it is our body and psyche's way of telling others to back off so we can have that sacred space. There are beliefs that the "moon time" was a time when women would gather together to basically have a kind of vision quest. This is the original meaning and intent behind the Red Tent, before it was appropriated and turned into a place of shame. It was a sacred space to gather and support other women, not a space to shamefully hide or be hidden from society while you were "dirty." There are beliefs and anecdotes that show us women would gather during their shedding and commune with Spirit to receive messages for themselves and their communities. There is belief that the menstrual blood is sacred and contains wisdom...and there's science here and there resonating with this, like the discovery of stem cells in menstrual blood.

If you're interested in such things, here is one blog I really found interesting on this topic: [http://www.moonsong.com.au/the-spiritual-practice-of-menstruation/](http://www.moonsong.com.au/the-spiritual-practice-of-menstruation/). I'm happy to share any other info or links. It is really amazing how being on birth control can lead a person to mates they otherwise would not pair with. That kind of truth makes me want to put on my tinfoil hat, because it's real. I've also experienced rage wrt my cycles. I'm still in the process of sorting that out. Gotta run--family calls, but I look forward to interacting more here on the forum! Love your posts

### drezy — 2017-07-08T22:21:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-218261>

Since you ladies are so open here I've also tracked my cycle and will detail it below:

Day1: Wake-up, calculate possibility for morning intimate session. Eat lunch, calculate possibility for noon session. Eat dinner,calculate possibility for evening session

Day2: Wake-up, calculate possibility for morning intimate session. Eat lunch, calculate possibility for noon session. Eat dinner,calculate possibility for evening session

Well, OK it just kind of repeats from there with regular "You look good in that" statements thrown in.

Also the first time you yell at your husband and he gets quiet, it's not actually that he's just become a great listener. It's that he's taking a moment and thinking "Wait, so there's probably not going to be any sessions today. Yeah probably none at all. Well! Maybe tomorrow!"

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-11T11:00:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-218470>

@[ScottishEmma](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20059/)
A quote from my journal, thought you might find the links on topical DHA and EPA interesting.

_"After putting on the rest of the DHA mix I sunbathed for a while in the morning sunlight, would definitely have got a fair bit of UVA but with things I had to do I didn’t get as much UVB as I would have liked. But with what I did get my skin seems to have developed more of a tan anyway. And my skin liked it. A lot. And my energy levels were really good. So that got me searching for topical DHA and I found this,_ [_https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26747719_](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26747719) _Topical Docosahexaenoic Acid (DHA) Accelerates Skin Wound Healing in Rats and Activates GPR120. The mention of GPR120 had me looking that up, and_ [_https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GPR120_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GPR120)_. Interesting, particularly the mention of rhodopsin. So thinking quantum links to the eye I did a search of rhodopsin in the blogs – several to reread, but have started with Time 11, can you supplement sunlight. Big clues in there already. Lots on topical EPA but one is Topical EPA and protection from sun damage-_ [_http://www.jlr.org/content/47/5/921.full_](http://www.jlr.org/content/47/5/921.full)"

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-16T09:56:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-219037>

I'm pleased to say my boy was happy for me to apply Lugols on him this morning at 7am provided I put it on in the shape of a SPINOSAURUS! He's dinosaur obsessed. I can happily report that my spinosaurus looked pretty damn good despite, you know, applying it on to his tummy whilst he was standing up with only the top of the bottle.

A spinosaurus!

Couldn't get my daughter to comply.

I love the deeper tan that I'm getting on my arms.

@[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) my face didn't like the DHA mix. I started to break out pretty bad. Whenever I consumed fish oil internally I always got pretty nasty acne. (I've had acne since I was 15). Only just recently gone away apart from the two times I was pregnant and I'm not keen on it coming back. I've been consuming a lot of honey recently in chai tea and I'm noticing my skin get bad again.

The sun is out on the East coast of Scotland on Monday and Tuesday so get this, we're actually going to the seaside and staying over so we can get more sun on the Tuesday. We are that family chasing the sun now!

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-16T11:29:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-219040>

I've got hold of the full study of Topical DHA accelerates wound healing in rats and activates GPR120. In the wound healing with it, pro inflammatory mediators come in the first phase, followed by anti inflammatory, so my n=1 could because I don't have a tendency to acne, (had it briefly in my teens, still remember the boy at the school dance who told me my spots weren't so bad in the dark ). The study on the rats wouldn't have accounted for sunlight either .... It could also be the GPR120 connection that is working for me, I'm connecting some big dots with that for my own n=1. "_Its role as a fatty acid receptor was first noted in the intestine, where GPR120 mediates the effects of fatty acids to control the secretion of the incretin hormone glucagon-like peptide 1. Taste sensing is yet another important function attributed to GPR120, as it is highly expressed in the taste buds in the mouth and acts in concert with GPR40 to differentiate the palatability of medium and long chain fatty acids. "_ I'm still going through the study (having to look up what a lot of the terms mean, I could be a while..)
From wiki
**“_G-protein coupled receptor 120_** _is a protein that in humans is encoded by the GPR120 gene.
GPR120 is a member of the rhodopsin family of G protein-coupled receptors. (GPRs).
GPR120 has also been shown to mediate the anti-inflammatory and insulin-sensitizing effects of omega 3 fatty acids.Lack of GPR120 is responsible for reduced fat metabolism, thereby leading to obesity.
Additionally, GPR120 has been implicated to be involved in the ability to taste fats.It is expressed in taste bud cells (specifically cell type II, which contain other G-protein coupled taste receptors), and its absence leads to reduced preference to two types of fatty acid (linoleic acid and oleic acid), **as well as decreased neuronal response to oral fatty acids**.”_

Eating loads of seafood isn't getting me the response I want, so I'm hoping that going in through more than one surface, and optimising light will give me a better neuronal response and turn up the volume on the gene.

If I get a teenage break out I'll let you know.

### JanSz — 2017-07-19T20:06:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-219413>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=219408)

>
> Ha, I don't have much fat going on to keep me warm but I do have a lotta cellulite on my upper back thighs.
>
> Does CT get rid of cellulite?

If you feel cold overall, check your
Cortisol(7;30AM) should be in upper 1/4 of laboratory range
freeT3 should be in upper 1/8 of laboratory range
RT3 should be in the middle
sodium should be 141-143 or possibly higher, eat only unrefined salt, from teaspoon, as much as feel good, at least 1/8-1/4 teaspoon a day
all that on top of food seasoning

////////
You should feel warm in the core of your body (including during CT)

////////

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-23T11:32:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-219807>

Update: -

Quite a lot of pink clothes. I'm off to order purple clothes dye from amazon to turn them purple.

Raynauds seems to be making an appearance the last couple of weeks. Not sure if salt or iodine a cause. I'm going to the docs this week to request full hormone panel for estrogen - seem to have a lot of cherry angiomas - mostly on and around my breasts...

Also thyroid panel. I'm just too fucking cold for my liking.

Decided to approach CT baths properly but slowly. Realised they're probably not cold enough so I'm going to add ice but start very slowly by submerging in a small amount and build up rather than sit in empty bath and fill it...

Almost ready to become a member. Looking forward to hearing the July webinar.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-23T13:32:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-219815>

Emma I had horrendous Raynauds in my 30s. By the time I joined here I had overcome the worst of it years before, but still had a "predisposition". CT was tough to start with, becoming cold adapted from warm adapted wasn't without some bumps in the road. From CT6 "The cold adapted human thyroid FXN: does not bother with T3 at all. Why? When you are supremely LS by cold you go straight to the source, the hypothalamus and make TRH from the brain. The brain controls all thyroid function in cold. Forget the Moose thyroid." Also "If you have been an estrogen collector as a warm adapted hominid (most are too) you will notice some bumps as you adapt. Fear not. It soon shall pass."

I went through numb fingers and toes, (get out now), and a lot of feeling cold afterwards. In hindsight I would have tackled it differently, been a little kinder to myself, I was too focused on losing weight rather than my redox/heteroplasmy. I knew I was getting better at CT when I would start generating heat before getting in to the tub, whilst breaking the ice with a hammer, and became more resistant to the cold (and heat) generally.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-23T19:09:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-219838>

I've remembered another hack I did, when the water wasn't cold enough, but it could help your transition ... I made ice in soap sized blocks, sat in the cool/just warm water and "washed" myself with the soap, just kept it moving over my body to avoid ice burns, to stimulate the surface receptors without it being too extreme. I just flipped it between my hands, but you could use a glove or put socks on your hands to keep them warmer whilst you do it.

Feeling great despite no fucking sun for days and none until next weekend  Less than two months til the move!!!

I'm no forager but I'm getting in more yard plants for a more "edible landscape." Purslane sneaks into potted plants on my patio -- they get added in to smoothies/salads/bone broths; I planted comfrey and the young leaves, steamed with garlic, butter & salt, are fun -- although I prefer spinach. A friend took some comfrey that didn't like where I planted them and now always adds comfrey as fodder for her farm & pet animals. She'll even dehydrate some as they die here with the first frost. (I think comfrey fodder is better known in Australia?) Foraging for Fiddleheads up north was our Mum's compulsory family-activity every Spring.

Alas, I never beat the birds to the wild, or someone-put-them-there, grapes.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-31T20:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-220544>

Thanks to @[fitness@home](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5474/) I used genetic genie to get some info on my SNPs. I almost paid for a report at MTHFR support - is it worth it for more info??

Here's my breakdown from GG. Putting it here so I've got it handy... LITERALLY NO IDEA WHAT IT ALL MEANS!

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

COMT V158M rs4680 AG +/-
COMT H62H rs4633 CT +/-
COMT P199P rs769224 GG -/-
VDR Bsm rs1544410 CC -/-
VDR Taq rs731236 AA +/+
MAO A R297R rs6323 GT +/-
ACAT1-02 rs3741049 GG -/-
MTHFR C677T rs1801133 GG -/-
MTHFR 03 P39P rs2066470 GG -/-
MTHFR A1298C rs1801131 GT +/-
MTR A2756G rs1805087 AG +/-
MTRR A66G rs1801394 AA -/-
MTRR H595Y not found n/a not genotyped
MTRR K350A rs162036 AG +/-
MTRR R415T not found n/a not genotyped
MTRR A664A rs1802059 AG +/-
BHMT-02 rs567754 CC -/-
BHMT-04 not found n/a not genotyped
BHMT-08 rs651852 CC -/-
AHCY-01 rs819147 TT -/-
AHCY-02 not found n/a not genotyped
AHCY-19 rs819171 TT -/-
CBS C699T rs234706 AA +/+
CBS A360A rs1801181 GG -/-
CBS N212N not found n/a not genotyped
SHMT1 C1420T not found n/a not genotyped

First we'll look at a few of your MTHFR mutations. According to research, these mutations are important and can be implicated in various disease states.
You have 1 heterozygous (yellow) mutation(s). These are generally not as bad as red homozygous mutation, but they may still worth paying attention to. They include:

-   MTHFR A1298C

Now let's move on to discuss what these MTHFR mutation(s) mean.
**MTHFR A1298C**
MTHFR A1298C is involved in converting 5-methylfolate (5MTHF) to tetrahydrofolate (THF). Unlike MTHFR C677T, the A1298C mutation does not lead to elevated homocysteine levels. This reaction helps generate BH4. BH4 is important for the detoxification of ammonia. The gene is compromised about 70% in MTHFR A1298C (+/+) individuals, and about 30% in people with a heterozygous (+/-) mutation.
BH4 acts as a rate limiting factor for the production of neurotransmitters and catecholamines including serotonin, melatonin, dopamine, norepinephrine, and epinephrine. A MTHFR A1298C + status may cause a decrease in any of these neurotransmitters or catecholamines. BH4 is also a cofactor in the production of nitric oxide. A dysfunctional BH4 enzyme may lead to mental/emotional and/or physical symptoms. Mercury, lead, and aluminum may act as a drain on BH4.
**All of Your Other Mutations**
Now we are going to look at all of your mutations. You do not necessarily need to worry about all of these mutations, but certain mutations may cause problems in certain individuals. Genetic Genie does not look at the expression of your genes, it only looks at specific gene SNPs. Keep in mind that even if you are homozygous or heterozygous for a certain mutations, it doesn't necessarily mean there is a problem with the functioning of that gene. You have 2 homozygous (+/+) mutations and 6 heterozygous (+/-) mutations.
Here are your homozygous mutations as indicated in your SNP gene table above (not including MTHFR):

-   VDR Taq
-   CBS C699T

Here are your heterozygous mutations as indicated in your SNP gene table above (not including MTHFR):

-   COMT V158M
-   COMT H62H
-   MAO-A R297R
-   MTR A2756G
-   MTRR K350A
-   MTRR A664A

Now it's making me wonder if all of green smoothies at the time had more to do with them. Also, speaking off odd skin features, as I've gotten older (I'm 46 now) I've been getting lots more 'white' freckles. I have fair, Irish skin with regular brown freckles, but have been wondering if all people with freckles eventually get these small, white confetti spots? Or is just an indication of nutritional defeciencies?

### ScottishEmma — 2017-08-18T08:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-221864>

Started looking at my snps

I saw a post yesterday on the forum about the CBS and JK recommending avoiding sulphur foods. I have 4 CBS homozygous mutations.

Totally fucking confused because that includes the cruciferous which would help my estrogen CYP1B1 mutation.

But I'm going to give it a try.

Started taking DIM with a view to balancing estrogen to do with CYp1B1 and general cycle imbalance. So far I'm feeling a positive impact from it. I know, SUNplements not supplements but still.

Doctor is running more labs after I told him I wasn't happy with just assuming everything is fine because my TSH came back within range - didn't test T3 or T4 because no need apparently. I do have cold extremities and feel cold a lot of the time. Not going to get too hung up on labs.

Generally I'm ok. I'm approaching my Winter in my cycle and I've had a couple of intrusive thoughts but no rage which is good. Day 22 so we shall see. I genuinely feel the DIM has helped me.

I blacked out my room and dropped the use of the eye mask after some great conversations with @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) The sleep is definitely different - our room was super bright before.

45 DAYS TIL WE MOVE TO SPAIN

### ScottishEmma — 2017-09-16T15:57:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-223800>

2 weeks until we move to Spain. I'm slightly worried about the RF when we're there as it'll be much more dense population that we're used to and we've got it really good here at our house in terms of no other WIFI signals etc. Hoping the sun negates some of the nnEMF we'll face. I've said to my husband if we get to the apartment and it's seriously bad, we'll just need to move to another house and lose the deposit

I'm seriously getting fucked off with the mast situation here. I phoned a house builder to complain about a mast they have right beside apartments they've built. Like, within 8 feet of them. I took my Cornet to the west end of Glasgow, a place I love to hang out and discovered the RF is really high. So disappointed about what's going on.

I've been taking DIM and Calcium Gluconate for about a month now and this is the second cycle where I've come on 24 days i.e. my cycle has shortenend.

Still experiencing mega shitness after ovulation in terms of low mood, motivation and moodiness. I'm sure the hot sun will help.

I've hacked lighting for Spain by attaching filters that block blue on to various torches so I'm comfortable with what we've got out there.

Dropped CT completely and planning on starting in Spain. It's just too fucking cold and I'm already massively feeling the cold post ovulation. I lie in bed with jumpers and blankets and duvet and I'm still Baltic.

All in all, excited to move to amazing weather and food in two weeks

### Jake Tassell — 2017-09-18T12:07:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-223890>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=223848)
>
> I'm actually learning Spanish and loving it. All my high school French is coming back to me weirdly... And yeah, Greece is next on my list for a move after Spain

My thing is, and has been for a while, "I wanna live at the beach".

My girlfriend (who is French) favours The South of France. I'm more inclined towards Greece, 'cause of the land/sea/sun/sky/people/old skool-ness, and all the obvious things you might think of, but also, personality-wise, I'm a bit of a Zorba. I'm basically a loud peasant who enjoys the company of other loud peasants.

A few years ago whilst on holiday in The Epirus, I hopped over the border to Albania. Wow, that was a revelation. They had no techno whatsoever. No bugger had a cellphone, computer, or anything remotely like that, and, as per Jack's comments re: techno having rendered the Japs neuter, you saw the total other side of that coin: All the women and men there were, like, physically and temperamentally _real_ polar opposites. Soft, dreamy-eyed women with hourglass figures, strapping, muscular, powerful-looking blokes who'd probably never been near a gym in their lives. It was the first thing you noticed, and it was quite startling. Made you think about everything else.

On the other hand, things move so quickly, I'd probably go back there now only to find they've got smart phones coming out of their ears and gender neutral toilets. LOL.

### Jake Tassell — 2017-09-20T10:30:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-224001>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=223995)
>
> hahaha, I had to Google them.

That was a reference to that whole 'Queen-Victoria-smoked-marijuana-to-alleviate-her-menstrual-cramps' schtick.

Dunno if that's true or not, but that story was put about a lot by 1960's drug culture proselytisers, for obvious reasons.

Another one was, "Sherlock Holmes smoked opium", but wait a minute - Sherlock Holmes was a fictional character! LOL.

What they never tell you on those 'psychedelic swinging London' TV documentaries they have on BBC4, was how much of a feature opium was on that scene.

I used to know the painter Duggy Fields who was Syd Barrett's flat-mate. He told me that the Barrett-era Floyd weren't into acid, but opium. 'The Piper of The Gate of Dawn' album probably reflects that.

Ripley's believe-it-or-not: Fields also told me that Syd wasn't particularly into drugs, at least no more so than anyone else at the time. That the 'Brylcreem' thing never happened, that he didn't know if Syd had actually ever taken LSD, and that the whole Syd-Barrett-acid-casualty legend was a mountain of apocrypha and gossip set in concrete by those who sought to benefit most from it.

Fields still lives in the 'Madcap Laughs' flat in Earl's Court Square.
[http://atagong.com/iggy/img/StormWatch/TheMadcapLaugs\_BC.jpg](http://atagong.com/iggy/img/StormWatch/TheMadcapLaugs_BC.jpg)

It looks a bit smarter now than it did then.

A lot of British psychedelia drew on that whole British Empire drug-culture thing. Laudanum-drenched neo-gothic architecture, etc...

I never take drugs, unless you count ciggies and beer.

### Jake Tassell — 2017-09-20T20:59:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-224040>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=224033)
>
> I figured you meant weed  Definitely helps take the edge off, right?
>
> I certainly had my fair share in my teenage years. Now half a bottle of wine and I'm steamboated
>
> I definitely want to know what the 'Brylcream' thing was!

Oh some legendary yarn about Syd on stage being out of his head on acid with a pot of Brylcreem dribbling down his face. LOL.

I mean, I wasn't there, I was at infant school. I can only relay what the Dugster said.

Prior to being told this, I'd also heard similar stuff from another member of that Cambridge circle, along the lines of 'Syd-Barrett-Legend-sob-story-cover-for-Floyd-boardroom-coup'. And then Waters & co spend the next two decades going to the bank making concept albums about The Legend of Syd Barrett. Bizarre. And I met _that_ guy in the middle of a series of strange Syd Barrett coincidences that happened to me whilst reading Julian Palacios 'Lost in The Woods - Syd Barrett & The Pink Floyd'. I remember, I was reading that book on the top deck of a bus about to go over Westminster Bridge, and some guy gets on with a black Fender Telecaster (Syd guitar). Later on that night I was DJ-ing, and talking to a punter up at the decks, I mentioned in passing that I needed a guitar amp, and he says: "I've got one I never use, you can have it if you like". It turns out to be a Selmer Treble 'n' Bass 50 MKII (Syd amp). Then a few days later, I get a call from my agent "We've got a job for you, it's in Cambridge". I went to do the job, the studio complex was like just a few streets away from Syd's mum's house. A short while after that, I went for a casting, and the casting director was called so-and-so 'Barrett', and that sort of stuff just went on and on. It was all very weird with a great big capital W up the front, and a huge ! at the end of it. LOL.

### Jake Tassell — 2017-09-21T10:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-224074>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=224069)
>
> So basically Syd Barrett is your spirit animal?

HAHAHAH. No not really. Basically, I finished reading the book and all the coincidences stopped.

It was proper bizarre. It was like there was something in the causal part of my head-gear superconducting all this stuff.

Good job I wasn't reading 'The Day The Earth Caught Fire'. LOL.

I'm not _that much_ of a Syd Barrett fan, but he was an interesting figure, very talented_,_ and it's an interesting scenario.

My own subjective take is... It's a fast life. The other Floyds wanted superstardom, sports cars, big houses in the country, trophy women, huge bank balances and coke on tap, and they made the probably correct assessment they weren't going to get it with this guy driving, so Syd's all washed up and out of The Floyd at age 21 and Waters & co. go on to achieve their end.

Life can be cruel. If Dylan, Morrison, or Morrissey (LOL) had written "No Good Trying", it probably would have been taken as proof of genius, instead it's cited as evidence of Barrett's 'clinical psychosis', but I think a less hysterical, more down-to-Earth view is preferable to either of those, i.e. It's a quirky, charismatic young guy, not all that long out of Camberwell Art School who's immersed himself in Hilaire Belloc, Bob Dylan and Arthur Rimbaud, and it's a charming record...

Weirdest thing that ever, EVER happened to me. Sitting in a tea shop reading a book - may have been The Holographic Universe - reading a bit about rocks falling from the ceiling inside (not sure what this related to, some kind of poltergeist behaviour I think). I SHIT YOU NOT, this random guy comes in and sits at the table next to me with a young relative. He was clearly not all there. He starts talking to me randomly and tells me about a house he lived in where stones fell from the ceiling. I've never freaked out so much in my whole life. Fucking WEIRD with a capital WEIR.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-09-23T09:09:19+0300

### ScottishEmma — 2017-10-05T07:28:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-225051>

Three days in to our Spanish adventure...

Pros

We're eating the BEST FISH EVER - can't find oysters here but I'm eating anchovies and sardines daily including the sardine heads which I never thought I'd do.
CT in the ocean and pool every day. CT is easy when you live in the sun. Sorry, but living in a cold wet environment (West of Scotland) meant the last thing I wanted to do was get cold and wet. Here, it's a piece of piss because it's so fucking hot!!
Grounding on the beach and in the ocean is fantastic.
The sun all day is amazing. We're outside all day with the exception of the hottest part of the day.
It's £1.50 a bottle of red wine. Which I am consuming A LOT of. And I love it.
Everything in the market is grown in Spain. I'm eating fruit again.

The cons -

there are a lot of street lights where we are.
I've seen a lot of masts but not where we are, on the mountains from the airport to here. I think they're more noticeable here because in Scotland they're hidden by big trees and buildings.
The nnEMF is higher in our apartment, only moderately but still.
We couldn't plug into Ethernet but hoping when we move in 4 weeks our villa will be able to.
There is obviously a fuck tonne more people here than where we lived in Scotland. Everyone is still on their bastard phone.
We've uprooted from our friends and I feel a bit isolated. When my husband goes back to work next week I may feel a bit different.
I have to sleep with air con on because it's just too fucking hot at night.

But all in all, this is the ultimate for me health-wise and the best decision we've made.

### caroline — 2017-10-05T11:16:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-225061>

I am hoping that if we have enuf sun, water and grounding we can handle mostly anything else within reason ......?

How are the kiddos doing Amazing Emma?

All of you will make loads of new friends and prosper and not only grow but blossom and thrive on this optimal journey you are on!

well done Emma!

### Sue-UK — 2017-10-09T18:54:09+0300

I was in Algarrobo just up the coast from you for the week - drove through Nerja, was going to do the caves, but went to the naturist beach instead.  Didn't see the Nerja beach, but the naturist one near there is pretty stony, hard on the feet going into the water, so maybe not so child friendly. The other one past Torre del Mar was the same. But only being there for a week I was in photon harvesting mode. Getting so much sun deffo seemed to help offset the increase in cell tower and population density.

Algarrobo is pretty densely populated, especially where my brother lives, and there's a nearby cell tower, but despite that, the later eating, and my wine intake, I slept really well and I've come home feeling my battery is better charged, and I've had a vitamin D top up.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-11-16T17:02:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-228192>

Been in Spain almost two months.

Feeling great! Drinking too much wine, eating all the carbs. surrounded by nnEMF (except in my basement bedroom at night - but blue light coming in) and fucking LOVE IT.

This was the best move for my family. The sun shines EVERY DAY and we're outside A LOT.

Was told to start wearing sunscreen by a local GP as she thought my face was too red, but then it's always really been red.

The Spanish life is fantastic and we'll be back to Scotland next Summer (I couldn't deal with the Summer here) then back next year. That's it!

I noticed my last cycle here was the first where I've not had major hormonal symptoms. Going to keep an eye on it for the next few months but I think this environment is helping a lot.

Scotland is in my news.

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=217614)

> Figured it was about time I started a journal.
>
> I'm Emma, 38 from Glasgow, Scotland. Being from Scotland I'm naturally hilarious and I swear a lot!
>
> I'm a stay at home mum to two kids, aged 4 and 2 <-------- crazy times!
>
> I grew up in the countryside outside Glasgow and currently live in an amazing ecohome in the country.
>
> Population density within square mile: 15
>
> Haplotype H (Northern European)
>
> My Mother and Grandmother were from East coast of Scotland. Both indoor workers all their life. My gran had Alzheimer's before she died age 75. My Mum is 64 and doesn't give a shit about health preferring instead to watch TV all the time. Both had/have excessively used alcohol. My mum suffered unreal hormonal swings and heavy menstruation.
>
> As for me, I'm pretty amazing. I've been on a health journey since 2007 starting with raw vegan then doing what all raw vegans do and discovering paleo. I've messed around with ketogenic and bulletproof diets but I'd say my diet is pretty dialled in.
>
> I eat local and organic as much as possible. Far from a SUKD as you can get.
>
> I'd say my only 'issues' that I would like help with are my hormonal imbalances around middle of my cycle. I get the rage for a few days and right before my period I get intrusive thoughts and feel anxious.
>
> Other than that, I think I'm in great health physically and mentally.
>
> We're moving to Spain in September to get closer to the sun as there is fuck all sun here. Seriously. Zero.
>
> I'm currently learning about the EMF stuff having just bought a cornet meter.
>
> Having been freaked out by the reading on my iphone I'm working out how to biohack having a phone.
>
> I've just come off all social media and I LOVE IT!!

> Also getting into the CT starting with face dunks which I've been doing a couple of weeks now. Not ready for cold baths yet - our home in Spain in November has an outdoor pool so YAY!

> I'm not an official member of the site yet. I'm reticent to spend much more time online - particularly as I'm now having to use my husband's computer plugged into the Ethernet because I demanded we kill WIFI in the house

> Also, I just spent $1000 on a vaginal kung-fu course (yes, really) so I'm buttering up my husband (not like that) before diving into another online investment. But I've no doubt it's on the cards because when I get a bee in my bonnet (is that a saying outside Scotland?) I totally go ALL IN.

### ScottishEmma — 2018-04-10T18:58:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-239158>

Hola! It's been a while since I logged on.

I was listening to a podcast interview with Jack earlier and figured I should pop on to say hi.

We are now 7 months in Spain and due to return to Scotland for three months at the end of June. Because quite frankly I'd be toast here in the Summer.

The weather is picking up now heat-wise although it's been mega wet - apparently the wettest Winter for 8 years.

I have a delicious very, very light tan - I don't tan well being a pale Scot and all - which I love. I'm pretty much naked outside 80% of the time and still the running joke in the playground as being the half-naked Scottish woman. - My boy has started school.

Generally I feel pretty good. I'm not going to bed at like 7pm any more and even stretching it out until 10pm - GASP! (That is huge for me.)

Sugar has snuck its way back in to my diet so that's my next focus. I reckon it's playing havoc with my moods.

Menstrual-wise (that was my primary 'challenge' if you're new to reading this), I have some months where I'm rocking right through and others where come day 14 (ovulation) I'm a total bitchmonster. My intrusive thoughts all but went away a few months back but I do get the odd one or two.

When we come back here in October we're moving to a different house which will be a fucking godsend because the ALEXA next door (which I can read through the wall) is doing my box in.

But yeah, still the best move ever.

### drezy — 2018-04-12T20:07:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-239306>

FWIW (and in a totally non-creepy way) your body type is similar to my wife's. I can only say that I'm quantized to appreciate that type. Through last summer and with CT my wife lost weight and found it easy to maintain weight afterwards.

She didn't, however, appreciate where nature allocated the weight. I, on the other hand, do appreciate the reshape. There may be tons of BS programming crammed into women's heads about what is "correct".

For my wife it's hips and arse that she doesn't appreciate natures plan for. Any time she complains I always ask her to come closer and show me. When she does I always tell her to "Tell me more about your feelings." I usually gets shoved, by the face, away but she notices that I notice and appreciate.

Having been here a year and pushed through material, I think nature's blacksmith will forge an alluring body for anyone given the chance. Obviously there are 2 possibilities here:

1.  All of this is quantized and built into me is the appreciation for nature's preferred design and I'm part of a long cycle.
2.  Nature has spent billions of years waiting for me to exist and rewards me by shaping females, like flowers, into personally pleasing shapes for my own solipsistic purpose as a thanks for gracing this Earth.

My Y chromosome informs me that it's the 2nd option.

#its\_only\_solipsism\_if\_its\_not\_true

### ScottishEmma — 2018-07-28T15:13:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-246841>

We came back to Scotland at the end of June for three months - I couldn't handle the endless heat of the Spanish Summer...

Being back has been pretty amazing. It's great to be around friends and all the old haunts. We are living in an old farm house in the country with minimal EMF around and no masts phew!

It was nice and sunny but the cloud cover has returned making me realise once again (because I was tempted to just stay back in Scotland - honeymoon period) that we need to be in Spain for health.

Intrusive thoughts have popped back having disappeared at the tail end few months in Spain.

I read T. S. Wiley's book Sex, Lies and Menopause and now _I'm wondering whether I should have another BABY!!

Started using progesterone cream pea-sized amount vaginally (dr cabeca's) the last six weeks or so (only on days 14-28) in an effort to get my boobs back - I know, still talking about boobs - and they do seem a bit better. They don't look like two testicles haha.

Overall I'm good._

### Phosphene — 2018-07-28T17:12:02+0300

Hope to hear more—you really crack me up!

### ScottishEmma — 2018-07-29T08:23:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-246882>

Thanks, ladies.

Yeah, Spanish life is wonderful. The food is fresh, local and not organic haha.

The sun shine every day definitely raised my mood so that when it was cloudy or rainy I noticed quickly I felt down - even though that was my life in Scotland.

The kids loved the beach and the warm weather - although not towards the Summer. Lots of moaning  And my wee boy is speaking some Spanish so that's a gift. My DH enjoyed it (the wine is unreal) but he finds it easier to keep fit over here in Scotland so we may compromise going forward and do six months in Spain and six months in Scotland. That would also bypass tax implications

### ScottishEmma — 2018-07-31T11:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-journal-of-amazingness.19718/post-247063>

So I'm trying out this latitude diet idea.

My husband wants to do it with me but he travels every week with work (tomorrow is North Carolina) so the likelihood of him doing it full-time is zero.

This is my last day before Day 1 of the experiment. I'm excited and probably also very naive as to how tricky it's going to be. I don't even know what fucking grows and when. Apart from wild food. I'm a seasoned forager. But when it comes to 'normal' food the most I could tell you right now is berries. That's it.

I'm interested to see how this goes and I hope that posting in my journal will give me some accountability...

no more chai tea for the next 31 days
or chocolate
or...

What am I going to eat??

How ridiculous that this should even be difficult!!!

# Thread: What's behind lowered thyroid levels?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-behind-lowered-thyroid-levels.19727/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Doyourcycle — 2017-07-03T18:11:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-behind-lowered-thyroid-levels.19727/post-217854>

If cortisol blocks thyroid production why would someone with high nighttime cortisol have low Tsh/t3 to where I'm now hyperthyroid?

Most of my thyroid was removed due to cancer yet my levels are not stable and I'm now lowering my dose again.

Thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-04T01:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-behind-lowered-thyroid-levels.19727/post-217881>

AM sunlight is behind their creation as the picture shows. Without it you'll need drugs.......all Aromatic amino acids absorb light frequencies between 250nm-780 nm to create T3 and T4 from tyrosine and phenylalanine. This is the same place melanin, dopamine, Adrenalin and Noreadrenalin come from too. This is animal photochemistry or photosynthesis at work with visiable light.

# Thread: Your smartphone versus your friends...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-03T18:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217855>

I'm in the process of hacking my smartphone. Ideally I don't want to own one at all.

I've just come off all social media (facebook and instagram) which has meant a lot less 'checking' my phone.

I'm getting used to not satisfying that immediate urge to look something up - you know, random things that come into your head and you just automatically google them?

There's something quite satisfying about waiting to look them up later when I get on the computer at home. but yeah, I guess it's all about convenience.

My biggest obstacle is Whatsapp. I use it to stay in touch with my friends through voice messages (I hate texting as it takes forever). I feel that switching to a normal phone would be fine but I wonder what would happen to my connection with friends.

My question is, if you have rid yourself of your iphone and social media how did you replace that connection with your friends?

Did you notice less contact?

Gone are the days of the landline just phoning people up, right?

It's sad but I genuinely fear I'd have a lot less contact through getting rid of this one app.

At the moment my hack is having the phone on flight mode at all times with the exception of once in the morning and once at night. I take it out of flight mode, allow the messages to download, put it in flight mode, record my replies and repeat to send.

Faffy, I kmow!

So yeah, just curious what others have found...

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-07-03T20:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217867>

Emma, I hardly use my phone and I still maintain my connections with family and friends. My home phones are landlines and everyone knows that they can call and I will pick up and if I don't pick up, leave a message and I will get back with you. Really, the only thing I will use my phone for now is an occasional picture and for emergencies.

I have two lady friends who I now meet with for naked tanning sessions in the woods and we catch up that way, out in nature and it's good for us.

### caroline — 2017-07-04T10:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217921>

I have always hated my iPhone .....hated being a beck and call girl. It is mostly always on airplane.

I keep in touch with a couple of friends on FB messenger - or whatever it is called. Just very short messages when needed.

I have had less contact the last few years anyway .....most people don't get my obsession with all things "Kruse"

I am finding that "real" connections come in different ways these days. Face to Face contact with strangers at the ocean or shopping mall or grocery store or parking lot have been a goldmine of very interesting and exceptional people. We treasure these chance meetings.

When you are open - people are attracted to you........We have made some wonderful friends from chance meetings. My Partner sold his business to someone who casually sat down beside us at the ocean.....

### Inger — 2017-07-04T16:31:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217939>

I never had a smartphone because I knew it makes one addicted, huh!
I have an old samsung phone that I use very little, it does not even have camera, no touchscreen, and no internet.... even this phone is almost too much for me.. I would love to have no phone at all, just land line. I use my landline when talking to friends, or internet at my computer, corded. I do not like talking on the phone or internet much at all tho... I love to talk face to face to people... I never feel lonely, I rather have the feeling I need to run away to find some silent time with me, which I love.....

In the evenings I switch off my landline and cellphone early, no one can reach me.. and that is for me the most valuable time. I love to completely disconnect! That is when everything that happens is so ... real

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-04T18:31:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217946>

> [Mystic Rose60 said:](/goto/post?id=217867)
>
> I have two lady friends who I now meet with for naked tanning sessions in the woods and we catch up that way, out in nature and it's good for us.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=217921)
>
> When you are open - people are attracted to you........We have made some wonderful friends from chance meetings. My Partner sold his business to someone who casually sat down beside us at the ocean.....

That's amazing! You're so right, I've often met fantastic strangers! I'm going to go out with the intention of meeting amazing people, no matter if I see them again or not.  Thank you!

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=217939)
>
> I never had a smartphone because I knew it makes one addicted, huh!
> In the evenings I switch off my landline and cellphone early, no one can reach me.. and that is for me the most valuable time. I love to completely disconnect! That is when everything that happens is so ... real
