# Thread: Hello from Atlanta, GA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-atlanta-ga.19755/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stephanie Rimka — 2017-07-10T04:14:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-atlanta-ga.19755/post-218322>

Grateful to finally be here.

I have avoided joining for 3-4 years when I first started "stalking," er I mean STUDYING Jack Kruse's stuff on here and listening to podcasts etc. I didn't think I could find the time or the cognitive function to implement much on myself while in the intense health crisis that moving into a new home with an unseen mold growth would help be the last step of a perfect storm to give me the gift of being forced to review every aspect of my life and everything I thought I knew. Trying to figure out how to stop the severe psychiatric symptoms the oxidative stress caused in my then 9 year old son was critical. I was dealing with first time ever asthma attacks and relentless allergy symptoms and no ability to breathe or sleep for a year before I figured out to test for mold. Two years later my adrenaline faded and now the symptoms of how I let my body become destroyed over the years has hit me very hard. I didn't lose my practice or my home which was a miracle because I could barely work. Then I blew another disc. Augh. It's all related, obviously. A functional neurologist finally clued me in and said- "this isn't really about the discs at this point, it's why nothing you did before when you did your first one is working." Basically her take on it was- "you had some fundamental problems with EBV and your thyroid that you didn't cure just kept putting into remission that the mold allowed to be unleashed...and now they are all attacking your nerves, and your eyes are a mess, and well, you saw what all the neuro exam revealed." And then the hand tremors started. And I just had to be in bed every second I wasn't seeing patients and using all my energy to help other people.

But the biggest issue why I never wanted to join the form and pay for membership was my fear of cold thermogenesis. All the hell no's that have ever existed is how I feel about cold...welcome to thyroid disorder. Augh. But, I am ready to suck it all up and heal all the things!

I even have started eating disgusting oysters, sardines, and all other seafood that I despise. But, whatever. No pain, no gain? I guess my mom was right, that 14 year (12 years as a vegan) vegetarian stint was making me sick. But it was all the rage in Chiropractic school, from which I hail, although I am not much of a "Chiropractor" anymore but more a brain-based-witch-doctor who has to re-think everything to heal my son and myself.

I do neurofeedback and other neuroplastic therapies based around computers...so my job is a serious health hazard, but I love it and never want to stop helping people, and sort of need the computers to do it.

So I am ready to hack the living fuck out of myself so I can make some high dopamine based decisions for my kid and myself, because my gut has been telling for at least 2 years that I need to move him into the woods to fully recover him and myself...but as kid who grew up in the streets of Detroit, woods and me and bugs and ew.

But I love him more than my love of comfort. So now I am fantasizing about some amazing properties in Mexico. So I am looking to be a better me than I have EVER been so I can find the strength physically, emotionally, and financially to move us out of this city. I have wanted to go further south for over a decade, and I crave all things sun and heat. And too many whispers in my heart are telling me: you can't really heal him here. Move to the beach.

It has basically pissed me off. Because I don't have the "how's" all figured out, and I sound like a crazy person to anyone, and I have an ex-husband to convince I know wtf I am doing and to trust me.

So, I guess I will start by downloading some of those PDFs and print them, bc i hate reading on screens, and see what's my best fit. Or maybe just a consult to net-net it? I have been trying to eat 50 grams of protein in the am and i feel like i will barf. I have been cyclical keto for so long that I am just not that hungry and used to eating 1 or 2 times per day. But realize I am still a hormonal mess, but not sure how much leptin I need to reset. Did the Mastering Leptin protocol about 10 years ago or something.

So my plan is to get the pdf in the Newbie section, I just joined as Silver but can't login yet, get some labs, and do a consult unless someone can suggest a better plan to apply the principles in a healthy manner? There is even more info once you delve in, which I am ready to do inn all the spare time I have like the rest of you!

Already read his book, too. But I basically need like step by step cookbook to implement inn the phases I can. It's harder to figure it out with a kid with special needs or chronic illness...I can't count on consistency of his emotional state right now. But, I am going to figure it out and will apreciate all the gentle nudging to be efficient in time management to get results.

Thank you for creating this place!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

The first goal is to identify the underlying stressor and to address that stressor , to get your autonomic nervous system out of sympathetic mode and into parasympathetic mode which is rest and healing.

Hey Stephanie, Good day!

Say, what was the results on your CBS and BHMT. These are two mutations that can cause alot of issues with your methylation pathways and sulfur metabolism. I am compound heterozygous and have the CBS, and BHMT mutations and have huge issues with sulfur metabolism and potentially high ammonia levels.

I have the dreaded HLA Dr1 gene as well. Just finished the survivingmold protocol for the 2nd time. I had to sell my last house, and couldn't start the 2nd go round until I was out.

Here's a few things that I have tried that I have seen huge benefits from , that you may not have tried just yet.

1.  Ozone Therapy- I started this at Progressive Medical Center 2 years ago in Atlanta after a good friend who is a ND there educated me on it. I moved to Atlanta for 6 months for therapy. I started with UVB - ultraviolet blood irradiation which combines IV ozone therapy and uv exposure to your blood as it reenters the body. I immediately started feeling better first day or two. When I left, I purchased an Ozone Generator from Progressive so I can do rectal ozone therapy at home. I think that this is one of the biggest weapons that I have used to defeat lyme and mold. The start up cost is very little considering the wide range of benefits. Ozone is especially effective for virus as well. It modulates the immune system, kills the bad guys, increases O2 utilization, increases Glutathione production, amongst other great benenfits.
2.  EWOT - Exercise with oxygen therapy, I have my own HBO chamber and love it, but it you cant afford a chamber then I'd stongly recommend EWOT. You can by a mask from Longevity for $40 , a regulator for $35, and get an Oxygen tank from Airgas in Altanta, for $40 per month and you can do EWOT at home 3 to 4 days per week, breathing pure oxygen. Ozone therapy is super important here for increased oxygen utilization.
3.  Eng3 Nano Vi - if you can afford this machine, I certainly would suggest it. It repairs DNA and Protein damage from oxidative stress. It emits Exclusion Zone water that has a bio-identical signal that is the same as the "Good" Reactive Oxygen Species ROS that is responsible for refolding and repairing damaged protein and DNA. I have seen incredible results with my Nano Vi. eng3corp.com
4.  Hidden food sensitivities/intolerances is massive. Have you had yours tested, and if so how?
5.  Sulfur metabolism issues. Most of my chronically ill patients have sulfur metablism issue that have been overlooked for years.
6.  Near infrared sauna, you can make one yourself with low EMF for about $120 in material from home depot. I love mine.
7.  "Destructive Interference Technique" to energetically neutralize infections in your body. Lastly, I also feel that utilizing a technique based on quantum physics called "Destructive Interference" has been one huge key in defeating Lyme and mold. The law of destructive interference says that if you have the exact frequency of something (Freq, A) and then make an inverted copy of that frequency which would be its mirror image (Freq. B) and then emit Freq B into Freq. A, ...the result would be that Freq B will energetically neutralize Freq A. The new noise canceling headphones use this very same technique. So here's how its done with a person. First through ART/Direct Resonance Testing discover the specific strains of infection the patient has, next make an inverse copy of that infection imprinted into a homeopathic solution, next take a laser and laser the inverse frequency of the infection into the biophoton field of the patient. If lasered in at the right place into the biophoton feild, the inverse frequency of the infection will travel throughout the entire biophoton feild energetically neutralizing the infection where ever it may be. This technique has worked extremely well for me and my clients.

Hope this may help in some way!!!!

### WalterNL — 2017-07-21T21:05:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocados.19767/post-219660>

> [Verena1028 said:](/goto/post?id=219652)
>
> I reall y would like to know if there are more postive sites of using foodlike avocado , coconut from elswhere , or negativ...because it s a bad mismatch and i confiuse my body?

Stuff like turmeric can be seen as a useful biohack, we live in very unnatural environments after all so we use unnatural biohacks to deal with it as much as possible.

As for the positive and negative, keeping in mind the seasons when it comes to foods is probably more important than eating solely what grows/lives near you. But if you want to be really sure you have to test how you respond to certain foods like avocado. I never had problems with avocado but I can find other fat sources so dont eat then anymore, I also never had problems with coconut oil, but I had major problems with MCT oil which goes to show least refined foods are better. Its simple trial and error, not assuming anything.

I have been in search for a new job/career. I wanted a job outside and there isn't many. I did find a unique one for a company called Geese Police Inc. Do any of you know someone that does this. Just curious and wondering what others think.

Thank you

Following your bliss is a courageous path. And to keep it relevant, Dr. Jack is a lovely example of a person following his bliss, IMO, as are the people on this site in general since mitochondriac-type of thinking challenges institutionalized (mis)understanding, and all here have my deepest respect.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-14T03:57:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-about-this-job.19774/post-218763>

You are doing the right thing............

Here are 3 signs that someone's dopamine is so low that you cannot help them.

1\. They aren't open to feedback. The term "skin in the game" means someone is willing to be corrected and act on that correction. The key word is "willing". Lots of people talk about self-improvement. Yet they're unwilling to listen to feedback, good or bad. It's difficult to help someone improve when they only want to hear what they want to hear. Low dopamine people hear the orchestra of cognitive dissonance in their ears and ignore wisdom. No, actually it's impossible to do things so i just tell them to save their money and by supplements and diet books.

2\. They are in the wrong job. I'm sure New Orleans Saint's quarterback Drew Brees has some exceptional coaches and mentors. Even if I was fortunate enough to be next in line for Drew's job, there's no way these people are going to get me to superstar level. If we're being honest here, I'm not sure if they could even teach me how to play QB. The team would be better off having me serve in a management role. I am quite good at that.

I'm frequently asked to help people who never should have been placed in the job they're in. Few of them understand how their jobs are killing them. Most of them say they need the money. These people are not aware of the high cost of living with a crushing professional life. I know. I am innovating that change in myself all the time. Being sick is far more expensive than the peace of a steady paycheck at a shit job. However, many "low dopamine" people soar when they are moved into a job that's more suitable for their biology and how they are built by Nature. When they reconnect with nature their skill sets improve and their passion returns and they become interested in curious in things they used to ignore. If you have someone who you think may be in the wrong job, take the money you were going to spend for "helping them" and invest this in retooling their mindset by getting them connected with nature, so they can move into a position that is well suited for them.

3\. You are more invested in their growth than they are. Look, you can't help people who don't want to be helped. This was the hardest lesson for me to learn as a physician and a husband. If you find that you are more invested in helping your tribe grow than they are then it's time to come back down to reality. You cannot keep these people in your life. You've got to declutter.

Wellness creation with a physician is a partnership in sharing knowledge. To be successful, both parties have to work in concert with one another. Be honest. Is the person you're about to help "all in" or are they merely going through the motions? Do they behave like they have skin in the game? If it's the latter, take the time and money you've allocated to this individual and give it to someone who is super excited to be given an opportunity to become the best version of themselves. You'll love your job and you'll never burn out trying to help mankind. This is your dopamine lesson for today.

### JoeT — 2017-07-17T15:52:51+0300

Nights/Rotating shifts are a big NO though! So just try to find something that will give you some flexibility to catch the sunrise and maybe a little more, and then to get outside around midmorning and lunch.

Even driving to work with the windows down can help a lot!

There are a few jobs I have thinking about. One is a Certified CPI instructor. CPI means crisis prevention Institute. The other would be a teachers assistant. I know a school job would be bad but I waited the options some, and that would be not on a computer all day, less work days, less hours. I am okay with less money because its not about that. As for the CPI I can get more pay with that working in a school and as an instructor maybe what I make now or more but this job would also be not working too much in front of a computer with possibly more flexibility. Feedback please.

### Billybats — 2018-07-06T02:56:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-about-this-job.19774/post-245109>

Hi All,

I got laid off. Good thing. Reason why I haven't been on here lately along with job hunting, dealing with unemployment and enjoying the sun. Well doing chores too.

My job finally gave me my June 15th paycheck July 1st. Still waiting on the second one that was due June 29th. Ion June 25th I emailed them saying I wasn't coming in until I received both checks. Long story short a week later they agreed to lay me off. They have been later and later with the past 5 checks this one was the longest. They kept saying they are waiting on the investors money. Yeah they said that the last 4 times. They can't even pay for the parts to do the work. The day I sent that email I filed a complaint to the labor and wages along with other employees. I found out they are getting audited to find out where the money is going.

Now........let us extend Vermont : Is low dopamine in the eye the REAL first step of AI? Is this what Terry Wahl's misses? YEP

Dopamine and melatonin creation begin in the eye. Now that my Vermont lecture is up you can see how the central retinal pathways work.

Now let us discuss how the eye is the beginning of AI conditions:
Dopmaine is created first in the eye in humans and it even affects our immune system.

A study published this month (shown below) examined the effects of dopamine on immunity. The findings revealed that changes to dopamine in the nervous system influences gut inflammation and the immune system. This suggests that manipulating dopamine levels could potentially treat conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis, autoimmune disease, cancer, inflammatory bowel disease, and Crohn’s disease.

Because of the complex relationship between the immune system and the brain, conditions associated with dopamine are difficult to understand when you do not understand how light is slowed down in the eye and why the retina is built backward to take full advantage of slowing light to make things that have mass and structure. Dopamine and melatonin are two things with mass and structure that are created by aromatic amino acids and slowing AM light with IR-A and UV-A light. Crohn's is an AI, and I think it's tied to a lack of AM sunlight, in addition to modern toxic lifestyle: living indoors, nnEMF, and blue light. His low quantum yield lifestyle unfortunately has created leptin resistance, so he has low POMC activity, hence why he needs to "supplement" so much to function. It's clear to me just how important it is to activate POMC, as you get the anti-inflammatory effects of ACTH, the natural sunscreen of MSH, in addition to sexual function and appetite control, as well as B-endorphin for the "feel-good" and pain relieving effects. ### Huck — 2017-07-13T18:33:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2017-video.19778/post-218710>

I think a few years back when I thought I had prostate cancer, I hit on the solution accidentally and never realized what it was.

I was walking 6-10 miles a day. I would go out first thing in the morning and walk at least 2 miles. My route was a 1/4 mile path along the edge of my property, so about 1/2 lap was looking toward the morning sun.

Then I would do the same about noon and another about 6 or 7 pm. I lost a lot of weight which I assumed was from walking and restricting calories. I also felt a sense of well being like I had never felt before.

It didn't last. Maybe it was a balance of light, exercise, and food and I messed up on 1 or more of them. Maybe it was because the season changed from summer to fall.

I have never been able to recreate this experience and maybe it is because I am not walking at the right time to get the right sun exposure. Well, it's still summer, so back to walking on the right schedule.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-13T21:25:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2017-video.19778/post-218724>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=218711)
>
> Jack, love your linkedin blog on sun, shared it recently. Problem is the low dopamine folks are so damn blinded, and still keep believing the bullshit. So many people still counting calories, trying vegan diets, exercising more under blue light, taking supplements, etc.
>
> So crazy to think that the data is out there, but low dopamine literally creates a fog. Industries, media and Government have successfully lowered humans dopamine to the point where we have lost control, and are fear driven that disease like AI, diabetes and cancer are so complex and misunderstood and that they cannot be cured, only treated. And all this info is presented through a blue-lit device. And hence all the obedient idiots...

Here are 3 signs that someone's dopamine is so low that you cannot help them.
1\. They aren't open to feedback. The term "skin in the game" means someone is willing to be corrected and act on that correction. The key word is "willing". Lots of people talk about self-improvement. Yet they're unwilling to listen to feedback, good or bad. It's difficult to help someone improve when they only want to hear what they want to hear. Low dopamine people hear the orchestra of cognitive dissonance in their ears and ignore wisdom. No, actually it's impossible to do things so i just tell them to save their money and by supplements and diet books.
2\. They are in the wrong job. I'm sure New Orleans Saint's quarterback Drew Brees has some exceptional coaches and mentors. Even if I was fortunate enough to be next in line for Drew's job, there's no way these people are going to get me to superstar level. If we're being honest here, I'm not sure if they could even teach me how to play QB. The team would be better off having me serve in a management role. I am quite good at that.
I'm frequently asked to help people who never should have been placed in the job they're in. Few of them understand how their jobs are killing them. Most of them say they need the money. These people are not aware of the high cost of living with a crushing professional life. I know. I am innovating that change in myself all the time. Being sick is far more expensive than the peace of a steady paycheck at a shit job. However, many "low dopamine" people soar when they are moved into a job that's more suitable for their biology and how they are built by Nature. When they reconnect with nature their skill sets improve and their passion returns and they become interested in curious in things they used to ignore. If you have someone who you think may be in the wrong job, take the money you were going to spend for "helping them" and invest this in retooling their mindset by getting them connected with nature, so they can move into a position that is well suited for them.
3\. You are more invested in their growth than they are. Look, you can't help people who don't want to be helped. This was the hardest lesson for me to learn as a physician and a husband. If you find that you are more invested in helping your tribe grow than they are then it's time to come back down to reality. You cannot keep these people in your life. You've got to declutter.
Wellness creation with a physician is a partnership in sharing knowledge. To be successful, both parties have to work in concert with one another. Be honest. Is the person you're about to help "all in" or are they merely going through the motions? Do they behave like they have skin in the game? If it's the latter, take the time and money you've allocated to this individual and give it to someone who is super excited to be given an opportunity to become the best version of themselves. You'll love your job and you'll never burn out trying to help mankind. This is your dopamine lesson for today.

The Sun's light doesn't need to be 'out' for the LIGHT to be there for you to use to do hybrid tanning. This is why cloudy days are often great ways to get balance visible light with very little UV in it to precondition the skin. People who just go out duing mid day when UVA and UVB are out, while never geting any IR-A is why the sun gets a bad name for burns. The diurnal production of light is critical context to understand for health. Now the more controversial position I hold on the sun is that sunburns really do not matter. I don't avoid burns any longer because I understand how to use diurnal tanning to my advantage. ### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-17T03:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2017-video.19778/post-219115>

**The take home from my wife in 2016 was simple about my time spent on line............**_The world is filled with people who, no matter what you do, no matter what you try, will simply not like you. But the world is also filled with those who will love you fiercely. The ones who love you: they are your people.

Don’t waste your finite time and heart trying to convince people who aren’t your people that you have value. They will miss it completely. They won’t buy what you are selling. Don’t try to convince them to walk your path with you because you will only waste your time and your emotional good health. You are not for them and they are not for you. You are not their cup of tea and they are not yours.

Politely wave them along and you move away as well. Seek to share your path with those who recognize and appreciate your gifts, who you are.

Be who you are. You are not everyone’s cup of tea and that is OK, focus on your tribe and forget the critics. You're wasting your life on them._

### Limpei — 2018-01-30T09:09:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2017-video.19778/post-233793>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=218793)
>
> Was it John Ott's book that Jack mentioned the thing about not wearing pink?
>
> So curious about this, my little girl has some pink clothes. I often wondered about the difference between the pinks and reds she wears and the blues her brother wears...

I'm reading John Ott:

"On another trip to Florida I gave a lecture to an advertising club, and after I had finished my talk, Mr. Richard L. Marsh, manager of radio station WILZ near St. Petersburg, told me of a similar situation. He said that some of the staff at the radio station had taken it upon themselves to try to brighten up their surroundings in both the studios and the control rooms by replacing the regular white fluorescent tubes with those of a deep pink color.

About two months later, they began to have personnel problems. For example, announcers began performing poorly on the air. Everyone became irritable and consistently at odds with management decisions and generally difficult to control. Two resignations were received from employees without any known reason for their wishing to leave other than general dissatisfaction with themselves and the staff. Then, one morning one of the men said, “If those pink bulbs aren’t removed I’ll go out of my mind.”

That sparked an immediate reaction, and that very day all of the pink tubes were removed and replaced with the white tubes. Within a week, as if by a miracle, tempers ceased to flare, congeniality and a spirit of working together began to redevelop and resignations were withdrawn. The airwork improved, with mistakes at a minimum."

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-22T00:17:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2017-video.19778/post-237706>

Last year I showed definitely this happened in your retina in Vermont in 2017......ten months later they are telling the world I am right. PHYSICISTS ARE ABOUT THE 'IMPOSSIBLE' -- TURNING LIGHT INTO MATTER
Theoretically, it should be possible to turn light into matter. In practice, well - "easier said than done" is an understatement.
Now, 84 years after the process was first theorized, some researchers reckon they're going to be able to do it - and they're about to start the experiment.[https://www.sciencealert.com/light-into-matter-breit-wheeler-process-hohlraum-experiment-start-2018](https://www.sciencealert.com/light-into-matter-breit-wheeler-process-hohlraum-experiment-start-2018)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-06T03:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2017-video.19778/post-245116>

Mammalian brains produce biophotons with wavelengths of between 200 and 1,300 nanometers—in other words, from near-infrared to short wave ultraviolet range......... Another link to the Vermont 2018 talk.

The brain makes UVC light.........go figure.

[https://www.technologyreview.com/s/608797/are-there-optical-communication-channels-in-our-brains/](https://www.technologyreview.com/s/608797/are-there-optical-communication-channels-in-our-brains/)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-06T04:47:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2017-video.19778/post-245123>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...arkinsons-patient-so-badly.18266/#post-244837](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/why-does-anesthesia-affect-alzheimers-and-parkinsons-patient-so-badly.18266/#post-244837)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-17T13:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2017-video.19778/post-246037>

My Vermont video of 2018 just got more wind in the sails....Janel said, "If it was midnight in the lab, the brain would be forwarded a couple of hours to 2 a.m., which matches what scientists expected from previous research. But they found signs that the clocks in the gut, liver and pancreas were much further ahead as if it was noon."

The mounting research and recent Nobel given for work in circadian biology have researchers astounded by how important this is. If your ⏰genes are disrupted throughout your body, diseases like cancer, neurodegeneration, and diabetes will inevitably be the result.

☀️Melanopsin is a LIGHT sensitive protein in our body. It's in our eye, brain, and skin for starters. The light you are exposed to matters -A LOT! We evolved under sunlight and moonlight/fire for millions of years. Artificial light is still very new for us from an evolutionary perspective and the spectrum can and does differ greatly from what nature provides.

Meal timing is important - we are designed to eat during ☀️ and this has impacts on metabolism as well as our clock genes.

Focus on actions to align your circadian rhythm for optimal health and disease prevention. [http://www.cbc.ca/radio/quirks/night-shifts-put-your-brain-and-gut-clocks-out-of-whack-1.4744868](http://www.cbc.ca/radio/quirks/night-shifts-put-your-brain-and-gut-clocks-out-of-whack-1.4744868)

# Thread: My journey so far......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-so-far.19780/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joanna Wong — 2017-07-13T09:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-so-far.19780/post-218659>

Grandma was from a province called Fujian from Mainland China, married to grandpa and moved to Malaysia. Don’t know much of her health background, what I remembered was that she loved all her grandkids, best grandma ever. She passed away when I was 16 of a heart attack. She was the daughter an opium merchant at the turn of the century before the communist took over mainland China, followed the then Chinese tradition of wrapping her feet. One thing I remember is that her legs had never seen sunlight, they were fair like snow white.

Mum had Graves Hyperthyroidism, operated and then radiated; I was in the same hospital, shared the same taxi on the same day when she got her radiation, I was in my early teens. I strongly believe that because of her thyroid problems, she had cholesterol problems and was prescribed statin and she has diabetes too. I am not very sure how long she had been taking the statin, according to her boyfriend, it was just a while.

On hindsight, I realised that she had been doing all her routine after retirement around the TV set, the satellite dish is just positioned outside the window of where she spent her day, she even falls asleep in front of the tv set. Again, on hindsight, her mental state deteriorated after her cataract operations.

Thinking back, I believe I had had thyroid problems symptoms since my teenage life then, I grew fat and thin with the same food intake and sometimes I could sleep for 12 hours in my early teens, but no adults made the connections then.

I was formally diagnosed to have hyperthyroid at around age 35, had a lot of family issues and very stressed out the, was over medicated with anti-thyroid medication for 2 months and swung to hypothyroid, gained 10 kg within that period. Went to traditional Chinese medicine and did my own lab tests and adjusting my antithyroid med accordingly, struggled for 5 years and became euthyroid, did not know better, never really checked my antibodies then and not able to lose much of the weight I gained.

My mum had her first stroke in late 2014, I was travelling approximately 500km every other week up north to Kuala Lumpur to visit her, that was when I started to wear a Fitbit HR watch, by the end of 2015, my yearly blood test showed that I am swinging to hypothyroidism, further blood test showed that I have very high anti-bodies and have Hashimoto. I was having problems focusing, totally lack motivation, having hot flashes, just a tired walking zombie.

Early 2016, I started taking Lugols iodine, had a 24 hours urine loading test, it showed high bromide and high iodine in my urine, meaning I have very low iodine in my body. Had horrible detox symptoms for 6 weeks, but after that the symptoms just poof and went away. I was hoping that with the iodine intake would take care of my thyroid problem, no such luck, but after years of PCOS and lumpy breasts, my Jan 2017 ultrasound tests show that all my growths had halted and one had even shrunk slightly; my GP downgraded my bi-yearly scan to a yearly scan.

In May 2016 started to take NDT and quickly realised that I don’t convert T4 to T3 well, so, began to take compounded T3.

I don’t know, I think I am quite instinctive, somehow, I stopped applying sunblock to my face, I hardly use sunblock on my body, I am usually in pants, I have two main melasma spots on my cheeks for a decade now, even with my sunblock, then there’s that big write-up on the danger of chemical sunscreen and also the danger of titanium oxides etc etc. So I stopped and my spots go dark and lighten at will, I think they go with my hormones and perhaps copper, but I don’t know which.

I also got a Philips InfraPhil Infrared lamp, at first I was thinking of letting my mum use for her wounds, but it was difficult and not practical in the care home , so, I use one on my face at night to see whether it is good for my melasma or wrinkles, that was before I found Dr Kruse and this tribe. I like the effect on my face because I can feel my sinus clearing up, sinus blockage has been with me since I can remember, it’s the “family” problem, my mum has it, my brother has it. After that, I read about infrared sauna, so, I bought one more and gotten the Ruby lux near the infrared lamp and do my own very basic homemade sauna. I then found out that after the red light, I sleep much better, I have no idea why, but I continued with it, it may not be for the sauna, I will just switch one of the red light on and switched off all the other LED lights in my bedroom before bed. Imagine how I feel when I stumble upon Dr Kruse’s website!

So, now I found this website, I bought a trifield meter, starting to unplugged a lot of kitchen equipment, I am looking for ideas to replace my induction hob, I am not sure whether an infrared hob would do. Trifield meter is not good for RF, so I bought a Cornet 85E. Guess what? I am being fried every day, twice for at least 45 minute each when I am commute to and fro work in the tube in Singapore.

I started to sit in the green field in the CBD during lunch for about 10 min to get me some sunshine, but, this is not good, the RF reading outside is scarier than when I am in the office with all the PC as they are all wired and not wifi, we have a small wifi router but it is not as scary as sitting in a flat grass field in the middle of the central business district amongst all the tall buildings.

I know what I should do, I should move out of this crazy country, but, financially, it is not possible yet. I bought a blusheild plug-in for my home and a mobile blusheild for me to carry around. I know it is not ideal, but ……

My ultimate aim is to wane off from all my thyroid medication, so I will not be held hostage here in this little island of sin, the medication is expensive, and the medical bills are high, the testing is also expensive.

### caroline — 2017-07-13T10:36:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-so-far.19780/post-218660>

Hi Joanna and welcome to journal land.

Please listen to Dr. K's Vermont talk - there is a thread with links.

Can you get outside for sunrise?

what are you eating? drinking?

Do you have all electronics out of your bedroom?

### Joanna Wong — 2017-07-13T11:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-so-far.19780/post-218665>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=218660)
>
> Hi Joanna and welcome to journal land.
>
> Please listen to Dr. K's Vermont talk - there is a thread with links.
>
> Can you get outside for sunrise?
>
> what are you eating? drinking?
>
> Do you have all electronics out of your bedroom?

Thank you! I listened halfway through this morning, will continue tonight.

I usually go outside during lunch time, that is around 1pm sunlight. I only have the luxury of morning sun on Saturday. Weekdays, my morning sun light only will be like the "smoke" break of about 10 min top but will be in the fully loaded nnEMF environment.

I am trying to figure how to eat 50g or protein in the morning, I usually eat 2 raw eggs within 30min from waking up, I know it is not enough, but this is really challenging for me. This morning, I just tried a burger petty with onion and I could not get the two raw eggs in. Work-in-progress.

Lunch and dinner eat out, usually is very low carb and high in protein and fat, I eat lots of shrimps and shellfishes, around 3-5 times a week.

Water is a big problem here, our government has wisely follow the US government and fluoridated our water, because we are a small island and our water source depended on the undependable Malaysia (my beloved country ;p) the govt recycle the water by calling it new water, who knows what is inside the water. I use filter, but cannot get the fluoride out, started drinking spring bottled water from New Zealand, but cannot afford a total replacement. My lugol iodine is taking care of dispelling the fluoride from my system hopefully.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-13T16:38:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-so-far.19780/post-218698>

The Vermont talk 2017 is built for rebuilding nature's wisdom in you.

This is very important in avoiding your dopamine drop.............The eye is the key for Hashimoto's .

Dopamine and melatonin creation begin in the eye. Now that my Vermont lecture is up you can see how the central retinal pathways work.

Now let us discuss how the eye is the beginning of AI conditions like thyroid disease:
Dopmaine is created first in the eye in humans and it even affects our immune system.

A study published this month (shown below) examined the effects of dopamine on immunity. The findings revealed that changes to dopamine in the nervous system influences gut inflammation and the immune system. This suggests that manipulating dopamine levels could potentially treat conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis, autoimmune disease, cancer, inflammatory bowel disease, and Crohn’s disease.

Because of the complex relationship between the immune system and the brain, conditions associated with dopamine are difficult to understand when you do not understand how light is slowed down in the eye and why the retina is built backward to take full advantage of slowing light to make things that have mass and structure. Dopamine and melatonin are two things with mass and structure that are created by aromatic amino acids and slowing AM light with IR-A and UV-A light. [http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982216306753](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982216306753)

So you don't think they eye is where illness and autoimmunity begins? You've got a lot to learn says Mother Nature about the light you live under and see with your eyes first thing in the morning. [https://medicalxpress.com/news/2017-07-eye-microbiome-immune-cells-fend.html](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2017-07-eye-microbiome-immune-cells-fend.html)

### Penny — 2017-07-20T21:55:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-would-you-want-combined-with-methylene-blue.19783/post-219527>

When you get that SkQ1, I want some...

[http://www.aging-us.com/article/100493/text](http://www.aging-us.com/article/100493/text)

**The mitochondria-targeted antioxidant SkQ1 but not N-acetylcysteine reverses aging-related biomarkers in rats**
****Abstract****
Although antioxidants have been repeatedly tested in animal models and clinical studies, there is no evidence that antioxidants reduce already developed age-related decline. Recently we demonstrated that mitochondria-targeted antioxidant 10-(6'-plastoquinonyl) decyltriphenylphosphonium (SkQ1) delayed some manifestations of aging. Here we compared effects of SkQ1 and N-acetyl-L-cysteine (NAC) on age-dependent decline in blood levels of leukocytes, growth hormone (GH), insulin-like growth factor-1 (IGF-1), testosterone, dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) in Wistar and senescence-accelerated OXYS rats. When started late in life, supplementation with SkQ1 not only prevented age-related decline but also significantly reversed it. With NAC, all the observed effects were of the lower magnitude compared with SkQ1 (in spite of that dose of NAC was 16000 times higher). We suggest that supplementation with low doses of SkQ1 is a promising intervention to achieve a healthy ageing.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-02T17:01:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-would-you-want-combined-with-methylene-blue.19783/post-349968>

Today I give you a gift. It is my mitochondriac anniversary on April 2nd. I post this for my Mom. She used to call me at this time every year. Now i am calling you, my tribe. It is time to wake up.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154)

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

# Thread: Sun of a gun

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3

### shiran — 2017-07-14T19:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-of-a-gun.19790/post-218830>

A question about sun exposure :

Here in Israel 31 latitude lots of sun.
Also lots of nnemf to take in count
I have built my sun protocol exposure by intuition
would like to know your thoughts about it:
Since January 2017
almost every day I started AM sun: 20 min / bikini
Almost every day solar noon : dminder app 1-2 hours in average/ bikini
Sun set : not always could get there ... 20 min /bikini

Vitamin d level 90.7 nmol /L
Ordered a bikini from kiniki
Of course no sun screen
No sun glasses

What do you think about putting natural zinc sunscreen on the face only?
Do you think a woman between the ages 20-40 should put protection on here face to prevent Age wrinkles,
Or there is no connection between the two?

Wondering how other people living close to the 31 lactate , exposure to sun is ?
Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-15T02:20:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-of-a-gun.19790/post-218870>

That is a good start. I would say using a spectroscope to map out the IR-A and UV-A transition at the 31st latitude is wise and getting your 23andme.com is wise to figure out how much sun you need is wise. I would not worry about wrinkles........from the sun. Red light reduces wrinkles and sunlight is 42% IR-A. Bluelight from man's devices however cause many skin issues including wrinkles because they are pro growth pro fibroblast and do not allow elastin regeneration........red regenerates cells.

### shiran — 2017-07-15T11:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-of-a-gun.19790/post-218901>

### Wrighturbo — 2017-07-14T22:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-happy-to-be-closing-the-information-loop.19793/post-218852>

Howdy folks, I'm Greg

**Age:45 Sex:** M / F **Joined JackKruse.com:** 6/12/2017

**Location:** Sacramento, CA

**Dx:** None

**Surgeries: Tibial Nail and lateral fibular plate + 12 screws installed about 11 years ago after motorcycle induced distal Tib/Fib spiral fracture.**

**Current Rx: None**

**Current Therapies / Protocols:** Autophagy (daily 16 -18 hr increment on two meals daily), CT in local river cold showers daily, looking into acquiring a big freezer for ice and full immersion barrel/trough to decrease water temps and increase immersion time.

**Health WINS:** shifted diet to increase DHA/EPA and lower Omega6/3 ratio, cleared up pestering rosacea and dropped soo much weight I had to get myself a whole new wardrobe.

**Health Goals:** Longevity and optimum maintenance. Finding a solution to manage the effects of metallic prostheses in my leg that doesn't involve the trauma of surgical removal!

**My Optimal Journal:** None

I practice health on myself and since I had some metal installed in my body I've had some serious ups and down with depression (non-medicated), drive, etc. I've been blessed with good health in that I've never suffered from anything chronic outside of rosacea on my cheeks and jowels which my body healed after cutting grains, dairy, legumes, controlling for high omega 6 foods and upping my intake of DHA in all forms. I'm a health DIY'er and care deeply about self and collective empowerment through health. Health is the signal that I cue off of, with all else being downstream issues for me. Going native, in the sun, eating organic, maintaining an important connection to living ecosystems has always been a no-brainer for me, so, much of what Jack is talking about (once I am able to understand it...lol) has confirmed in $50 verbiage much of my intrinsic approach. That being said, I've had a difficult time finding any reliable information on the long term effects of EMF on metal implants. I've been lucky in that I haven't experienced any joint (knee, ankle, hip) inflammation, but there was a period of time a few years back when I wasn't very active for about 2 months and my lower leg began to ache enough that it interfered with my sleep. I had three theories: 1. I wondered if the metal was acting as antennae somehow and triggering abnormal levels of inflammation. 2. Maybe elements present in the metal were leaching into my body, elevating heavy metal concentrations, crossing the blood brain barrier, etc. and causing problems. 3. The presence of a tibial nail in the center of one of the largest bones in the body was impeding the creation of RBC's contributing to a variety of unquantified follow-on effects. I haven't had the $$'s to get any testing done outside of the Omega6/3 ratio so I'm operating by anecdote. So far, so good! The ache went away once I began exercising (swimming, yoga, long brisk walks) it went away almost immediately. As a consequence, I've maintained a level of fitness that allows me to "straight off the couch' things like rock climbing, mountaineering, long distance hikes over technical terrain, etc. I was introduced to Jack's work through some EMF material I was reading and find Jack's integration and application of biology, physics, and clinical medicine to be a huge breath of fresh air in a field that seems more intent on individual and collective economic liquidation. I had no idea the magnitude of importance light and the timing thereof is to overall and optimum health, EZ water's contribution, the why's of soo many aspects of my inflammation downregulating lifestyle and diet are much clearer now; at least to the depth of the rabbit hole I've fallen down so far. Happy to be a fly on a wall in a community with this kind of depth

# Thread: Breaking the laws of nature
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breaking-the-laws-of-nature.19797/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Technosmith — 2017-07-15T22:43:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breaking-the-laws-of-nature.19797/post-218964>

Hey folks,

This guy Darko Velcek - [https://darkovelcek.wordpress.com/2017/06/02/ketosis/](https://darkovelcek.wordpress.com/2017/06/02/ketosis/) - strongly believes that humans should not be consuming dietary glucose at all and that it is toxic (he advocates fructose instead)

Without some form of processing, glucose is locked away in vegetable cellulose matter, and only gets released in significant amounts when consumed through cooked foods.

It kind of makes logical sense that the body would not do anything to harm itself, and therefore any glucose in the bloodstream produced via gluconeogenesis would be the exact amount needed at that particular time. However, perhaps by manipulating plant foods through cooking and then eating it, subjects the body to an uncontrolled amount of glucose that challenges it beyond what it can cope with? If done on a regular basis this is an ongoing stressor.

Also see this article he wrote - [https://darkovelcek.wordpress.com/2015/03/05/fructose-the-toxic-sugar-or-is-it/](https://darkovelcek.wordpress.com/2015/03/05/fructose-the-toxic-sugar-or-is-it/)

Is cooking potentially another way in which humans are breaking the laws of nature and thus creating health problems?

Your thoughts?

### drezy — 2017-07-15T23:21:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breaking-the-laws-of-nature.19797/post-218969>

Oh no! Are you going to break my heart about my love of grilled lamb with a korma dipping sauce?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrise-or-dawn.19799/post-218980>

When waking up to catch the morning sun - can we start at dawn before full sunrise? What is the optimal window for catching UVA?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-16T01:15:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrise-or-dawn.19799/post-218985>

The window depends upon your location on Earth. UVA shows up based upon latitude and altitude

>
> The window depends upon your location on Earth. UVA shows up based upon latitude and altitude

A UV Checker would be helpful. Still researching one (the cheap ones don't have good reviews).....but I've been wondering if this would work.....for example here at the the 40th latitude sunrise was 5:40 am, and it looks like there were trace amounts of UV-A by 7 am; but the real transition appears more likely between 8 and 9.

[http://uv.willyweather.com/nj/middlesex-county/woodbridge-township.html](http://uv.willyweather.com/nj/middlesex-county/woodbridge-township.html)

Kvond....here is a helpful lesson from me. I was so focused on trying to hack my Vit D that I was getting minimal sunrise exposure and almost no other AM exposure, and then spending an hour outside at Solar noon. Not optimal.

Once you catch the sunrise, just get as much sun as you possible can during AM hours. I've adjusted my schedule and commute to max the AM out. I still go out in the pm, but then my body is properly prepped by morning IR and UV.

### drezy — 2017-07-17T02:29:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrise-or-dawn.19799/post-219105>

OK. Beware of uber dork engineers (like me) we can overcomplicate things like nobody's business. I just hopped out of cold water and my clarity is higher now. Simplicity mode.

Imagine, on our round clocks, the hour hand's position from your sunrise (5:49 these days but it changes over time) and solar noon (1:07pm for now but it changes). My reference ( [https://sunrise-sunset.org/us/newark-de](https://sunrise-sunset.org/us/newark-de) ) If you want UVA a good rough rule is to get 15-60 minute of UVA light. I always get it and don't think about it anymore by getting sunrise light to about 1 hr past sunrise light on my skin.

Like you I'm curious so I've actually just ordered that UVA module I linked to get a good look for myself. Once I get a sense of how the cycle works I'll be open to shipping you the unit. Just keep in mind that developing the habit of being in the early morning sun is the important part.

Kathy

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigSkinny — 2017-07-16T01:41:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sun-diego.19801/post-218991>

I look forward to learning and applying more and more.

BS

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-16T03:04:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sun-diego.19801/post-219009>

We do too........begin with a journal. The beginning is always starts with today. Living in SD does nothing for you if you miss the right light consistently. That is the story of Vermont 2017 talk. Watch it. Have you ever seen the dawn? The monk said 25,000 happens in alife. Be honest and tell me in SD how many have you seen? Not a dawn groggy with lack of sleep or hectic with mindless obligations and you about to rush off on an early adventure or business, but full of deep silence and absolute clarity of perception? what did that mean? It means owning a gym membership and never going to the gym will do shit for you. It is akin to placing a fit bit on your Roomba and expecting to lose weight. A dawning which you truly observe, degree by degree as light changes from IR-A to UV-A is the most amazing moment of birth of the mitochondriac. And more than anything it can spur you to action. Have a burning day Big Skinny..........I'm ready to help you if you've got your skin in the game and your eyes in the AM light. Welcome.

### BigSkinny — 2017-07-16T17:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sun-diego.19801/post-219056>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=219009)
>
> We do too........begin with a journal. The beginning is always starts with today. Living in SD does nothing for you if you miss the right light consistently. That is the story of Vermont 2017 talk. Watch it. Have you ever seen the dawn? The monk said 25,000 happens in alife. Be honest and tell me in SD how many have you seen? Not a dawn groggy with lack of sleep or hectic with mindless obligations and you about to rush off on an early adventure or business, but full of deep silence and absolute clarity of perception? what did that mean? It means owning a gym membership and never going to the gym will do shit for you. It is akin to placing a fit bit on your Roomba and expecting to lose weight. A dawning which you truly observe, degree by degree as light changes from IR-A to UV-A is the most amazing moment of birth of the mitochondriac. And more than anything it can spur you to action. Have a burning day Big Skinny..........I'm ready to help you if you've got your skin in the game and your eyes in the AM light. Welcome.

Yes. As early as it actually can be seen. We do not have a flat geography here. So I sit, grounded and wait until the clouds burn off as we tend to have a light marine later most mornings and I am usually getting about anywhere from half an hour to 45 minutes in absolute bliss surrounded by trees, song birds and even bunny rabbits before UV kicks in, at least based on my D minder app. But I would not call it the dawn as it isn't usually visible til around 7 am or so. I either walk right out in front of my apartment or, as I just related, I go down to a local Lake we have after dropping my kid off at school. Is this actually doing anything for me? Then once the UV does kick in there's a huge hill by this Lake and I workout barefoot on that Hill with squats and doing HIIT walking up the hill walking back down the hill. I honestly don't spend too long trying to catch UV at that time of day because it's not as efficient as going out at noon. Should I avoid other light sources before I go out to sun gaze? Thank you for your help Jack.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-16T18:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sun-diego.19801/post-219059>

Is there's still time to change the road you're on........Do you hope so??? Adversity is inevitable, but difficulties or misfortunes don't have to keep you from achieving your intended goals and finding the happiness you seek in life BigSkinny.
It's how you overcome these adversities can make all the difference. The road less traveled requires you to open your mind by closing your eyes to see the unseen world within you which lies in within you. Hope never abandons you, you abandon it by choices about your light environment. That is the story of Hope built into my 2017 Vermont video. Everyone always asks me about my wife.........well now you can see she was the slow poke to my message but boy has she gotten it right. Instinct is wisdom we come equipped with and my wife learned to tap it after 7 years of my chronic barrage and alchemy. Nature is a re-doubtable alchemy at work behind impenetrable veils of beliefs as the forest prepared its nocturnal mysteries as we sleep. The best way to make your dreams come true is to wake up. My wife woke me back up by telling me to stop helping others and begin to double down in helping myself once again like I did 12 years ago. She does not want me sacrificing my wellness for anyone but myself. That is the deep story between the words of Vermont.........buried in the comments I made there on the You tube video. They seem to be important for you to hear and see..................Begin.

Thank you for your comment and encouragement. I can still go to Mexico at the end of my job. The way it is in Cyprus we can get work from March until November and after that there's hardly any work available until March the following year and I refuse to work any indoor or office job. So I'm working hard constantly for these months to save money to travel to the equator and Yucatan to improve my health. The thing is that I can't afford to spend my savings to stay at an expensive hotel. Ofcourse that is my choice.. (actually the choice of my wallet lol).. for me, staying in Yucatan and around the equator for at least 3 good months will cost the same as staying 1-2 weeks at the hotel resort.. so the travel for 3 months will be better for me.
And Of course if I had the money I would choose the hotel resort, AND the travelling to Yucatan and equator AND attending all the events and meetups to meet all the members.. but until I find a way to make good money whilst trying to be optimal, i will have to continue make choices between all these on my lifeguard salary.

### Antonis — 2018-08-21T09:11:25+0300

> I've regularly searched the web for metal containers and horse troughs near you. Nothing came up yet, but I'm done now.
>
> If you complain you can't also cultivate gratefulness.

Interesting response full of real rudeness and condescending ignorance.

By linking to that thread it seems you are still offended by my words there.. You say other people are low dopamine people but aren't you if you keep thinking about this? Anyone reading that thread will see that I meant some people spend a lot of money on unnecessary items, and that I don't have that sort of money- but even if I did I would live a simple life without such items... Also anyone who's been in the threads long enough would know that I often write English badly. Not intentionally but just because I'm still learning it and my words sometimes read out as abrupt or I phrase the sentences wrong. I've apologized about this a few times in different places and still continue to try to make my English better. I guess you simply can't or won't accept that and you jump on and quote my badly written English statements as some sort of ammunition. Well done for making a fellow human feel like shit for trying to learn and speak in a language that is not their own. Every non-native English speaker should have you as their friend.

Where am I playing "stupid fucking identity politics game"? When I say I'm from Cyprus and trying to save money here on a seasonal job? When I say there is no winter work here? When I say I don't have much money to spend on things that are expensive for me? When I show that I'm literally at my own 'meager beginnings' stage and trying to get my finances going somehow? When I refuse to work any job working 40 hours a week in a toxic office environment just to try to make more money? I don't get what you're saying.

I think you can understand from my comments in the thread here that I don't have the funding (money) and I've mentioned my lack of funds in other threads too so your statement of me being "too egotistical to share that you don't have the funding" doesn't quite make sense AND contradicts your own statement of me playing 'stupid identity politics game'. And how do you know I'm not already seeking other people's help to better my finances? Nah, just take a snapshot of my comments in the forum and classify me!

Thanks for keeping an eye out for horse troughs.. I did manage to find one myself.

I would say at this point that I never expected people to be so rude and condescending to other people who ask questions in the hope of bettering themselves.. especially questions relating to money or cheaper options because not everyone has as much money as they need right now to do certain things.
People should also understand that there are others who are striving to learn but may lack the language capabilities to ask the right questions, the right way or say the right things the right way.

BTW. I had to get help to write this in good English so you could understand me.

You said a day pass may be an option but you don't recommend coming at all if we're not staying on the grounds, which answered my question, thank you.

I wanted to come to this particular event more than the other events because it is in the special place Yucatan. I was thinking I could get both great knowledge and natural healing in one.

Do you think sharing a cup of coffee with me is a worth while investment once a month? If so.......then come join my journey on Patreon where I will take you the ride of your life through the quantum biology of humans. You will see sides and perspectives of the human system you've never contemplated before with my own narrative ideas of why it matters to you, your health, and your mitochondrial colony in your tissues.I will be releasing my coming book's ideas in this format. This will be a way to get in on those ideas and mito-hacks early. I think my ideas are worth a cup of coffee, once a month now we'll get to see who feels the same way. # Thread: Hi from South Africa
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-africa.19821/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jilli13 — 2017-07-18T11:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-africa.19821/post-219239>

Hi All,

I'm Jillian, aka Jilli.. I hope you are all well.

I've had a weight issue since I was 3 months old ( yes, believe me, our baby clinic nurse put me on my first diet at 3 months). I've had a myriad of weird diseases, from Tick bite fever ( what the rest of the world calls Lyme disease) and scarlet fever ( the only child in the area to get it ... sounds funky hey?) both before the age of 4, I'd get sudden very high fevers and a red rash that would come and go as a baby and toddler. At age 6 I had Bell's Palsey, and trench mouth all in the same year. Between the ages of 6 and 14 I'd get terrible tonsilitus, so was on copious amounts of anitbiotics, my immune system was totally shot.

I started puberty early, at age 10, and later developed Polycystic ovary syndrome with complications so bad, I had to have a full hysterectomy at the age of 36, I'd never had children.

And I've suffered from depression and anxiety diagnosed by the "medical" professions as Bipolar, for as long as I can remember....my first memory of being depressed and anxious, being around 5/6ish.... from 2011 to mid 2015, I was medicated to the point of being a walking zombie .

At the same time that I was diagnosed with Bipolar, I was also diagnosed with an autoimmune disease, CIDP, Chronic Inflammatory Demyelinating Polyneuropathy, I'd lost all feeling in my feet and lower part of my legs, and was (still am) loosing feeling in my hands, not because of diabetes, but because my immune system was (is) attacking the myelin sheath around my sensory nerves.

I'm not telling you all this for you to pity me, but to understand what pushed me to, to start hacking my life...

After being under treatment for both my "Bipolar" for almost 4 years, and actually getting worse I stumbled across articles about alternative plant medicines, to treat depression, like Ayahausca, and different ways of looking at diseases.

At this point I was being hospitalised once a month to have intravenous gammagobulin (IVG) treatments, to try trick my immune system into attacking the antibodies in the "gammagobulin" instead of my myelin. All this really did was push me over from being just insulin resistant, without sugar issues to being again diagnosed by the "medical" profession as type 2 diabetic. The reason for the latter the gammabobulin that was being pumped into me once a month was held in a PURE SUCROSE solution, 2 litres once a month pumped directly into my veins... I'd go in with a sugar reading of 7mmol/l \[126mg/dl\] and I'd leave with a sugar reading of 20mmol/l\[360mg/dl\] to 25mmol/l\[450mg/dl\] ... feeling like absolute ...BEEP..

I've never really trusted the medical profession, all my life I've been told my weight issue was because I ate too much and didn't get enough exercise, as a child I was on the swim team in primary school (what you call grade school) and a teenager I was on the netball team (an English version of basketball) and I walked to and from school. I never sat in a corner and stuffed my face, the image I imagined every doctor I've seen, has in their minds of me... The neuro who treated me for the CIDP, even told me, but you put food in your mouth, when I told him I don't eat a lot... I didn't then and I still don't...

It took seven different gynecologists to finally diagnose the PCOS, all of the first 6 telling me it's because of your weight, and putting me on birth control for 3 months to sort the cycle, only for it to start all over again as soon as the birth control was finished. The last doctor, was the first to actually test my insulin level... the first test with a sugar reading of 4.8mmol/l \[86.4mg/dl\] (very good), my fasting insulin level was 52mlU/L\[361.14pmol/l\] (VERY VERY VERY BAD)... he sat me down and looked me in the eye and said your Insulin level is high enough to kill an elephant... I laughed, I didn't understand what he was talking about... he put me on the maximum strength glucophage (aka metformin) for 6 months and a low sugar diet to try bring my insulin down ... I followed it religiously... 6 months later sugar levels still good at 4.8mmol/l \[86.4mg/dl\], but my insulin level hadn't decreased it had increased to 58mlU/L\[402.81pmol/l\]... we were at a loss... clearly metformin didn't work on me ( we now know it does nothing for insulin)... so basically we'd work on loosing the weight and controlling the sugar... which wasn't a problem at this stage.. I was 27 ... 9 years later I'd have a full hysterectomy and miraculously and weirdly suspiciously by insulin level would drop from 52mlU/L\[361.14pmol/l\] to a fasting insulin level of 26mlU/L\[180.57pmol/l\], but with a sugar average now of 7mmol/l\[126mg/dl\]... a healthy insulin level is, as far as I can pick up, between 2-6mlU/L\[13.89-41.67pmol/l\].

Nothing added up, the more I researched the more frustrated I got, the more I told the "medical" professional I saw, that something wasn't right the more I got that... OH SHE'S A SELF DIAGNOSER... look and treatment as if I was a hypochondriac ( which I KNOW, I am not)... being laughed at, humiliated and belittled on a regular basis, eventually one starts thinking but maybe ....

I carried on researching, I found alternative plant medicines, which lead me to stop taking my psychiatric meds cold turkey, and within 4 months of doing this, I took part in an Ayahausca ceremony, the 1st of five I've done since mid 2015. Before I stopped the meds and did the ceremony, I hadn't been able to hold a job for longer than 6 months, I slept all day and clung to facebook for a connection to something, I weighed my heaviest at approx 146kg\[328.5lb\] on a 170.69cm\[5'6"\] frame... after the ceremony my depression got better, slowly it's not a magic pill, but it pushes you to move forward, I really started researching and following my heart...

I discovered Keto, yes I understand it's not for everyone, but it really helped me not just in loosing weight but in helping control my autoimmune... since that first ceremony, I've lost 38kg\[85.5lb\], I've fired my neuro, because poisoning me to make a buck, is just not on... although he truly believed, at least I'm giving him the benefit of the doubt here, that the IVG was helping... it's been just over a year and three months since my last treatment... and I'm controlling the progression of my autoimmune with Keto and newly introduced intermittent fasting..and how important gut health is especially with regards to leaky gut and gluten..

I live in the dark as much as possible, yes I know you understand what I mean, all my lights are yellow light, I try avoid blue light at all costs, so I stay away from malls etc. small problem is work as I haven't found away to convince my employer to put in yellow light bulbs... and I can't find anywhere local to buy the blue light reducing glasses to fit over my glasses. ( South Africa is wonderful light wise, but we are a bit behind in certain things)..next step is convincing her to let me come in after 10 am and leave at 7pm...

I really want to incorporate the Cold therapy, into my routine, especially as my autoimmune is a neurological degenerative disease, but here I have to admit, the anticipation of the pain involved scares me... I live with a pain level between 6 and 10+(very bad days during flares) daily, and extreme heat and cold does have a tendency to push the pain to truly unbearable levels... but at 44 and knowing what going against all "medical" advice, has shown me and taught me... I will.

In all of this I am very very grateful, for the experiences I've been through, without them I'd have been one of those people who believed all the lies, mis-truths and misguided understandings of the "demigods" .

I'm truly humbled and eternally grateful for people like Dr Jack, Dr Jason Fung, Dr Tom O'Bryan, and Dr Tim Noakes, for all exploding out of your boxes, for questioning and being curious.

If it wasn't for you all, I wouldn't have the courage to hack my life.. actually I might have, but I'd never tell anyone about it.

MAHALO, thank you from the bottom of my heart.

and thank you all for reading my rather long winded introduction... it was meant to be a simply...

Hi I'm Jillian from Johannesburg South Africa, I hope you have a wonderful grateful day. I'm truly grateful to be here.

Mahalo...

### JoeT — 2017-07-18T20:13:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-diy-material-for-blocking-emf.19824/post-219285>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=219283)
>
> I know testing is number one. Living in an apartment IMO is a double wammy. So I know without testing I need something. As much as I don't want to be on the internet I need to read of what materials effect different EMF. I just don't want to mess up and short something out or cause more EMF. So I have some reading to do.

Well, just to help out a fellow NJ'er.....here is what I've learned.

Unfortunately concocting DIY products for EMF blocking is not easy and not feasible for most.

1\. Magnetic/Electrical fields from the electrical distribution system are most difficult, and require very special and expensive products, like Giron. Luckily, the drop off distance is fairly short, so you mitigate these by staying away. Move the bed at least 6 inches from wall if you can, and stay away from running appliances.

2\. Radiofrequency is also very difficult......it has to be wireless and therefore penetrate most building materials! The link I sent you would be hard to beat for the money etc. Other products, such as bed canopies, are expensive.

3\. The most important one to block, by far, is blue light, which luckily can be done with an $8 pair of Uvex glasses.

You should also check your labs and see if you have any issues clearly related to EMF.
In short, blue light destroys DHA, and the other EMFs dehydrate you.

So, for DHA, just eat plenty of seafood and wear blue blockers especially at night. Ideally you would test your Omega 6/3 ratio, but you'll need a really good doc.
For signs of possible EMF related dehydration, check your Bun/Creatine ration. Should be 7 - 10; and look for signs of calcium efflux (Calcium is a high and Potassium is low (below 4 would be very bad). Staying off a cell phone (both voice/data) will go a long way; as will hardwiring your computer (Ethernet connection plus monitor/keyboard/mouse). You don't really want to be touching a laptop.

Hope that helps.

### Sufoto — 2017-09-28T08:05:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-diy-material-for-blocking-emf.19824/post-224409>

Shielding is possible on the actual meter but the pulse that goes through your wiring in your home can not be shielded and happens many thousands of times a day. I am now trying to figure out how I can have my new smart meter removed (which was installed while we were away on vacation). Electro-sensitivity is a recognized disability and I know it will be a fight but worth it. I will use that as my opt out valid excuse. In the mean time I won't be working in my office where the smart meter is on the outside wall.

# Thread: My High Level Overview: Light, Water & Magnetism
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-high-level-overview-light-water-magnetism.19828/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 45

### kris90 — 2017-07-18T21:29:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-high-level-overview-light-water-magnetism.19828/post-219293>

Lately, after dinner, when I sit outside on my deck watching the sun set and enjoying a glass of malbec, I've been taking out my notepad, and wanted to share some of my thoughts:

**Sunlight:**
\-IR-A light builds an EZ in water, UV expands the EZ
\-The eye slows down sunlight to make things with mass & structure
\-Light in the eye must be slowed as it travels peripherally through the body (eye clock must run faster than peripheral clocks)
\-UV light is important for signalling. Tightly coupled = signalling (run from lions), Loosely coupled = less signalling, more heat (stay warm)
\-Strong light tunnels electrons across ETC, increasing DC electric current (increased ATP, making more water to bind to proteins & unfold them)

**Magnetism:**
\-Cold/Dark increases magnetism via increased oxygen tension
\-Oxygen is paramagnetic, so increased oxygen uptake in cold/dark = increased electron tunneling (fat burning) via increased magnetic flux in mitochondria
\-More electrons = more chemically reduced = less ATP, more heat = less ROS for signalling
\-Cold expands water, IR light is released from mitochondria to shrink water, bring respiratory proteins together, further improving electron tunneling

**Water:**
\-Forms a battery when irradiated by IR light
\-Battery is further charged by UV light
\-Provide the free electrons to drive 100,000 biochemical reactions per second in every cell
\-Natural faraday cage to nuclei in cells, protecting and inhibiting nuclear genome activity
\-Made by the mitochondria via electron tunneling (i.e. cellular respiration)

Basically, life originated from the sea. When we stole a mitochondria hundreds of millions of years ago, we brought the sea inside the cell. Therefore, the goal of life is to maintain that sea (cytosolic water). This is the water that surrounds the nuclei, to protect the integrity of our DNA. The mitochondria makes the water via tunneling electrons. Electrons are driven by light and/or magnetism.

That being said, to me, the goal is using light and/or magnetism (interchangeably) to maximize our mitochondria's ability to make water. In summer, we just needed to soak up the strong sunlight, and be as grounded as possible to collect the free electrons. In fall, we needed the mismatch of light slowing down in our environment relative to photoelectrically created energy (food/carbs). The mismatch slowed the central clock relative to the peripheral clocks, triggering insulin to slow down the light from carbs to create mass (fat). Basically, in fall, electron tunneling slows to make mass (E=mc2). Then in winter, when trees stopped making food for us (CHO), they become more chemically reduced by releasing more O2. This increased O2 tension in us, and increased electron tunneling via increased magnetism, and we burned all our fat before spring. Spring slowly decreased O2 tension as sunlight became stronger to tunnel electrons.

Foolproof system, until man learned to alter the environment. Fast forward to today, and we have a laundry list of interfering environmental cues that negatively impact water production in our mitochondria to maintain life:

\-Artificial light
\-Man made EMF
\-More time indoors, less time outdoors
\-Artificial heating, and poor air circulation
\-Sunglasses, clothing, sunscreen, skin products, tattoos
\-City living, industrialization/power-grid, lack of trees, car traffic/air pollution
\-Chemicals in food, water, etc

So we need to become a mitochondriac and understand light, water and magnetism so we can biohack our environment:

1\. Move AWAY from interference factors mentioned above (hard pill to swallow for most)
2\. Increase sunlight exposure and outdoor time in an undisturbed environment (especially AM sun)
3\. Drink clean, natural spring water (EZ water)
4\. Increase magnetism (grounding is best, CT can be used to biohack)
5\. DHA (electrons, as Jack says, sunlight's "catcher's mit"
6\. Blue blockers to protect eyes, and clothing to protect skin when stuck inside
7\. Fasting when trapped inside (eating outside has always been favorable in my N=1)

As a mitochondriac, we need to ELIMINATE first, then MITIGATE, not the other way around.

Thoughts?
