@[Billybats](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20191/) I would say for at least a year, I'm always learning more everyday and between my son and I we share info and questions and I read everything I can of Jack's posts and watch all the podcasts. I do the cold shower in the morning and evening before bed. I have never worn sunglasses or sunscreen, I have been fortunate to be a stay at home Mom and haven't had to work under blue light since 1988. I exercise in natural light, I have been exercising since I was 18 and I'm now 55. I rise with the sun and get as much AM sun as possible. I follow Ketogenic diet for a few years now, I do eat some carbs in the summer. I stopped wearing nail polish a while ago, I don't put anything on my skin anymore, not even perfume. I go to bed when I would need lights on so I don't have to be using them. I have blue blockers for the odd TV I watch or when on laptop. I have f.lux installed on my phone and laptop screen to block bluelight. I'm working on getting my internet hardwired instead of wireless. My son and I have bought a Coronet meter and measure our homes to continually check how things are and make adjustments. In the winter I use red lights at night if I need lights on, I think this winter I will try candles. I have a near infared light and a UV light as well for days when there is no sun. I have things I'm working on, I need to get more DHA through eating seafood, I would love to not wear makeup anymore. Maybe I need to travel in the Winter to Mexico or maybe one day relocate. I find it difficult to connect with anyone as I live and think differently than the people around me. For now I'm doing what I can and everyday is a new day!!!

My thoughts:

Do you really know what I stand for? I stand for light, water, and magnetism and put food much further down on the list than most other people. Do you know why? Have you ever asked why I do this? I do this because my perspective as a mitochondriac is quite different than the rest. I want you to examine why I say as I do. The physics of cells is far more important than the fuel we put into a cell. If you doubt me, I want you to think about a Ferrari. Does it go 220 miles per hour because of cheap gas we put in or is the engine tuning the most important part of the equation? The physics of the organization of cells is the key to understanding how the basal metabolic rate of mitochondrion are set and this determines the life you get. The lower your basal metabolic rate the more life you get says all the metabolic scaling laws that we know about. As a neurosurgeon I know how our brain separates use from every other animal and how that scales to modern neolithic diseases. When inflammation is present in the human brain, three fundamental laws of nature are altered. This causes our blood to decrease their ability to deliver higher levels of oxygen to the neocortex below to maintain proper cognitive function. This decreased oxygen density over specific portions of the neocortex acts to limit its function, relative to other areas of the neocortex. This single event acts to uncouple’s mitochondrion in the neocortex and it raises that neuron’s basal metabolic rate. A chronic raised basal metabolic rate leads to many cerebrovascular diseases you all know about because it increases mitochondrial respiration and the amount of physiologic work a cell has to do. The problem is today in medicine they do not appreciate this fundamental linkage. You must if you are to win the battle of health. My book is beginning for this discussion. In the end, science is just a progress report of where we are now in our understanding. It is not our final destination, but it is a data point on the road to understanding to the deepest wisdom nature provides us. This is precisely how leptin resistance causes neuro-cognitive decline in humans. The CSF around the hypothalamus becomes less dense and has a higher temperature with higher levels of cytokines in it, causing us to lose of energy to the environment. We’ve know for a long time that only two things lower metabolic rate in living things, strong solar stimulus of light and cooling. That is it. The problem for medicine is that these scaling laws of energy flux have not made it into clinical textbooks of medicine yet. My book kicks that idea down. This loss of energy means less oxygen is delivered to the neurons in the hypothalamus, which is designed to work optimally when there is harmony and energy balance in humans. This is why in the EMF 2 blog post long ago, that the leptin receptor was an ‘account’ for protons and electrons. All bits of matter that make things up roughly have equal numbers of electrons and protons. The basics of what most people forget is that if this were not so, there would be an excess of positive or negative charge, and this would create a massive force pushing all the excess charge out, leaving behind a core group of neutral charges. When sunlight moves electrons in us with the energy in light waves is alters the arrangement of our bio-molecules. Different molecular arrangements have different energies associated with them. This difference in energy is how bio-molecules are quantized to light frequencies and this organized them in a very fundamental way using the electromagnetic force. Life is electric. It is photoelectric and it must be tethered to the sun to work properly. It also explains how metabolic networks became coupled to sunlight frequencies and this explains why circadian biology trumps issues around food at every levels of human biologic science. It is time you know that too. Always eliminate poor things in your environment before considering your next move. ### Da-mo — 2018-02-04T11:08:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-high-level-overview-light-water-magnetism.19828/post-234096>

I have a set of scales that supposedly measures BMR I think - it gives me a number in KJ and Cal and infers an age from it. I don't know how accurate it is but it is at least consistent.

This is it [https://www.wildfiresports.com.au/t...5YZyihdtUAyB7-5j\_-ElmZnfeteJLqcRoC3\_EQAvD\_BwE](https://www.wildfiresports.com.au/tanita-bc-587-innerscan-body-composition-scale?gclid=CjwKCAiA-9rTBRBNEiwAt0ZnwzdMnKHms94UIQ6rTv7BOv5YZyihdtUAyB7-5j_-ElmZnfeteJLqcRoC3_EQAvD_BwE)

Interestingly, this morning after the first dayshift following a run of 5 nightshifts, my BMR was down significantly from around 7500 to 7200. Does that mean I need less calories at rest than when I was doing nightshifts?

> Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR) – Displays an estimate of the minimum level of calories that the body requires at rest

At the same time, my total body water went down a couple of percent and visceral fat went up by 1kg from 12 to 13.

From: [https://www.metaboliceffect.com/are-you-measuring-weight-loss-or-fat-loss/](https://www.metaboliceffect.com/are-you-measuring-weight-loss-or-fat-loss/)

"It is important to understand this is a hydration measuring device NOT a fat measuring device (meaning it does not measure fat directly but estimates it based on hydration) and because water fluctuates so greatly in the body (the reason you can gain or lose 5 pounds in a weekend) you have to know how to read them. They are NOT accurate, but when used consistently at the same time, under the same conditions weekly, they definitely show a trend of muscle loss or fat loss."

Also:
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bioelectrical\_impedance\_analysis#Accuracy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bioelectrical_impedance_analysis#Accuracy)

# Thread: Rimka Renovation Ready- The Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rimka-renovation-ready-the-journal.19833/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 58
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stephanie Rimka — 2017-07-19T04:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rimka-renovation-ready-the-journal.19833/post-219343>

Currently in Atlanta since 1997. Born and raised in Detroit. Been trying to go further South for awhile in my mind. But, it might be critical now?

44 years old. Mixed race 67% northern European, 27% South Asian (Pakistani), African rest

Haplogroup: L1c3b1
VDRtaq +/+
MTHFR 677 -/-
MTHFR 39 +/-
MTHFR 1298 +/-
COMT 158 +/-
COMT 62 +/-
COMT 199 -/-
CBS 699 -/-
CBS 360 +/-

my 11 yr old: L1c3b1 and G-L42

VDRtaq +/+
MTHFR 677 -/-
MTHFR 39 +/+
MTHFR 1298 +/+
COMT 158 +/-
COMT 62 +/-
COMT 199 -/-
CBS 699 +/-
CBS 360 +/-

Where to begin? I have come a long way after a 3 year rough haul. A perfect storm of relationship ending, buying a new house, moving, transition, emotional toll, heartbreak, financial pressure increase, and then unknown mold growth and HVAC system blowing fiberglass throughout the house and into the body of me and my son led to rapid and severe health declines in us both.

It took about a year for it to get so bad that I figured it out. Obviously, my main focus was on him, and I just fought through it until I basically broke. But even then I just lived on the fear and fight response to keep me going to help my son because he couldn't help himself. Then I turned the immediate corner on him. And I crashed hard.

The house has beed remediated three times. Ozoned. All the things. But I had a recent exposure in my home 2 months ago in a major plumbing debacle, and I had to remediate the kitchen and ozone the house. But I couldn't even be in it for 2 days. I think I am experiencing a serious relapse.

The past affected me with sinus congestion, exhaustion that I cannot even comprehend, depression, mental drain, up all night, asthma attacks, pain, crying, just augh. To get through it until I could remediate everything I slashed my work schedule drastically, stopped working at night, stopped working on weekends, stopped gratis cases, stopped giving my energy away, and resorted to Albuterol, Flonase, Claritin-D, and oxygen on a daily basis. Within 2 weeks of remediation I was off everything and drastically felt better, But the fatigue and body pain needed more help. And then the severe back pain came while at the beach of all places. But I was throwing the boy off a surfboard or something, and BAM. That was the end of that.

Chiropractic, decompression, flexion-extension, laser, CBD, acupuncture, kinesiology, bought a NIR sauna, bought two Vielights, bought a Bio-Mat, bought TWO PEMF devices (I know he hates them...not sure why just yet???) Earthpulse and SOTA, and the back and the energy were still not recovering. All my money is going into reflexology, therapies, etc to keep going.

MRI shows 3 not the 1-2 I knew about. L3/4 certainly the most symptomatic. New CBP doc, my curves are quite good, but she evaluated me with a 16 mm R leg discrepancy. 8mm lift in my shoe. It's like I walk with a book taped to my foot. I ALWAYS work barefoot. It's sort of my thing. I have been teaching grounding and that shoes are an abomination for 10+ years, but I am trying to listen and try new things. But, back is still jacked, I am getting weaker. Oh, yeah, I broke L5 in a college soccer game. That ended my sports life. Several other sports injuries of broken bones and dislocated joints throughout the body. (I was a bit aggressive and immune to pain. Not so much anymore. I'm too old for that shit. Meditation calmed that down!)

So I started Prolozone injections and MAH with UBI in the blood. 6 injections later, and I am hurting like when I started. The UBI does feel better, and I am getting another one tomorrow. Considering buying a rectal ozone machine. But I can't keep throwing money at this at this rate.

Best thing I have done by far hands DOWN, is wear the Bob Beck DC pulse device on my wrist. I pump in 27 V DC current into my blood stream for months now, and it is was the miracle that kept me from closing my practice. Kept me knowing I wasn't going to die and leave my kid, and literally filled me with energy throughout my day. I started slow but worked up to 8-12 hours per day with it on. I still use it, but am not dependent upon it to get going. SO obviously, it has me super intrigued about the Quantlet because it's the same concept of wearing a device on the wrist and affecting the system through the blood there.

My son suffered from severe psychiatric symptoms from all this. Congestion and anaphylactic shock to peanuts twice, allergic reaction the Benadryl given to him twice, and then just rage, pain, anemia, fatigue, hypotension. I had to pull him out of school for the end of 4th grade. Anger he couldn't control despite knowing it was irrational. He was sobbing and frightened and knew something was wrong. We took him to Mexico, Playa del Carmen in fact, for spring break and we started to wonder if he had a brain tumor. So I was at a point watching him HOPING it was an operable brain tumor rather than Wilson's disease or schizophrenia or something. It was frightening beyond.

Ultimately, severe oxidative stress, pyrrole disorder (whether or not you believe it exists), neurotransmitters wacked, I took him to Chicago for the best I could find in the Walsh/Pfeiffer work. He's been doing it for a year, but suddenly at 11, he has become severely oppositional, very OCD, and almost uncontrollable. He is becoming unrecognizable to me. And I am wondering how much is related to just being in this city, going to school, and not living a normal life. Wondering if I can heal myself or him here. Wondering if I can heal while the stress and fear of him heading towards addiction and prison is so very real for me.

My health before all this was relatively hit or miss, actually.
I'm 5'11 and about 145 right now, but I was 120 when I bought this house. I gained 7 pounds in 4 days of the new mold exposure..I only know that because they weigh me every week when I get Prolozone...I prefer to be thin at 125-130. But I have put in zero effort besides cycling keto/paleo. Exercise isn't happening. I could barely get out of bed last year. We were chronically late for school and my work start time went from 8:30 to currently 10.

I did have an ovarian cyst explode and that sent me to the ER one year before I moved into this house. That was a fun time. Had no idea I had PCOS, but it explained the occasional shitty periods and the insane cystic acne that I developed after stopping breastfeeding. I went the acupuncture/chinese medical doctor/herb route and diet route and that's when i started keto to deal with that. Started feeling great. Then shit hit the fan, we broke up, i moved us out at christmastime, and he struggled and I mothered, and cried. Then...mold.

Now: back pain, inability to do much besides work, lots of massage/reflexology, CBD, chiropractic, is basically just holding me together and patching me up, but not correcting a damn thing. My kid suddenly is worse. I'm not sending him away to some rehab school.
So I am considering starting one. And moving. And whatever and however I have to do this. I just don't really want to have to lose everything I have tried to build. Starting all over isn't the way I need to do it this time.

But there is no comprehensive healthy school that honors nature and neurological development in existence. Then you couple it for kids with behavioral disorders or other mental illness, and I think there should be one. In Mexico maybe. Unless I am completely fucking bat shit crazy.
That's where I am.
Ready to figure this out and terrified of ice touching my body. Yet, so be it.
But honestly feel too "weak" or not ready.

I miss feeling good.

I miss my son feeling good.

I want to be helping people from a beach.

I felt so much better when I drove a convertible when I got pregnant. Augh. Oh, and the pregnancy was the worse time of my life. Ever. So sick. High risk, hemorrhaged daily for 10 straight weeks and then off and on every couple weeks at a minimum. In and out of the hospital from fainting and falling. I was put on bed rest at 6 weeks...it never ended. So I didn't move much the entire time. Gained 70+ pounds. Why? Probably because I was vegan for 12 years before, and in menopause, so I finally let all the doctors convince me to eat meat, so I ate shit tons of salmon and got off gluten 12 years ago, was on estradiol and armour and got pregnant in 6 months...we weren't trying, They said I was infertile. My estrogen was 25 when I said, "ok, you win. I guess to live, something must die."

Post pregnancy I had weird lymph node enlargements in my neck...tested for all the things. Not cancer, but a ANA + got me sent to Rheumatologists who said I had nothing, but that caused BCBS to throw me off health insurance. So for 3 years, I had none and had to get the inflammation down. Re-tests show negative. Doctor's opinion: I was a false positive.

So, I am eating seafood now, could do more. Oysters, not sleeping on bio-mat, trying to reduce how much time I spend in heat, use the NIR lights at night only, and not even as a sauna, just the light, have incandescent throughout most of the house but definitely bedrooms and bathrooms. wear blue blocking glasses at work and at home. stopped sunglasses, go outside in the am but do miss occasionally, and there's the rain issue. I need to know what to do in the house specifically- which bulbs to buy to simulate it when i can't get out there. PLus I live surrounded by trees and It is getting hard to find sunlight. I keep moving the chair around. And I am single with the kid and don't have tons of time to be out there with him inside still sleeping. I'm dreading school and want to just homeschool him but can't swing that yet, so we can just be outside.

There's the journal entry. Oy.

Rebuild. Renew. Regrow. Reboot.

Oh i forgot!!!

My mom was basically never sick- strong like bull.
But she is 7o rme2 now, is deathly afraid of the sun making her "blacker"-- lots of internal racism. I can;t get her in the sun. I think her experience of colored water fountains and worse in TX as a child has her so jammed up, I can;t break through it. After menopause, she became VERY different in every way. She suddenly crave sweets and gained about 10 ponds a year...well, in 10 -12 years, she got fat. Then diabetic, then hypertensive. She is 72, diverticulosis with a couple huge flares and stays in the hospital, colitis, bloating, diabetes, sleep issues, big time anxiety. So very different than the mom who raised me. Grew up in India but she cannot handle the heat, kept getting boils all over her body, so they moved back to America when she was 9. She loves the cold. Weirdest Indian/Black mix on earth.

Her mother, my G-ma: died at 88, only 25% hair grey, congestive heart failure a few TIAs at the end, mind completely intact; she never had a driver's license or worked, husbands took care of her, never sick until the end, LOTS of mental illness..suicide attempt when her baby (my mom's sister died in the crib), probably bi-polar, probably Borderline PD, chronically suicidal. Funny as hell.

I've been to that area of Mexico about 6 times. It's one of my favorite places to travel. Chitzen Itza and cenotes are so powerful spiritually for me. My son likes it. He is still a hot mess even at the beach. I only vacation at the beach. Except last year. I let them talk me into NYC at Christmas. Shoot me.

I think I tanked so hard last year because I didn't do my normal 2-4 beach trips during the winter. I was so sick, flying was really hard on me. I sucked it for a family trip to take the kid to Broadway (he wants to be singer and actor), and my ex-husband was dying for me to see it at Christmas. They are already talking about going back, but i suggested a beach. NOW, I am going to insist upon it. I understand what it really did to me to go there PLUS to not get to southern sun at least twice. It was my hardest winter here physically and emotionally.

Why does anyone want to go to Paris when you can go to an island? I don't get it.

Atlanta has a growing homeless population, with mental illness, usually schizophrenia as the top, as the primary driver. We are very mixed here in how we live, not very segregated like Detroit. ### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-20T05:24:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rimka-renovation-ready-the-journal.19833/post-219464>

Why are contacts, sunglasses, and eyewear really bad for our eyes? They polarize incoming light from the sun. Sunlight is un-polarized naturally. All versions of man made light is polarized in some way. This means that man made light always has qualities in it that are non natural and manufactured. Are animal cells mitochondria and/or chloroplasts able to handle this situation? NO..............why? Miotchondria and chloroplast both uses excitons to work. Excitons always degrade in vibrations or oscillations in both system. What happens to an exciton when polarized light is used as the incoming signal from your environment? DISEASE AND ILLNESS. Why? The study of sensitized fluorescence is an elegant method to follow the fate of excitation energy in multipigment systems in plant and bacterial cell as well as in pigment-protein complexes isolated from them. The main conclusion from these study is that transfer of excitation energy (or excitons) occurs from the accessory pigments to the reaction center chlorophyll A or bacteriochlorophyll depending upon the system we are investigating. Although these transfers are rather fast (femtoseconds to picoseconds), some energy is also lost as fluorescence, or as heat, depending upon the value of the various rate constants of the various de-excitation pathways. This is why mitochondria liberate heat and why plant chloroplast need water to quench the photosynthetic process to absorb this heat.

Excitation energy transfer between identical pigment molecules, particularly between chlorophyll A molecules in "photosynthetic units" of chloroplasts cannot be determined by the "sensitized fluorescence" technique, i.e., observation of acceptor fluorescence when you excite the donor molecule. However, one observes quenching of fluorescence, i.e., reduced fluorescence when excitation energy transfer is a major event leading to photochemistry in this system. Also, if the molecules are arranged randomly, excitation with polarized light leads to DEPOLARIZATION of fluorescence in these photopigments if there is excitation energy migration. Mitochondria use cytochrome c oxidase as a red light photopigment and NAD+/NADH is a UV light photopigment. Chloroplasts use chlorophyll A and B as its photopigments. What does this post mean? This means energy in the system DECLINES........electron chain transport and the exciton process of photosynthesis SLOWS........what happens when electron chain transport slows? Read up on what happens to electron chain transport when it is exposed to CYNANIDE. So.........about those contacts, glasses, and sunglasses........you still think they are wise decision?

### Stephanie Rimka — 2017-07-26T05:30:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rimka-renovation-ready-the-journal.19833/post-220098>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=219464)
>
> Why are contacts, sunglasses, and eyewear really bad for our eyes? They polarize incoming light from the sun. Sunlight is un-polarized naturally. All versions of man made light is polarized in some way. This means that man made light always has qualities in it that are non natural and manufactured. Are animal cells mitochondria and/or chloroplasts able to handle this situation? NO..............why? Miotchondria and chloroplast both uses excitons to work. Excitons always degrade in vibrations or oscillations in both system. What happens to an exciton when polarized light is used as the incoming signal from your environment? DISEASE AND ILLNESS. Why? The study of sensitized fluorescence is an elegant method to follow the fate of excitation energy in multipigment systems in plant and bacterial cell as well as in pigment-protein complexes isolated from them. The main conclusion from these study is that transfer of excitation energy (or excitons) occurs from the accessory pigments to the reaction center chlorophyll A or bacteriochlorophyll depending upon the system we are investigating. Although these transfers are rather fast (femtoseconds to picoseconds), some energy is also lost as fluorescence, or as heat, depending upon the value of the various rate constants of the various de-excitation pathways. This is why mitochondria liberate heat and why plant chloroplast need water to quench the photosynthetic process to absorb this heat.
>
> Excitation energy transfer between identical pigment molecules, particularly between chlorophyll A molecules in "photosynthetic units" of chloroplasts cannot be determined by the "sensitized fluorescence" technique, i.e., observation of acceptor fluorescence when you excite the donor molecule. However, one observes quenching of fluorescence, i.e., reduced fluorescence when excitation energy transfer is a major event leading to photochemistry in this system. Also, if the molecules are arranged randomly, excitation with polarized light leads to DEPOLARIZATION of fluorescence in these photopigments if there is excitation energy migration. Mitochondria use cytochrome c oxidase as a red light photopigment and NAD+/NADH is a UV light photopigment. Chloroplasts use chlorophyll A and B as its photopigments. What does this post mean? This means energy in the system DECLINES........electron chain transport and the exciton process of photosynthesis SLOWS........what happens when electron chain transport slows? Read up on what happens to electron chain transport when it is exposed to CYNANIDE. So.........about those contacts, glasses, and sunglasses........you still think they are wise decision?

Doc, i am sold. i read your blog on it.
I just don;t know what to DO about it?
i am batshit blind without glasses or contacts.
Cant drive, read, see, work, anything without them.
I can't find an actual practical recommendation anywhere.

What is someone to do in real life to deal with it?
Does it even matter then if we wear sunglasses since we are screwed?
Are we just fucked no matter what? Is there nothing to do to help in any way?

how do I recover with my eyes like this?
Knowing i have to wear something to see. And sometimes glasses just aren't safe like when i have my kid at the pool or the beach-- i just cant see to safely watch him etc without them. The water is terrifying without contacts.
What's the best "solution?"
Do all the mitochondriac members just have perfect vision and this isn't a concern?
if there's a thread, i'll go read it.
(sad face.) obviously in the am i am without anything on my eyes outside. but when i have to go to take the boy to school and go to the office, i have to do something to see.

### Queen of Elves — 2017-08-01T18:25:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rimka-renovation-ready-the-journal.19833/post-220635>

> [Stephanie Rimka said:](/goto/post?id=220099)
>
> crap and crap.
> please don't tell me i should get that damn RK surgery.
> that just doesn't feel right.
> but is that an only hope?

No surgery please! It will worsen the situation by changing the surface of the eye what will affect the way incoming light hits the retina.
You should wear glasses instead of lenses, put them at the tip of your nose and look over the frame whenever possible to get natural light in your eyes.
I need glasses for driving and do the same, especially in the AM light when I am on my way to work (when stopping at red lights for example...).

### Sheddie — 2017-08-01T22:38:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rimka-renovation-ready-the-journal.19833/post-220649>

> [Stephanie Rimka said:](/goto/post?id=220099)
>
> crap and crap.
> please don't tell me i should get that damn RK surgery.
> that just doesn't feel right.
> but is that an only hope?

"Damn RK surgery" is right -- if RK is "radial kerototomy" consisting of knife cuts to the cornea!

Truly, the stupidest thing I ever did. This was some 5 years before LASIK was approved. Today, there are a number of LASIK-related techniques, not just a one size fits all. Informally, I'm told most LASIK surgeons know about 3 (of more than 20) techniques and seeing the popularity of the procedure, may find themselves too busy to take the time to learn more procedures...

RK should be, and almost is, left on a shelf in the eye sciences of the dark ages behind us. After RK for myopia & astigmatism, I never had the crisp vision of glasses again. It ended a 20 year side-career as a photographer. And, I still needed glasses, albeit not such thick lenses. But wait, the worst was yet to come: Twenty years later scars were \*growing\* on my corneas! Thirty years later, needing cataract extraction, the cornea scars made this procedure extremely risky for an average eye surgeon to get past the corneas and operate on the lenses behind. It took 5 years, becoming legally blind and worse from cataracts, for me to find someone I could trust to handle my case and maybe not blind me for all time. Even so, aware of the post-cataract risk (link?) to developing glaucoma and macular degeneration up ahead, I was unaware of a probable correlation between the 1-2-3 diseases of the eye starting with cataracts and the common implantation of UV-blocking replacement lenses... It's on the back burner, but I made a note to myself if I'm showing signs of glaucoma to think hard about non-UV-blocking IOL replacements.

Your Intuition is telling you about RK. There are three legs to the stool here -- the Light-Water-Magnetism legs. Sun light/UV light to my eyes is likely the shortest leg for me although I practice exposing my sclera which are supposed to take in some UV in the prime location. (Remember that from Vermont 2017 on YouTube, around 1:45:00 or so?) And, exposing as much of my skin as possible will have to help me where I have a 'short leg length' to my step stool, and still not fall over. You recover with light AND water AND magnetism. Hope is still in good supply.

GURL. I'm sorry for what you have been through, but you sound like you are nothing but a comeback.
I'm not planning on any eye surgery, but I was told a few years ago my cornea was too thin for LASIK and I'd need RK.

Definitely not doing it.
I finally found the answer to what I should do from a youtube podcast Jack did with Luke Story.

I wish it was a bit easier to find some "solutions."
But basically: go change my script lenses to bluetech. get two pair to switch during the day 50% blocking and then the full 400-500 nm blocking pair for later in the day.
Look at the sun without them n during the day when I can.
Wear my contacts significantly less...not willing to go zero quite yet as that would eliminate the beach safely for me...I do have to be able to rescue and save a child in water. Often many more than my own.

So, that's a start for the optically challenged.

Seriously, that's a tough spot for you to navigate. Good thing healing is nonlinear!

In my recent Beginning, I got tied up in a knot about the earliest day light blocked for me by trees, and a neighbor's fence. I thought I remembered, somewhere, Jack said something like... _**just look up at the sky**..._ Maybe I got it wrong but I did get past my quandary! And, I may have this wrong, too, but I thought it was suggested as little as **3 minutes** was enough at this first step outside; Hello Sphinx, What's up?.. Then, I go back in and try, currently, to make the dreaded breakfast a joyful task... And it's a couple hours before the second, longer, scantily-dressed 20+ minutes of communing with UV-A rays (Vitamin A, between 8 and 10 AMs). Finally, it's been a built up 'routine' to lie out for 30+ minutes (11-1) at least three times a week for UV-B and Vitamin D. It's a lot easier than weeks ago.

Wow, all that 'work' and it's shrunk down to just a few lines of description! Good thing, too, because Water & Magnetism are lurking close, coming on next...

_Please, if my Routine is ineffective or a waste of time, someone please give me a heads up!! And, I got a UV LED black light bulb, screwed it in a re-position-able lamp, and now... can't figure when I could (should?) turn it on and expose myself? I thought, maybe, it would serve as an occasional back-up to the 'Sphinx time.' (Hope the gist of this loosens some of your knots about Time, Stephanie.)_

### Stephanie Rimka — 2017-08-02T03:48:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rimka-renovation-ready-the-journal.19833/post-220674>

Bwaahaaahaha! Glasses hawt. Ok. I'm mostly over it. But at 44, i'm still not gracefully ready to be in my 40's. I like to be fast and furious. Haven't been in along time. Yes, health is top. How lean I was was just how I was. No effort. I honestly out ate and drank everyone around me. My boyfriends would win thousands betting on me outdrinking everyone at a party bc no one would think this Olive Oil rail at 112 pds could do it. but, i just rarely got drunk or fat or anything. So, I seriously just want to be athletic again. The numbers aren't that important. I know how I feel when I like how I feel. but, that's further down the road. Probably alpha. Definitely and undermethylating competitive perfectionist type. But 15 years of meditation and lots of neurofeedback have chilled me waaaaaaay out....like the idea of Crossfit is barfalicious. Just not into competing like all my older friends anymore. But, I do push myself to be better. Always. I don't "get" not trying. I'd rather just read on a beach than just about anything else!

The only time i can't get out if my kid is maybe panicking for being alone...if he wakes up and I am not in the house, it can be catastrophic. so it can be stressful to manage it. It's not laziness, it's that I am a single parent and my kid is not neurotypical. Helping him manage himself and get us ready was pretty challenging before, now adding going outside every morning adds time, breaks in routine, and just changes for a kid who can't tolerate change in routine. So, I just wish I had something if morning goes to shit and I need to help him instead of myself. And yes, I am working on getting him out, too. but, right now, i can't always manage a physical war with him.
i'm trying!!!
I'll make it happen.
Wish he and I were on a beach and could see the sunrise together every day instead though! love the pine trees, but they block my rays.

I know mold and yeast are not the same, but... a friend got a DUI after a breath test and hadn't imbibed any alcohol. As a former alcoholic and sober many years, she was rabid to understand how this could be. It turned out she had a problem with Candida (yeast) which gave off whatever it takes to set off a breathalizer to accuse her as under the influence of alcohol. So, it seems mold and Candida may be partners in yeast-mold-alcohol aldehyde traffic offences, here? And, perhaps, felt changes in how we handle them less well than we once did?

### Penny — 2017-08-04T21:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rimka-renovation-ready-the-journal.19833/post-220933>

> [Stephanie Rimka said:](/goto/post?id=219462)
>
> can you tell me more why?
> i really don't like glasses
> i wear them occasionally ,but i can't see as well at all
> they give me headaches from the weight on my face..and they are all heavy bc my lenses are so thick no matter how much I spend on them.
> if losing my peripheral vision and feeling relatively unsafe in the world...because 1- i can;t see in my peripheral vision, and 2- if anyone were to try anything and they knock off my glasses, Im screwed. I can't fight or run or anything
> Atlanta has a growing homeless population, with mental illness, usually schizophrenia as the top, as the primary driver. We are very mixed here in how we live, not very segregated like Detroit. So, the homeless shelters and kitchens are right next to the million plus homes, and they are walking everywhere around my office. You need to be very aware of your surroundings here, especially with a child in tow.
>
> I grew up fighting in the streets, a lot. I have never been attacked or assaulted as an adult mainly because I move with a certain very strong tone...I don't feel that same way in glasses. But if I have to learn an entirely new way to feel strong in them because it is REALLY that different between what the contacts and the glasses are blocking, I can spend more time in glasses during the day. But I need to understand the why if I am willing to walk around basically feeling more vulnerable as a woman than I already do now that I'm all temporarily broken.
> What's the bonus, that I can take them off intermittently in the day when I am outside?

Truly, ditch the glasses/contacts - outside, look at the sunrise and sunset - see this book for what happens to your eyes when they are covered up: JK talked about this opthalmologist 's finding when he removed contacts - hormones were increased in the urine: it's on google books - otherwise, Amazon has it for $76.00 -
[https://books.google.com/books?id=cMrgBwAAQBAJ&pg=PA27&source=gbs\_toc\_r&cad=3#v=onepage&q&f=false](https://books.google.com/books?id=cMrgBwAAQBAJ&pg=PA27&source=gbs_toc_r&cad=3#v=onepage&q&f=false)

Have you tried methylene blue? It kills many different types of pathogens and acts like a proxy for a carb at cytochrome I without the ROS spike : [https://mitoblue.com/](https://mitoblue.com/)

Your SNPs are interesting - do most people with an African (closely coupled haplotype) have an almost completely blocked vitamin d receptor? Except for that, the rest of your SNPs don't look too bad -

Your kid's though are a disaster:

VDRtaq +/+
MTHFR 677 -/-
MTHFR 39 +/+
MTHFR 1298 +/+
COMT 158 +/-
COMT 62 +/-
COMT 199 -/-
CBS 699 +/-
CBS 360 +/-

Here is the reductionist place I go to decipher SNPs: [https://knowyourgenetics.com/](https://knowyourgenetics.com/)
It's free but you do know that if your son is in the sunshine, these genes won't express...

To calm him down (and use up a few methyl groups at the same time), I would give him glycine - it tastes just like sugar - lemon balm is also great (it's GABA-ergic) and nettle and querciten will help with the histamine response - It seems to me that your entire being is designed to work in a very sunny place - you can get a UV light at Batteries and Bulbs - you want 270nm - you can also go to rubilux lights: [https://www.rubyluxlights.com/](https://www.rubyluxlights.com/)

I have great luck with going into zen-mode with an IR light on the back of my head: I bought something like this:
[https://www.amazon.com/Phenas-48-le...780&sr=8-5&keywords=infrared+light+for+camera](https://www.amazon.com/Phenas-48-led-Infrared-Vision-Illuminator/dp/B00GFDAJEI/ref=sr_1_5?ie=UTF8&qid=1501870780&sr=8-5&keywords=infrared+light+for+camera)
You can also stick it on your forehead for 15 minutes the night before - Michael Hamblin has some good write ups on this -

There's always a Sperti light for vitamin d - and if I were you, I would be eating liver for the vitamin a... which goes to crap when you look at a screen - haven't been down that rabbit hole for awhile

Maybe Pensacola Florida?

### Sheddie — 2017-08-04T22:16:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rimka-renovation-ready-the-journal.19833/post-220939>

> [Stephanie Rimka said:](/goto/post?id=220923)
>
> PS i love gnomes!

WARNING -- Gnome Trivia:
My Swedish Nana (maternal grandmother) used to sing the phrase, "lilla tomtenisse" (trans. gnome) in a rhyming nursery song at bedtime. The next line,"Lilla snipa snu" is a phonetic spelling that, if correct, means 'a snap turn.' Doesn't quite work for me as "little gnomes, making a 'snap turn.'" Maybe a reference to gnomes popping in & out of visibility??( Alas, wish I knew the translation of the whole of the poem/lyrics...) And, in my childhood, Gnome artifacts RULED the Christmas decorations -- everywhere you looked. So, I'm especially glad the new century didn't see the Gnome pass into further distant dimensions, like Faerie, Dragons... The cartoons of today are so ugly, awful, fake...

# Thread: Outdoor office in the sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 38
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2017-07-19T18:47:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/post-219406>

A few months ago I purchased daylight viewable monitors so I can work outside in the sun. [See here for initial testing pics](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-did-you-do-today-to-be-more-optimal.17876/page-16#post-212126)

After picking up some long cables, I've been able to connect to my desktop computer in my house and setup a dual monitor office space outside where I can productively work naked in the sun. On days when the weather's nice, I get everything setup early in the morning, get as much direct morning sun as possible, and usually move into the shade or go inside mid-afternoon.

Setup Pic:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Outdoor equipment list:

-   Two 19" 1000 nit monitors
-   Cables: 50ft VGA, hdmi, and USB
-   Wired mouse, keyboard, speakers
-   AC Power cable

EMF's are minimized since everything is wired and the computer itself is far away from where I sit. Measuring with a Trimeter & Cornet:

Electric field: nothing significant other than at the power cord
Magnetic field: strong field at the monitors, at the power cord, and at the speakers
RF: nothing from computer

The setup is flexible enough that I can change position so that different parts of my skin are facing the sun as it moves throughout the day. I originally tried sitting on a chair at a table, but I found it was too difficult to get even sun exposure on my body and be comfortable. Sitting on the ground, sometimes with a bit of a back support leaves me feeling great even after a few hours of work.

### JohnPxl — 2017-08-11T16:56:48+0300

>
> After picking up some long cables, I've been able to connect to my desktop computer in my house and setup a dual monitor office space outside where I can productively work naked in the sun. On days when the weather's nice, I get everything setup early in the morning, get as much direct morning sun as possible, and usually move into the shade or go inside mid-afternoon.

I am a web designer, and today I tried something similar. Worked for 4 hours straight outside, drank 2 bottles of water, with feet on the ground. It was an unprecedented experience of flow, focus and productivity.
I have now moved inside as the heat is un-bearable, but still feel every cell in my body energised, and I do not feel exhausted as I usually do this time of the day. I will try to make this my routine every morning. Thanks for the pics, great setup

Best

John

### Rubicon — 2017-08-20T23:05:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/post-221977>

Joe, I forget the name of the monitor manufacturer. It's an OEM supplier from China, not a main consumer brand.
I purchased them as surplus from the local classifieds from an guy who specializes in outdoor monitors. Originally they were intended for use in a fast-food restaurant to be custom built into their kiosks...they're really basic in that they have no frame/stand. I think I got really lucky picking up three at $100 each. A bit of quantum luck/destiny there?

The key spec to look for is a monitor with around 1000+ 'nits'. Anti-reflective/anti-glare coating and matte finish instead of glossy can help too.

Panasonic has a line of laptops called toughbooks that feature daylight viewable screens: [http://business.panasonic.com/toughbook/fully-rugged-laptop-toughbook-31.html](http://business.panasonic.com/toughbook/fully-rugged-laptop-toughbook-31.html)
Tru-vu appears to sell daylight viewable monitors: [http://www.tru-vumonitors.com/products/sunlightreadablelcd.html](http://www.tru-vumonitors.com/products/sunlightreadablelcd.html) I never did get far enough along to enquire about prices on those.

@[JoeT](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19594/)
Inspired by this thread I picked up a refurbished toughbook CF-19 MK1 for £99. It's screen as okay outdoors but I have to put a hood around it to see well. Apparently MK1 screens are pretty poor so anything above that should work absolutely fine.

### drezy — 2017-12-02T16:21:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/post-229344>

> [mbhemming said:](/goto/post?id=229325)
>
> Very cool!
> Ever think about making a stand up desk?

I made one and spend half the time on it. The fact that Rubicon is sitting on earth is pretty instructive once I thought about it. Also the you hang around here log enough you realize that you're getting handed, better than flat answers, a thinking toolset. Here's what I learned with the "thinking toolset" used in quotes

> Why ask why

Why would Rubicon be sitting on the ground?

> Two people living in the same house aren't in the same \[quantum\] environment

Could sitting really make a difference? Armed with my meter, because I love seeing for myself. So I get a reading in my own backyard at shoulder level and then on the ground. The difference x24 quieter at the surface. Was it massive at shoulder level? No, but that leads to

> Life at the lowest level is non-linear

I have a relay that controls my cold tub chiller. The chiller runs use 120VAC. The silicon relay is turned on by 3VDC. So 2 AA batteries could switch on 120VAC and turn on my chiller. That's non-linear but nowhere near as impressive as the scales of non-linear magnification of life.

The last thing I note is that the colon and prostate are in the quieter "zip code" about 3' down and on an electron donor at the same time. I plan on keeping my prostate and colon in working order so now my butt is on the ground too. You're younger than me so you'll need a longer warranty on your parts.

That' just a a snapshot of the toolset I picked up here.

### Rubicon — 2017-12-04T00:20:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/post-229439>

Last summer I found myself moving my work area around a lot. Sometimes because one area of my skin got too hot or other times to be able to work in the shade. Also, throughout the season the place in my backyard that I wanted to be to avoid shadows changed as the sun's angle changed from month to month. At 55 degrees latitude, the location of the sun moves substantially. Dragging Rubbermaid bins around throughout the day and moving them throughout the season is not terrible, but not ideal.

For those reasons, I'm thinking something along the lines of an adjustable standing desk that would elevate the platform from sitting level to standing and also rotate the entire platform 360 degrees. That way I could change from sitting to standing anytime and often change the part of my body that's directly facing the sun. Ideally the whole thing could easily be moved around the backyard as well.

### Mommy3times — 2017-12-08T05:47:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/post-229767>

I work outside everyday. In the winter, I move east to west with Black plastic shelving from home depot it glides along the grass if you don't add the feet. Keeping my PC in the shade and my body in the sun on the North side of the house is easier with this set-up. Keeps me from cursing the shade from the trees or my husband (who thinks they are saving me from dieing from skin cancer) . I have 3 levels on the shelf to sit or stand. A Rubbermaid toat fits on the shelf and I carry all the stuff in that everyday as my "commute to work" setup is under 5 minutes. I have 160 ft of CAT 7 internet running from the routers along the walls and out the window. Extention cord with surge protector. My monitor raises or lowers by 3 inches and I have flux (Sometimes I throw on my sunhat to see the tabs at the top). Every plug goes into a docking station. Often the PC fan starts overheating so the extra shade on the 2nd shelf below my core monitor buys me extra time.

AND I'm thinking if its worth it to to setup a CT Tub outside as my husband is not supportive, so I get 1 hour inside before the sun rises.

I should have taken picture of all the "desks" I made over the past year.. enjoyed my bookshelf when the sun is high in the summer as the shade starts growing in the heat when I need it most. But that's not do-able with low winter sun and 10 feet of shade from the house.

If only I could stop the neighbors dog from barking, my set-up would be perfect.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-08T14:58:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/post-229775>

Out door office in the sun:

Gardening always has a point, and this is how life must be. As a neurosurgeon I have seen many people die at all stages of their life. Most were unafraid except the ones who felt they had not done anything worth while in their life. Those people always will claw and scratch for 2-3 more years to get it right. A garden, in the sunlight, when your hands and feet are connected to the soil is one of the most amazing hospitals I know that can give those people what they want.........TIME. It is such a pleasure to help a person understanding that sinking one's hands into the warm earth under the sun, and to feel at one's fingertips the possibilities of the new season and the possibility of new life. For a mitochondriac, there are no mistakes in gardening, just experiments for life.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-07-27T15:51:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excess-estrogen.19841/post-275057>

High estrogen really means one of two things: Not enough sun to lower it on the skin or your cells are making too much in the conversion because they have not gotten the same light stimulus below. People forgot what the blogs clearly said. **The Reality #7 BLOG laid it out clearly. Why don't you re-read it some time?

**

It said, "Sunlight increases sulfated Vitamin D3, histamine, and sulfhydryl groups while lowering (photolysis) adrenaline, steroids, testosterone, estrogen, thyroid hormone, DNA, and RNA. Sunlight is capable of inducing biochemical reactions via photolysis and it induces coordinated endocrine adaptation effects in the eye and the skin surfaces. It affects the sympathetic and parasympathetic systems in the brain by way of the central retinal pathways. The varying AM solar light is the key stimulus for the circadian timing mechanism of the body clock via the central retinal pathways. All these effects are built into the electronics of your proteins under solar power and magnetic flux."

People forget that the eye and skin/fat BOTH have melanopsin. That is what allows the deeper cells to make estrogen when the skin is not getting enough UV light to turn off the FLOW of estrogen in pathways lacking information quanta.

**It also said clearly:**
He who controls the sun properly can bring blood flow to the choroid surface (RPE) to energize red blood cells and the hemoglobin present in them. This is how phototropism works in the eye. The choroid/RPE/SKIN and FAT mass have many correlates to the phototropism in plant chloroplasts in leaves. The choroid of the eye is primarily a vascular structure supplying the outer retina. The skin has the same construction with most of the arteries just below the surface waiting for the UVA light stimulus to raise the blood vessels to the surface to be irradiated by the sun. Vermont 2017 showed this slide several times and you'll likely see it again this year. The outer portion of the retina is where the photoreceptor melanopsin is loosely covalently bound to retinol. All diurnal mammals have this arrangement. Nocturnal mammals have a very strong covalent bond in their systems to deal with different light stimuli to control their scotopic retina and SKIN/FAT.

Melanopsin/retinol controls the neural signaling along the central retinohypothalamic tract and the skin/fat/brain axis in humans. These tracts connect the retina to the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) which controls the ENTIRE circadian mechanism in the body including the one in the gut via the vagus nerve. It does this because it lowers the firing of the PVN by reducing adrenaline and norepinephrine as mentioned above. People forget these very basic connections are present in each and every one of us. Sunlight is the controlling arm of the gut by way of solar exposure of the eye and skin!!!!! It also controls the center that controls how sex steroid hormones all begin and interact with the pituitary. Why?
These tracts continue on and make connections between the retina and SCN to the leptin receptor in the hypothalamus directly. The leptin system, in turn, controls all growth and metabolism of the organism, including all the SEX steroid hormones, because this system controls the circadian release of hormones from the pituitary gland using specific light frequency changes present in AM sunlight. It is the AM blue light (1600K) balanced by the low powered red light that has MORE than 42% of AM light because of the ABSENCE of UVA and UVA on a RELATIVE basis.......
THE TAKE HOME? He who controls the frequency of sunlight via the eye, skin and subcutaneous fat calls the shots for the anterior pituitary hormones and its effect on human behavior and illness. Sunlight also controls the phototropism in leaves in plants as well as ANIMALS like HUMANS. The universe doesn’t speak with words. It speaks in frequencies and our proteins decipher this Rosetta code to innovate life and it explains why your estrogen is higher. It is time to find your problem in these words because they are buried here. Taking something to lower estrogen is not the answer. Getting more sun in one of these places is the answer. GOT IT?

I know sooo many women whose health drastically improved upon explant. The longer they are in the more likely they will rupture. Saline ones can have mold inside and Ive seen women who unknowingly had mold in their saline implants breast feed their baby and they baby got very sick.

You also say that you are surrounded by cell towers, radar towers, and antennas. All these things are not good. I would move and explant ASAP if I were you. If and when you decide to explant make sure the PS you are choosing is experienced with performing enbloc or at least total capsulectomy. If the entire capsule that has built around implant is not removed your health will not improve. Most PS don't do this....they remove or replace without removing the capsule.

# Thread: CT and BUN/Creatinine Ratio Improvements
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bun-creatinine-ratio-improvements.19842/>

### Dennis Durost — 2017-07-19T19:58:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bun-creatinine-ratio-improvements.19842/post-219412>

I am a 76 year old male who started following Dr Kruse in May 2017. I have done daily AM sun exposure, grounding and artificial blue light exposure reduction starting in mid May. At my last physical in early April 2017, labs showed a BUN/Creatinine ratio of 17.6:1. Just recently labs related to two surgeries showed the ratio to be at 12.5:1. Would assume the mitochondria have benefited. My CT experience took place in May 2017 for three weeks and consisted of ice applied to my love handles for one hour per day. I avoided contact with my abdomen due to an umbilical hernia. Saw no reduction in waist circumference and quit the process. Approximately 1 1/2 months later I noticed a marked reduction in girth of the love handles and a 2" reduction in waist size. Also lost a few lbs.

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T13:48:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk.19845/post-219599>

Hello and thank you to all for you kind messages and welcoming me into the community. It has come at just the right time in my life where I think I need some help, and intuition has led me here... so I know I'm on the right path now, thank you.

Caroline - I shall start a journal, this is good advice, as I think it will be very beneficial.

A bit of background on myself as requested, you might even be able to offer me some help right off the bat, as there is so much dense information available it's a little overwhelming to know where and how to start.

I'm not currently diagnosed with any specific medical illness or condition, however, I feel uneasy and dissatisfied with my physical and mental health. I'm also at odds emotionally and spiritually with my lifestyle.

I have 3 kids, recently separated from a long term marriage and am in a new relationship which is very supportive, and have inherited a step son, so 4 children - which is great as I love kids and a stable family home life.

I live and work from Manchester in the UK, which is typically quite a built up, densely populated city well known for being quite cold, grey and wet. I have my own business which is a digital agency, so I find the majority of my time is spent online with the computer or using the phone. Although I tend to avoid phone calls off the mobile device, I am inundated with SMS, emails and social media demands. My phone is often in my trouser pocket... Although I enjoy the creative aspects of what I do, overall, I have a lot of anxiety and stress that seems to overwhelm me mentally, and often it feels like I'm trapped on the typical hamster wheel, which I do try to juggle with an active family life with defined boundaries.

My parents are relatively healthy physically, with no known medical issues, though I think I've inherited their self employed anxiety and stress related tendencies, being brought up in a home where everything was lived for future attainment and goal achievement and not being present or enjoying the NOW, or gratitude for what we already have. My dad's parents died of heart attacks, and on my mums side, my grandad died of a heart attack but suffered much ill health from a genetic disorder which included muscle atrophy and diabetes. My mum's mum, my grandma, seems to be in good health at 87 and is only slightly over weight. My aunt passed away young from the same genetic disorder as my grandad, was very poorly and had diabetes. My uncle, my mum's brother, has the same genetic disorder and has diabetes and cancer and is obese. I've been tested for the genetic disorder and seem to be clear.

Overall, I'm slim although have battled and struggled with my diet and weight in the past historically. Being teased as a kid for being overweight led me to the gym, and weight training, which had positive and negative mental/body/confidence related associations. However, this path did lead me to become more educated about food and has been very insightful on my journey. I stumbled across Dr Mercola, Tim Ferriss, Dave Asprey and Mark Sissons over the years, and my intuition has strongly pulled me towards a low carbohydrate diet (ever since effortlessly losing weight on the Atkins diet many, many years ago). Something didn't sit well with me on the Atkins diet eating bacon and sausages however, and I slowly gravitated more towards Tim Ferriss's slow carb or Dave Asprey's Bullet Proof suggestions. Something still didn't sit well with me at a gut feel level.

After my marriage broke down, I found I used food as an environmental, emotional and mental control factor, and became a big fan of intermittent fasting and fasting. So I was eaiting only one or two meals a day, and wouldn't eat anything that wasn't strictly controllable. This caused conflicts with my family life and being surrounded by 4 kids, who have different parents on the other side, and being brought up with what you would probably term the SAD in general.

Over the past 6 months I've gravitated more to a no grains, or limited grains diet, and I eat a lot of smoked salmon, grass fed meats, grass fed butter, coconut oil, MCT oil and seafood, all of which sits well with my on an intuitive stand point. I do still occasionally consume dairy (pasteurised cheese & cream, not milk) which I know I should either eliminate or switch to raw versions. For convenience, I eat a lot of nut butters, usually trying to stick to things like almond or walnut, but I'm a sucker for the blends and the legumes like peanut and cashew nut. I know I need to eliminate these. I do take around 40gml of Carlsons fish oil a day, supplement with things like Vitamin D, NAC, Magnesium, Iodine and others.

HOWEVER, I started to note things about myself and my life that I would class as _red flag_ signals:

**1)** Whenever I exercised with resistance training I feel completely exhausted and drained for about 72 hours, and ache so much, I started to question the benefits and have eventually ceased all forms of physical exercise. I've been resistance training and bodybuilding since I was 17, and had a stint of steroid use in my early 20s, so I'm no stranger to motivation or the mental drive to exercise. I'm not lazy. But I started to lose faith in the benefits from the negative physical impact. To me this was counter intuitive as I figured we should be able to do physically demanding exercise as humans without feeling like we've been steam rollered for 72 hours and unable to lift our limbs without wincing.

**2)** On top of heavy caffeine abuse (10 - 12 strong cups a day), I found myself daily using Modafinil, or Ephedrine (or equivalent CNS stimulants), even down to seeking out and buying numerous legal research chemicals such as Isopropylphenidate, 4F-MPH & 3-FPM. For years I have been convinced I have some form of mild ADD or ADHD or bi-polar after reading so many other peoples descriptions. I felt these drugs helped, but over the past 6 months, I realised, I was addicted to how they made me feel, and was seeking a 'hit' from them constantly.

**3)** About a year ago, after seeking out more chemical 'hits' I came across Kratom. For fear of the downsides of chemical side effects, I found the idea of a natural 'herbal' solution very appealing. I realised I was just seeking something to change my chemistry and distract me from day to day feelings in life. I quickly have gone from using 3 - 5g of Kratom a day, easily to 20 - 25g a day throughout every day without fail.

**4)** 12 months ago, after never being a drug user for 35 years of my life until these recent times I even picked up some cannabis and LSD after reading about micro dosing benefits. Very quickly I settled into a routine of happily vaping weed and swapping between regular LSD micro doses to full, high doses.

**5)** My new relationship after separating from my ex wife is with a very understanding, supportive woman who I love. She was a none smoker when we met, and she's battled smoking cigarettes for her entire life. Due to some personal family related stresses, we both smoked some cannabis, and then it was like a trigger for her to start smoking again. The bizarre thing that threw up a red flag for me, is that being a lifelong none smoker, and only ever vaping weed for a year, I decided to try smoking. I liked the way it made me feel, and quickly progressed in about a month to a 4 - 6 cigarettes a day routine. I don't know if I'm addicted, as I haven't stopped but I have noted I craze the buzz and resulting relaxation.

**6)** When I'm not working late at night, I try to avoid checking my phone or computer, however, I still go to bed exhausted, sometimes unable to keep my eyes open, and in the morning I can't wake up. It's a mission and chore to get out of bed with any energy, and it takes me an hour to an hour and a half to mentally lift the fog from my mind - usually helped by kratom, cigarette and caffeine. I know with every fibre of my being, that this is not life and certainly not what life can be for someone like me of my age.

Overall I'm aware of what I am doing and am intelligent enough to know that factors in my mind and life circumstances are throwing me off balance and I'm seeking/craving externally what is not being produced internally as it should be. As I'm sat at the computer staring at the dark grey clouds and rain outside, I feel a deep imbalance and dissatisfaction with where my life is circumstantially and feel uncomfortable that I cannot be at ease with how I am living. So I know I need a plan to optimise my health, more for my mental and emotional well-being.

Now, my parents live at the coast, beach side, in Southern England and get a lot of sun exposure, bare foot walking, fresh air and so on. My incongruence in my life which hits me hard is that I yearn for a life based on principles which I'm educated to know are healthy for me and my children, however I feel trapped in my high stress 'digital' work life, and with my separation from my children's mum, feel I cannot relocate for fear of losing the healthy contact I have with my children.

Every day I face this dissatisfaction and dilemma. Every day I do a little more research and try not to be overwhelmed, to make positive changes somehow, some way to a better life. An optimised life.

When I stumbled upon an article citing Jack Kruse about magnetism, sunlight and other elements, it was like somebody had finally shown me the box with the picture on for the jigsaw puzzle. Like I always had all the scattered pieces scattered chaotically around, but no idea on the bigger picture. I know feel I'm scratching the surface to see the bigger picture and actually see the map & plan on how, maybe, to finally link all of the pieces together in my own life. There is just so much to learn, and there is a fear of doing the wrong thing, when you think you're doing the right thing, that I'm hoping the interaction on this forum will be insightful and helpful.

Thank you for listening and God bless you all for being here supporting each other.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-21T15:07:27+0300

Hi Andy
Living in a city in a country with less than optimal weather  is a tough one, but its all about baby steps. Assessing where you are now will probably help. Breast milk is just another form of sunlight slowed down by Mom to feed the baby. It is loaded with DHA and her excited electrons called excitons that do what? Slow sunlight down to make things with mass.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-21T00:23:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-in-breast-milk.19852/post-219543>

Discovery of how cells communicate to survive from the Nicolas Bazan Lab at LSU Health, New Orleans where I went to medical school...............
Big surprise to many but not to my mitochondriacs!!! , It appears that communication in the eye is mediated by novel lipid mediators of 32 and 34 carbons in length, the elovanoids. The following are links for the paper just published this week: [http://rdcu.be/t95r](http://rdcu.be/t95r) or [https://lnkd.in/ek5DF2b](https://lnkd.in/ek5DF2b) Known lipid mediators are derived from 18-22 carbon-length precursors, including prostaglandins, leukotrienes, lipoxyns, endocanabinoids, resolvins and docosanoids. Elovanoids are set apart from all of those because they are derived from 32 or 34 carbon precursors with different physicochemical properties than any known messenger and alternatively regulated biosynthetic pathways. This discovery opens a path forward to expand the science of neuroprotection and reveals novel mechanisms and potential therapeutic targets for retinal degeneration diseases and other neurodegenerative diseases tied to altered light spectrums we allow as humans. The major conceptual advances provided by this finding are of broad relevance for the biology of survival of neural as well as any other cells in the body by turning on neuroprotection using a set of molecules that mimic how the body naturally activates this protection.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-21T15:04:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-in-breast-milk.19852/post-219603>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=219526)
>
> I wonder what your thoughts are ...
> During pregnancy do you think a mother can transmit frequencies to the fetus that she has absorbed from the sun Through the placenta?
> Lots of women say they stopped nursing because they didn't have any more milk produced
> I think it's connected to the lack of sun light\[/
>
> I can say from personal experience that being stressed was the #1 thing that slowed my milk production. It wasn't tied to diet or hydration. That being said I somehow was blessed with an ample supply of milk even though I was living in a chronic state of depression and avoiding the sun as much as possible (being trained that because I am fair skinned that I would just burn, and I did when I wore sunblock. Also, my eyes hurt in bright sun and I was told that was because I had a "blonde retina" and there was no changing that). Coming from that Type A, stressed new mom place, I was doing everything I could to establish and increase supply. This included frequent feeding and pumping especially in the first weeks, visualizations, supplements including DHA and more. So my stress didn't affect establishment, but it did affect supply.
>
> Your point is an interesting one to ponder, and I'm not yet versed enough to approach it from the quantum bio POV but I can say that milk establishment and breastfeeding support is a serious area of failure in our society. It can become so difficult for a mother when the milk doesn't magically appear, health providers that don't know how lactation really works are giving different opinions, and so many people are so quick to say 'It's okay not everyone can produce milk" that I believe many women are discouraged from even trying, no matter how much they want to. When a health provider tells you "it's okay, your body just can't do that," a lot of people will believe it.
>
> Here are some words from Dr. Jack that I came across, to speak to the science. I thought I read somewhere around this website (or was it a podcast) that babies get their leptin from momma's milk. I was very intrigued by that, and wanted to read up. How do formula fed babies get their leptin?
>
> [https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/919812401416448](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/919812401416448)
>
> We just had a new baby born into our family yesterday to nurse in our family. Question came up in her room and on the forum that was similar. So here is my PSA Q & A for you all today to understand how leptin light and ubiqutin marry. "Long ago I said in leptin FAQ's babies get programmed via leptin/fat in breast milk...does/could high fat meal with no Gall Bladder no bile supplied etc..... possibly amt to surge of leptin further exacerbating leptin insensitivity...inhibiting or slowing Leptin Rx? Answer :. The leptin protein from mom has to act like a USB drive for the babies hypothalamus where is leptin receptor is awaiting instructions. The egg gave the child its mitochondrial clone genome. That genome has to work with the nuclear DNA in the CURRENT ENVIRONMENT the child resides. So breastfeeding gives the child's mitochondria that information from his mother's leptin protein in her breastmilk. . It needs the info of the precise environment it is in at birth. It gets this from momma's boob's assuming they are loaded with DHA. Women who have dry boobs usually are LR due to a serious lack of DHA or an excessive amount of protons. The worse situation is if both are present. If momma is LR, the baby will adjust the nuclear mitochondrial distance for optimization by altering its Tensegrity system to get the right distance between mitochondrial DNA and nuclear DNA to optimized the match. This goes on for 6 years. After that it appears it gets hardwired into the childs cells. Remember, the egg that the baby became is selected by leptin of mom. Leptin in women control fecundity. Have you ever wondered why women are born with over 1 million eggs but they can only use 400 in their life? In each egg is a mitochondrial clone from your ancestors pasts lives (immortaility in the stem cell line) that codes for a specific environment. When you ovulate a sperm climbs into your fallopian tube there is a complex interaction between its haploid number and the light it releases. That interaction of light and the atomic lattice selects the best egg for the current environment that mom senses.......If your environment is totally screwed.........you select no egg because the risk of mitonuclear de coupling is the worst out come or mismatch of energy utilization in biology. So you will be infertile. Now you see why fertility docs are printing money today........we live in a 24/7 blue lit microwaved world............and you have no eggs that match that situation. The egg has a plan......it wants to live in a child potential. If it senses that potential energy is not in the environment that egg will shrivel. Life preserves energy and light and will not manifest in a shit environment. The egg says: "Do not die while you’re still alive." An elevated ubiquitin rates tells the egg the information and won't allow that to happen. Oysters are a kiss from the sea that prevents the kiss of death or illness visiting a child too early. Modern humans have even figured out how to get around this........and this is why we see things like ADHD, autism, cystic fibrosis, and progeria today.

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T18:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-the-optimal-optimized-hman-be-outside-24-7-naked.19855/post-219644>

With no recent (6 months) physical exercise (although a history of bodybuilding) - following a general paleolithic diet, and supplementing with DHEA and a few other recommended supplements, I've noticed a rapid decline in bodyfat AND an increase in symmetrical musculature, with a better definition.

Previously following a low carb diet, or other dietary hacks for cutting bodyfat just for visual benefit, there was always an 'unhealthy' look to the body despite being lean, along with, for myself, a slight puffy watery look around the lower chest/nipple area which I always put down to a hormonal imbalance - perhaps estrogen related. What's interesting is the physical changes to the body seem to be very apparent and very positive visually (as well as the health benefits) and maybe answers the question I always was curious about like how all these ancient Greeks and Romans had classic V-shape bodybuilder style physiques when they clearly didn't hit the gym to do heavy resistance training 5 - 6 times a week. So now I'm wondering if the general hormonal output of an optimised human would result in a naturally defined, muscular, symmetrical looking physique even without resistance training. Not that I'm against physical exercise, I'm just struggling with it at the moment in the way it makes me feel after training...
