New article out on how to build your solar callus naturally. Your solar callus not not only offers protection but it is an ideal way to absorb sunlight properly to get light into your system to build wellness and improve your immunity. Our skin tones are tied to our solar callus and are perceived as most healthy and attractive to humans. We know know that red and yellow skin tones make you appear more attractive and send wireless signals to others that your immune system is optimized. Being pale is not a sign of wellness contrary to popular dermatologic speeches. Even animals besides humans use their solar callus to draw mates.

Folks who spend time in the sun can change their surface color by assimilating certain frequencies of sunlight to take red tones tones because IR-A light increases our RBC's production when our skin is hit with sunlight it undergoes dermal pooling to make our skin flush with blood. When the sun has powerful UV light more oxygen is found in our blood changing its color to a deep red due to the increased amount of oxygen in it. From a biological/anthropological perspective, this signals health and vitality and a high redox potential to members of our species who see us in the nature. I dont have access to the article but have heard some from the podcasts along the lines of preloading with morning light before the UV shows up..

My better half is fair skinned and always used to burn badly UNTIL...... she started doing CT with me . . . . . now she tans instead of burning. On sunny days we would CT in the swimming pool mostly with water up to the bottom of our ribcages and our heads, shoulders and arms in the sun for Founier effect.

Interestingly it seemed as though CT on its own was enough to tan without even getting sun - supposedly it increases melanin among other things. . . . when combined with a seafood diet . . .

> But Inuits vitamin D intake wasn’t dependent upon the sun. They get all that they need from their diet, heavy on types of fatty fish that are naturally rich in vitamin D. **The plentiful amounts of the vitamin kept them from developing less melanin.** In fact, before milk was fortified with D, people living outside of Northern Canada and Alaska loaded their diets with fishy products, such as cod liver oil, to get their daily supplement. **So despite their chilly climate and lack of sun exposure, it’s the Inuit diet that has kept them in their natural glow**.

When the sun has powerful UV light more oxygen is found in our blood changing its color to a deep red due to the increased amount of oxygen in it. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/13077291](https://www.patreon.com/posts/13077291)

I recently had a pow-wow with my son's oncologist. He is in remission now, and we are questioning whether his protocol is necessary for the duration, and without dosage change. He had a 12x12x14 cm tumor in his chest that was absorbing the chemo that is now so very thankfully gone. Side effects are sky-rocketing.

I'm sharing because the first thing the dr said in our meeting was to compare his cancer to melanoma, because of the well established confusion about how some people can be in the sun all day and never get cancer, some people will burn instantly and develop melanoma. He was being supportive, actually, of our right to choose the course of our son's treatment because he can't know for sure at this point if the chemo potential benefit is worth the risk, and this was his way of describing that.

These onco docs and nurses mentioned frequently that the chemo makes him extra sensitive to sun, and therefore more susceptible to sun burn so "make sure he's covered!" "wear a hat!" "never too much sunscreen!" They told me there was absolutely no way to increase his RBC during treatment, and once it started falling it wouldn't stop, and they had a magic hemoglobin count # for when they would do a transfusion.

They also told me there is no evidence that the smart meter or tablet would have caused or contributed to my son's rare cancer. Genetic testing was negative, even though 70% of people with this rare cancer (pleuropulmonary blastoma) have a mutation.

Now that he is in remission and we are taking a break from treatment, I'll be putting together some educational information for his docs, and I'll be all over this site for the links and logic!! Thank you so much. Of all the docs out there, pediatric docs NEED to have EMF danger info on their radar. (I cannot avoid that pun.)

### Lahelada — 2017-08-15T14:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-callus-rx-naturally.19859/post-221694>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=221685)
>
> Do you think Epsom salts helped you tan? That's interesting!

A tan is good and bad but not for the conventional reasons. It is "bad" to get it too quickly as it reduces your ability to make Vitamin D. A tan is good because it reduces your ability to make Vitamin D . It is in effect a regulating mechanism of your vit d level.
The epsom salt helps with not burning too quickly, i. e prevents erythema appearing too quickly on newbie skin.It is magnesium _Sulfate_.
To recap a tan means you are getting to optimum level for your n=1. If you are optimal everything is in balance but in constant flux. It can also mean that you have had all the vitamin D your body can make with the cholesterol and sulfur it can lay its hands on. And then there are parts that will not tan at all. That story is more complicated.

# Thread: Hacking Make Up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 49
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-21T18:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219639>

There was some discussion a few weeks ago about stopping using make up.

I know it's the right thing to do and because I execute fairly quickly I began about two or three weeks ago.

This has been such a huge thing for me as my skin has always been terrible (only just realised that blue light was probably the cause of my acne from 14 - 37 (with the exception of two pregnancies/breastfeeding where my skin was flawless).

Because I have a bit of colour on my face it's working out not too bad. My skin on my face has always been quite red but it's not looking so red now against a browner complexion than a white one - if that makes sense?

I'm still using some powdered eyeliner and Dr. Haushka mascara (wearing mascara all these years is probably the reason my eyelashes need mascara to stand out) but that's all I'm putting on my face.

Psychologically this has been a real turning point: being willing to face the world without paint.

I wonder if other women here could share what they do with make up or what they've found since giving it up?

Any other hacks?

### Rela — 2017-07-21T22:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219675>

I am also going makeup free..... Since last 3-4 months I just did it twice for night parties.My melasma is worse but I am way more confident.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-22T00:36:10+0300

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-07-22T01:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219694>

Epic blog Jack and am looking forward to that new book!

My skin has done nothing but improve over the past two years and especially since giving up the foundation and powders. I only wear a little organic mascara and eyeliner once a week when hubby and I go out for our date day. Morning sun has been so amazing for my skin, and not just my face. I keep it simple ladies. I have nothing on my facial skin in my profile picture. That is my sun callus and just a little organic mascara and eyeliner. Your natural beauty is going to shine through when you begin to make your melatonin and dopamine and you sleep well and regenerate. You are going to go out in the world and sparkle like a beautiful diamond! I'm not being vain here either, just sharing the truth with you. You just have to begin and you will know it then. Take off your mask and set yourself free and begin! You won't look back. My favorite facial moisturizer has become organic coconut oil with some added jasmine oil and a little MB now. I also hack with a derma roller twice per week, to help build the collagen when I go out into the morning sun and I drink nothing but my good spring water and my seafood and fish are crucial too.

### caroline — 2017-07-22T02:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219700>

I never liked makeup anyway - it always felt like a mask to me so it was no big deal to go with out - and I have never had the patience for mascara etc.
A few times I would stop at a Channel makeup counter at a department store and they would make me up. I would just go -"Are you kidding me??? Is this how you think I look better??????"

I just use some oil in coconut oil with MB now . Ghee made my face breakout a little.

Just to throw a spanner in the works Amazing Emma ....A lot of us gave up all jewellery quite awhile back ......

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-22T12:11:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219727>

I think I wore a bit of mascara last year for DD's graduation. My vanity (I prefer to call it my dopamine lift ...) is getting a bit of colour through my hair every 12 weeks. Not enough grey for it to be a graceful grey IYKWIM, but its enough to look obvious in my otherwise naturally brown hair. But I do have a bet on with my hairdresser that I can stop it getting worse, or even possibly reversing it. She's interested because a lot of women are going grey early, and she herself is in a high nnemf blue light toxic environment. (She's made changes. She has not only got the pics from my handbag, I took in the file ) But I've seen women in there who obviously dye their hair, they are plastered in makeup, sat there having stuff painted on their nails. And staring at their phones if they are waiting. My hairdresser usually asks what crazy thing I'm up to .... not sure next time what she'll make of my fish oil hair conditioning treatment and fish oil face and body cream. Don't think she'll be offering to sell it through the salon.

### Billybats — 2017-07-22T14:23:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219733>

Hi Emma and others,

I am 53 years old and used make up on and off only for special occasions because I was allergic to it all. I have tried DIY, it was ok but still bothered me. I had awesome skin in high school but when I was 20 I tried birth control pills for less than a year and broke out ever since really bad. It has gotten better since menopause and when I was using progesterone cream for the same amount of time I have been in menopause but since I stopped using progesterone it started back a little. So I shall see what happens down the line. As for sunscreen, maybe used that a total combined of 2 years my whole life, allergic to that too.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-23T11:47:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219809>

@[ScottishEmma](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20059/) This is one of the pics I've shown to my hairdresser, about the implications of blue light generally, but also on hair greying prematurely. (Pic From Reality 3). My hair hack is based around this, because I'm aware that having the hair colour is technically makeup, and I'm just disguising the heteroplasmy rate on my scalp.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-7-23_9-0-4-png.5386/)
Its also why I used a sun hat in very high UV (when we get it ). It has holes I can see through, I call it my kiniki hat . Still lets a lot of light through, but my hypothesis  is that my grey springs mainly from my hair parting, and the hat helps protect my scalp RBCs and hair pigment from over bleaching from high UV, and keeps the CSF cooler. My scalp massage with my mix was really good, (hindsight - maybe should have done a patch test first, but luckily my scalp and hair loved it).

### kvond — 2017-07-25T15:58:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-220035>

I did here from a practitioner of a kind of brain pulsing - sorry it's a thing out here a doc invented for newborns and trauma from birth. In any event, metal glasses change the pulsations of the limbic system fluid in the brain.

Here's a blurb from them: Craniosacral Fascial Therapy (CFT) is based on Dr. Gillespie's theory that the Craniosacral and Fascial systems are one. This system originates in the body's core around the brain and spinal cord. The body's fascial web extends from that core outward in all directions. It surrounds and permeates every structure in the body down to the cellular level. Strain anywhere in that system may effect brain function and craniosacral mobility. CFT techniques are designed to facilitate and follow the release of strain allowing optimal mobility,
and therefore better function.

### Just Breathe — 2017-07-25T23:37:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-220078>

I need less and less makeup the healthier I get! I'm down to eye makeup only and lip color for the office. No acne or old lady spots (melasma?) on my face anymore.
I also don't need coconut oil on my face (was helpful for acne and moisturization back then.) I use less body and hand lotion now too.

When I'm feeling rough, I get more sunlight, a red light treatment, or use a small bit of vitamin c in water and smear it on my face.

> If I could just hack long beautiful eye lashes (my 2 year old daughter's are to die for!!) I'll be happy  I feel that without mascara I kinda look ill. My eyes are small.
>
> Any hacks for mascara?

My youngest brother is the one in the family with the best eyelashes . I got mine dyed once, didn't last long enough and I decided life's too short to fake it. When I talk to people if what they are noticing is that I'm not wearing mascara or other eye makeup, rather than the light in my eyes, they are not in my tribe. My DD's tutors at uni were horrified that she had never been vaccinated, and I think they were expecting some hippy type parents to turn up for her graduation last year, (I was soooooo tempted to oblige ....), but that was the last time I wore mascara. I now see my eyelashes as antenna - to be covered or clogged up as little as possible.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-26T12:30:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-220114>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=220050)
>
> That's interesting! I haven't been paying attention to my hearing acuity. I am very auditory.

I wonder about people with pierced ears ...Especially those who have several piercings on their external satellite dishes. Had mine pierced in my 20's but eventually stopped using earrings because if I left them out for a few days, my lobes were attempting to heal. Realised I was reopening a wound.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-27T08:28:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-220185>

I got mine repierced last year. I wore earrings a few times then kinda forgot. I'm done with piercings now. I even got my nose pierced but that was an epic disaster from start to finish...

### Stardust — 2017-07-27T23:13:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-220247>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=219841)
>
> Wow! Well done. I'm using a natural handmade exfoliator and moisturiser right now. I find my skin gets all gunky if I don't use something...

Can we get a recipe?! I use an exfoliator for my body and face from time to time. I use honey, sugar, coconut oil and essential oils.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-28T08:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-220271>

> [Stardust said:](/goto/post?id=220247)

Sounds lovely. I buy them from a woman who makes them handmade with essential oils. Too lazy to make my own

### Janet Shold — 2017-12-31T21:38:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-231355>

I haven't worn makeup in years, and stopped colouring my grey years ago, too. I stopped just to avoid all the chemicals in them. I also make my own soap, so I know what's in that, but I can't seem to stop using commercial shampoo and conditioner.  That's the last thing to go, I suppose, as soon as I find a good recipe to make my own. I also stopped wearing earings in my pierced ears many years ago because I reacted to most metals. So at least I've been doing some things right, even if accidentally!

### JanSz — 2017-12-31T22:10:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-231357>

Way back then, say 1940-1950 when my Mom would wash my hair (using plain rough soap, that often she made herself), she would mix water with vinegar for the last rinse.

\-----

### Just Breathe — 2018-01-10T23:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-232208>

> [Just Breathe said:](/goto/post?id=220078)
>
> I need less and less makeup the healthier I get! I'm down to eye makeup only and lip color for the office. No acne or old lady spots (melasma?) on my face anymore.
> I also don't need coconut oil on my face (was helpful for acne and moisturization back then.) I use less body and hand lotion now too.
>
> When I'm feeling rough, I get more sunlight, a red light treatment, or use a small bit of vitamin c in water and smear it on my face.

I'm wearing more makeup to the office and glasses these days, in an attempt to block fluorescent light. I remove glasses once I'm outside.

### julietanannen — 2023-01-18T12:15:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-319057>

It's all about finding what works for you and what makes you feel good
about yourself. And don't be too hard on yourself, taking care of your
skin and yourself is a journey, not a destination. Keep sharing your
experience and let's learn from each other!

### Rohitstad — 2023-01-18T12:30:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-319058>

Giving up makeup can be a big step, but it sounds like you're already seeing some positive changes in your skin. I agree that paying attention to skin care can help reduce the need for makeup. I also recommend checking out [bringingbeauty.co](https://bringingbeauty.co) for some tips and tricks on natural skincare. It's all about finding a balance that works for you and makes you feel confident. Keep up the good work!
And don't forget, even if you choose to continue using makeup, it's important to be mindful of the ingredients and choose products that are non-toxic and gentle on your skin.

# Thread: Please can you tell me if there's anything I've missed?  Thanks a lot.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JKLearner123 — 2017-07-22T14:54:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-219735>

Looking at ways to improve health, and currently doing:

\* Sunlight exposure - AM (including circadian rhythm 'reset'), midday (vitamin D), sunset (feels good!).
\* No UV avoidance - no sunblock, no sunglasses. (Please ignore my out of date mugshot!)
\* CT - I swim in cold seawater.
\* Grounding - occurs when I go for a swim, plus I use earthing mats/sheets at home.
\* I.F. - 5 hour eating window.
\* DHA - I eat lots of seafood.
\* Wireless avoidance - I don't use any wireless devices.
\* Exercise - walking, and resistance, eg chest expander. Would like to start weights training.
\* RF blocking - RF blocking fabric on/around bed.
\* Blue light avoidance -
\+ My PC uses f.lux software.
\+ I completely turn off my PC monitor's blue color component.
\+ I use blue light blocking glasses in the evening.
\+ I use 'old fashioned' red colored light bulbs.

Not sure if this helps:

\* Freeze fruit and veg, to structure their internal water. (Would like to do this with water, but TBH, I can't be bothered ATM.)
\* Eat prebiotic fiber. (I find digestive enzymes helpful.)
\* Switch off most electrical circuits at night.

Wish list:

\* Infrared sauna/exposure. How can I do this on the cheap?

What about:

\* Blocking RF around me during the day?
\* Magnetise drinking water?
\* 'Color therapy' - red and purple?

Thanks very much for any comments about any of this!

**EDIT:** An "edit list" of things that I forgot to mention when I originally posted this thread -

\* Stress reduction techniques. (There are many.)

### drezy — 2017-07-22T16:16:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-219736>

> [JKLearner123 said:](/goto/post?id=219735)
>
> Looking at ways to improve health, and currently doing:

\* Sunlight exposure - AM (including circadian rhythm 'reset'), midday (vitamin D), sunset (feels good!).
\[\\quote\]
Nice. I just tested my solar callous with 3 hours of UVindex 12(and thereabouts) light followed 2 days later by 4 hours of UV 13. Zero burn. I just look all bronze.

> \* Grounding - occurs when I go for a swim, plus I use earthing mats/sheets at home.

Careful, it could be double edged if what you're grounding to is noisy or just a bad ground.

> Wish list:
> \* Infrared sauna/exposure. How can I do this on the cheap?

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) is all over this angle look at the big light near the bottom of this post
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)
[https://www.amazon.com/CMVision-IR130-Outdoor-300-400ft-Illuminator/dp/B004F9LF7E/ref=sr\_1\_fkmr3\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1488125922&sr=8-1-fkmr3&keywords=infrared flood light built](https://www.amazon.com/CMVision-IR130-Outdoor-300-400ft-Illuminator/dp/B004F9LF7E/ref=sr_1_fkmr3_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1488125922&sr=8-1-fkmr3&keywords=infrared+flood+light+built)

What about:

> \* Blocking RF around me during the day?

Avoidance is best. All I can say is that you wouldn't the first person walking a daily route with a meter on if you tried it...\[/quote\]

### JanSz — 2017-07-22T16:46:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-219738>

> [JKLearner123 said:](/goto/post?id=219735)
>
> plus I use earthing mats/sheets at home.
> \* DHA - I eat lots of seafood.

If you are grounding earthing mats with very short copper cable, to a separate 1" 10feet long copper pole that is sunk in the ground far away from any underground electrical stuff,.................
ask @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) for more details

\=====================================
Note;
DHA is a super very important.
Luckily small amounts last long time, very long half life.
Seafood usually comes with highly excessive EPA (that wrecks havoc with Omega6).

To decide if I should eat seafood I do:

**Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum**

Currently I eat seafood rarely.
But plan on getting small amount of linseed oil (ALA)

My results 10 days ago.
On a chart below, in column %Status=0 (zero)=ideal
acceptable is (plus minus 25%)

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### JKLearner123 — 2017-07-22T17:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-219741>

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) & @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), thanks a lot for your help/information!

Re: solar callous. I will investigate this topic. I saw JK discussing this topic recently. (Might have been on his faceboook feed?)

Re: grounding. I will investigate about the possibility of poor grounding. You've jogged my memory about something. I use dirty electricity filters in the house. I've tested them, using a DE meter, and they work well. For instance, with the DE filters switched off, the DE meter reading is high. With the DE filters switched on, the DE meter reading is about 10x less.

Re: 850nM LED light. Thanks! I had no idea about these devices. Very interesting. I am currently browsing 850nM LED lights on my local (UK) amazon site. I guess I'll need one that:

\+ Can be set to "on" during daylight hours, and not just automatically when it's dark.
\+ Works indoors
\+ Uses a standard (for UK) indoor electrical power point socket.

My first infrared sauna came from Craigslist for about $250. They were downsizing and I was the first to call. Not sure where you live, but check ads for nearby cities. I had an Craigslist search app set up for Houston and Austin. It notified immediately whenever a sauna was posted.

### JKLearner123 — 2017-07-24T11:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-219902>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), one of Dr Mercola's online articles says -

"It’s important to realize that photobiomodulation is highly biphasic in dose, meaning the benefit can disappear by using too much light...Ten minutes or half an hour does no harm at all … Mostly, I tell people they can use these things for 10 or 20 minutes a day and it’ll have major benefits."

You may want to reduce your timing from hours to minutes. I'm really sorry, but atm, I can't find the comment online that says that there were some problems with that particular LED unit. It may be due to its wattage? (For instance, aim for a wattage less than 10W. That unit seems powerful. It may be greater than 10W?) If I find that comment about the unit, I'll post back. I was just browsing the net two days ago, and spotted that comment, but now I can't find it. It would be great if Dr Mercola would post a follow-up about that specific unit, and if he still uses it, or has switched to using a different model. In the meantime, I have ordered a much smaller and less powerful unit, and will aim to use it for about 15 minutes a day..

### JKLearner123 — 2017-07-29T20:40:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-220385>

^ Thanks for everyone's comments so far. I received the small 850nM LED light from Amazon. I'm just messing about with it atm, not sure what I'm doing. Just experimenting to see if anything works -

\- Put the light on the back of my neck. (Was thinking vagus nerve.)
\- Put it on my wrist etc. (Was thinking Quantlet when I tried this. Don't own one of those yet.)
\- I hold the light, so that its LEDs are facing my palm.
\- Put my bare feet on it. (Not sure what I was thinking, lol.)

The lights are warm, but don't burn. Too early to say if any of this helps. I'd also like to get a ~6hundred nM LED. I guess these will be brighter, and hotter?

# Thread: spectroscope to map out the IR-A and UV-A transition

### WalterNL — 2017-07-23T21:44:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-219852>

### Ted — 2017-07-24T02:32:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-219872>

The Dminder app tells you when the uv appears in the AM...that would be uva. Prior to that time would be red.

### David Moorefield — 2017-07-24T16:25:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-219933>

D is from UV-B which is what dminder shows, we need a chart or app showing when UV-A starts yearly by latitude if a spectroscope isn't practical

### Ted — 2017-07-25T00:00:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-219972>

That's true, but it shows you the uv index at 1 and 2, before you can make vit d. That should be the uva. Start checking it after sunrise and you'll see what I'm talking about.

### drezy — 2017-07-25T16:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-220036>

Does anyone know what angle UVA shows up at?

If so we could probably make a crude guess using a site like this [http://solarelectricityhandbook.com/solar-angle-calculator.html](http://solarelectricityhandbook.com/solar-angle-calculator.html)

At sunrise (5:40) here its 0 Deg, at midday (13:10) 50 Deg. So you could calculate roughly the angle of the sun at any time of day...

### Sue-UK — 2017-08-16T09:35:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-221755>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=221699)
>
> According to this graph, UVA appears around about 5 degrees. This makes me think I am missing The VIS + IR bit of the sun as due to terrain, I don't see sun until 35 minutes after rising. I estimate that the sun raises at around 7 degrees an hour....

If its light there's photons, and you don't need a lot for signalling (think how few blue ones can shut off melatonin at night ....). As the sky colour changes the ratio and dominance of different colours changes, and the brain also registers the increasing crescendo. Many species live on terrain that blocks the direct rays or the camera image of the rising sun. I see it that the photons are not the sun, but they are of the sun, information carriers, and that I am still bathing in the light, and harvesting, even if I can't see the source.

PS thanks for the pic ... It seems to suggest (from the pic I posted earlier) that when yellow becomes the dominant visible frequency in the morning, that UVA is kicking in.

### shiran — 2017-08-16T14:52:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-221763>

Jack mentions the importance of the transition in the morning .
If we are anyway out there every morning, I'm curious why is it so important for you guys to know exactly when the transition is happening ?
People who have limited time in the morning for example..
I would like to be more specific in my recommendation to people regarding the morning exposure..

Another thing I was thinking about was..
If we sleep in a dark room no light gets in ( not like in nature)
how does the body senses it's morning and wakes up naturally ?

### Sue-UK — 2017-08-16T16:18:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-221771>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=221763)
>
> Jack mentions the importance of the transition in the morning .
> If we are anyway out there every morning, I'm curious why is it so important for you guys to know exactly when the transition is happening ?
> People who have limited time in the morning for example..
> I would like to be more specific in my recommendation to people regarding the morning exposure..
>
> Another thing I was thinking about was..
> If we sleep in a dark room no light gets in ( not like in nature)
> how does the body senses it's morning and wakes up naturally ?

If I understand it right, the blue and red in the early visible light switches the pituitary on, stimulating releasing pituitary hormones. The UV coming in switches the hormones off. So if you go straight out to UV it can affect hormone release. If you see the VIS but miss the UVA transition, you could leave the pituitary hormones switched on when they should be off (or at least the seasonal volume turned down IYKWIM). Also the early morning light and a period of UVA preconditions the skin for UVB. IIRC the first bit of UVA may cause DNA damage, but then it stimulates DNA repair, so the longer I stay out in the morning, the better overall response.

Because of animals underground, hibernation etc, I believe we are also heavily influenced by the earth's rotation, as well as the light cues from the environment that fine tunes us to day length and season. If I look at a picture of the magnetosphere, and think of my location entering the magnetotail at night, (change....), in the middle of the magnetotail (change) and then emerging in the morning, (change) I think of it as the darker the room is, the more sensitive I am to that part of the biological magnetic field rhythm of the earth at night, that it improves my sleep biological rhythms and I wake up at the right time without needing a light signal. Then my aim is to "fine tune" it with the natural frequencies of light during the day, turning things on and off, or turning the volume up and down at the right time, with hormones fluctuating, in line with the season and latitude.

Funnily I can sleep past sunrise tho, when sun rises very early I can still sleep until 7 AM, that is when i mostly wake up.. My eyes face east-south when I wake up, but the light still come into my room and I love it . I do not like curtains and closed windows at all!
But I get no fake light at all into my bedroom window.. outside is only green and the heaven...

### drezy — 2017-08-31T03:39:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-222621>

Brent, I loved your questions to doc in the recent Q&A concerning Cuba. Your curiosity may have actually opened up a future residence for my wife and I and I'm grateful that you got doc to dump the info.

Short story:
We are making changes to the unit to make it more sensible. Even @[kvond](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17418/) did not receive her Beta yet. Sorry @[kvond](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17418/) I hope you are getting your morning light regardless

Longer story:

The bright OLED display really kinda sucked from a mitochondriac perspective. We've switched to a LCD screen. It does not emit any light since there is no point for a light sensor to emit it's own light. If you're using it in the dark then you need a little psychological counseling anyway...

Also we found a good LiPo rechargeable battery to slap in there to recharge over micro usb.

Reviewing the sensor manufacturer's data the UV-B is really stretching it:

-   UV-A sensor peak 365nm. The bandwidth (λ0.5) of the UVA channel is within a range of about 350 nm to 375 nm (ok I'm cool enough with that)
-   UV-B sensor peak 330nm. The bandwidth (λ0.5) of this UVB peak is in a range of about 315 nm to 340 nm. (That's kinda stretching it)

We don't want to BS people. The 330nm detection(spurious as it is) does actually help with UVindex erythemal calculations. So I think we're settling on UV-A+ UV-A accumulation(J/cm2) + UVi on the display at this point. I'll hit you with a PM. The good news for you is that a 12 yo performs the labor here. Child labor is cheap, but it's not just one way. I'm building my son up for kick-ass science projects through his educational period.

I literally shoveled horse crap for a few bucks when I was his age. If he puts together UV-A + UVi monitoring units for a few bucks then it seems like a fair generational improvement to me...

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-31T04:11:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-222625>

August webinar is all on Cuba FYI

### brent-next — 2017-08-31T19:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-222663>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=222621)
>
> Brent, I loved your questions to doc in the recent Q&A concerning Cuba. Your curiosity may have actually opened up a future residence for my wife and I and I'm grateful that you got doc to dump the info.

Yes, Dr. Kruse gave some very useful information on Cuba in the August 2017 webinar. Members who haven't listened to it yet should do so, and non-members should consider signing up.

I asked a professional guide to Cuba (from Norway) what she thought about pollution in Cuba. She confirmed that downtown Havana is very polluted, with the outskirts of Havana being slightly less polluted.

She liked the resort towns of Cienfuegos and Trinidad and had this to say about levels of pollution away from Havana.

"Apart from Havana there is very little traffic in Cuba as very few people own their own car or can afford to pay for fuel."

It should be easy to find Canadians who have spent a lot of time in Cuba and can tell you more about it. Canadian travel agencies might also be a good source of up to date travel information.

### Lahelada — 2017-12-26T14:10:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-231092>

How is the spectrometer going @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) ?

I was just checking that my internal clock has adjusted to summertime (it hasn't ) and found out my UVA window. I looked at your figures for when UVA showed up for you in your post ([https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-and-uv-a-transition.19869/page-2#post-221513](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/page-2#post-221513) )when you measured the jump from ) to o.1 % UV. ### drezy — 2017-12-27T22:17:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-231144>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=231092)
>
> How is the spectrometer going @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) ?
>
> I was just checking that my internal clock has adjusted to summertime (it hasn't ) and found out my UVA window. I looked at your figures for when UVA showed up for you in your post ([https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-and-uv-a-transition.19869/page-2#post-221513](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/page-2#post-221513) )when you measured the jump from ) to o.1 % UV. Assuming you measured in Texas that would mean that there is no UV in summer for around the first hour after sunrise. However...
>
> Sean's graph however say 5 degree elevation. That would mean though that your actual window would end at 6.25 not 7.30 ,unless my calculator does not account for Daylight savings time. Or maybe your reading was not adjusted to daylight savings time ?
>
> This is the calculator I use.
> [https://www.esrl.noaa.gov/gmd/grad/solcalc/azel.html](https://www.esrl.noaa.gov/gmd/grad/solcalc/azel.html)

> [philip malone said:](/goto/post?id=231121)
>
> Sounds quite cool : )

Uh oh, I'm getting called out on this one. This side project has been a dream project to work on with my son. It's also become a bit of an engineer-gone-wild (not as exciting as [girls gone wild](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Girls_Gone_Wild_\(franchise\)) BTW ) venture (imagine a pig discovering a cool patch of mud on a summer day).

The hardware designer on this project is pretty caught up. It's his software engineer that is behind a bit(though rumor has it he's tall and handsome FYI).

I'll break it down several month's design changes in pictures:

On the left is the spectral Irradiance responsivity of Rev 0.1. On the right is Rev 0.4 (worked on over the last 6 months). Note a bit of a spectra difference (2 to 14).

Our design goal when viewing simple color temperature meters ([lumu](https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/1365060-REG/lumu_lumu_power_light_meter.html) - $269) was to come in lower than $200 as well as add fine resolution UV & IR detection. Would aspiring film students appreciate a cheaper light meter? I've got feelers out for this currently as well as a dialog with a film equipment rental house. Could many small film producers bankroll some mitochondriac economies of scale savings... Maybe, we'll see.

We bought [this UV meter](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01GT4EF24/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1) ($250) 4 months ago. Did we buy it just to use it personally? Nope. Can we match the results for less on a consumer level with the rev 1.0 sensor? Yep.

Note that from the image on the left(0.1) to the image on the right(0.4) it goes from 2 spectra to 14 spectra. The design goal is that if you have consumer level dollars but are interested in the spectrum from 300-900, like only people here would be, then there should be a solution for you.

We feel strongly in this house about people seeing their environment. We want to become a solution to part of that.

### Lahelada — 2017-12-28T04:00:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-231159>

### WalterNL — 2018-06-06T22:46:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-242862>

I found this website where they modeled the solar spectrum for each location and time on earth: [http://cprm.acom.ucar.edu/Models/TUV/Interactive\_TUV/](http://cprm.acom.ucar.edu/Models/TUV/Interactive_TUV/). Maybe you can use this to check your measurements [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

I mapped the spectrum for my location, didn't change all of the variables except the wavelength start, end, location, date and time. And select Output option 2 if you're gonna try this. Sunrise supposed to be at 5:25 AM now, so it's somewhat odd that it gives a solar spectrum before that. X-axis is nm of light spectrum, Y-axis is W/m^2/nm. So for every nm of the spectrum, the energy of sunlight per square meter. The period around sunrise might not be correct for this model, but you can immediately see the difference in spectrum in the morning and at noon. I also checked my location in winter, and that spectrum looks almost like the summer morning-spectrum below. When calculating the balance between blue and red light (450-500nm and 620-750nm), blue is only 25% of spectral intensity in morning, and increase up to 40% at noon. Meaning red light is always more than two times the spectral intensity of blue light (that's even without infrared). So no wonder that the blue-light devices we have that are ONLY blue, mess with circadian biology.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Here just for UV-spectrum during the hour of sunrise (5:25 actual sunrise). UVA is 320-400nm, and factors like clouds, aerosols, ozone column limit how much UVA reaches Earth surface (not accounted for in this graph). So the UVA-transition period probably needs a certain threshold of spectral intensity before those rays get to where we live.

That's a cool site. Thanks. I'll dig in.

Yeah you're seeing the particle-like

I have my co-developer back this summer. He and I have plans to move this further along together. He was busy with school this year. He worked hard and aced every class.

I've got some test software ready to start talking to hardware and could even be useful to an end user. I'm not a fan of any light emitting screens and will opt for old-school monochrome LCD. Still I think it's be nice to plug into a laptop for a "better" display if possible. Here's a look of beta debug + user software running hypothetical spectra obstacle courses:

It's written to run on mac, linux, and windows to display a live spectra. The black dots are discreet measured data and the lines are interpolated in between the points.

I know that my spectra is inverted. I need to mirror (horizontal flip) it around the 550nm green point where red goes higher spectrum and blue lower spectrum.

Just wanted to show that this is not a dead effort.

### Phosphene — 2018-06-07T06:04:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-242890>

I hate how this “quick” TUV radiation calculator rather nonchalantly provides input fields for Aerosols. Right below Clouds. As if aerosols have every right to be there...

More disturbing (at least on first glance) is the info in the calculator’s instructions indicating aerosols have been used/studied since at least 1967? I know very little about all this but my gut is saying NOOOOOOO! How sad.

(Or is this trying to account for the effects of everyday use of aerosols on the ground—like grandma’s Aqua Net hairspray? )

“Aerosols: The typical continental mid-latituide aerosol profile from Elterman (1967) is used, and can be scaled by a user-specified optical depth at 550 nm.”

[http://cprm.acom.ucar.edu/Models/TUV/Interactive\_TUV/InstructionsWebTUV.pdf](http://cprm.acom.ucar.edu/Models/TUV/Interactive_TUV/InstructionsWebTUV.pdf)

### WalterNL — 2018-06-07T07:53:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-242895>

[@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/) Even worse on this page: [https://www2.acom.ucar.edu/modeling/tropospheric-ultraviolet-and-visible-tuv-radiation-model](https://www2.acom.ucar.edu/modeling/tropospheric-ultraviolet-and-visible-tuv-radiation-model)

UV radiation is also harmful to living organisms and detrimental to human health. High doses of UV radiation are considered the major contributing factor for the development of skin cancer or cataracts. UV radiation can weaken the human immune system and can affect crop yields and phytoplankton activity (to only name a few effects).
​

Still a useful tool though, for general purposes.

# Thread: Grounding sheets and pads
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-sheets-and-pads.19870/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Technosmith — 2017-07-23T18:42:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-sheets-and-pads.19870/post-219834>

Hey folks,

After listening to Jack's podcast with 'The Life Stylist' (

) I have become concerned about my use of grounding sheets and pads.

I tested both these devices with a volt meter and they registered as bringing down the voltage. However they are both hooked up directly to the UK electrical mains circuit, which Jack is saying is likely a really bad thing to do.

Can anyone confirm the best approach to take here?

Jack - should I ditch these products?

Thanks,

Phil

### JanSz — 2017-07-24T00:05:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-sheets-and-pads.19870/post-219868>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-71#post-213547](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-71#post-213547)

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-24T14:55:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-sheets-and-pads.19870/post-219918>

Personally, unless I could plug it straight into the ground I wouldn't bother.

I used to have grounding pads and sheets but I heard Daniel Vitalis talking about the sheets conducting RF due to the metal in them.

I think you're better using the real thing as much as you can...

Leather soled shoes too!

### drezy — 2017-07-24T15:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-sheets-and-pads.19870/post-219928>

I'm a huge fan of people increasing their ability to see their environments more fully(meters and understanding the physics). It really all depends on your environment.

With ever new device/tech you add to fix a problem you can also be making it worse. It's like the game Jenga except it's like adding blocks and still trying not to knock over the tower.

Any ground that I use on my body runs no longer than 4' to actual ground. I'm not happy with that and trying to improve on it too. Nothing is going to beat a couple hours with feet on earth per day. That said, I'm sure I can find some product out there that will claim to "increase my grounding speed so that I can fit it into my busy schedule." Technically such a thing does exist, it's called a stream or river.

The canopy systems look interesting to me. I get how there can be less downside with them. Grounding to the grid just keeps putting this image in my head:

Thanks folks.

So the consensus it would seem is it's probably better to do without?

I guess the idea of being able to be grounded all-night is the appeal. Maybe I should just find a comfortable rock near my house and adapt. I wonder how good you would feel in the morning?

Anyone tried sleeping on the beach?

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-01T17:28:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-sheets-and-pads.19870/post-236060>

Yes, the only safe ground is the earth, and where it's not contaminated by stray fields, which is usually the case in heavily populated areas, especially close to power plants and industries. And the "ground" in house wiring is never a clean ground.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-01T18:31:45+0200

# Thread: Valerie's Radical Mitochondriac Adventure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 56
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2017-07-23T22:04:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-219854>

**How I found my way here** ([Meet & Greet thread](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/re-awakening-and-happy-to-be-here.19743/)).
During my research and brainstorming on how to help my son prevent a cancer relapse, and to prevent other chronic health issues for myself and the rest of the family, I had an ah-ha moment. I cannot address all the toxins he will face in his environment. They are constantly increasing and too numerous already. Instead, how am I going to raise his frequency to make him more impervious to the toxins he does encounter? That sums up my approach to wellness--minimize the toxins, detox anyway, and maximize the strengtheners.

**10,000 foot view of my relationship to weight and food** (my previous markers of "health"):
Currently 5'7" & 180lb. I have crossed the threshold of obesity. I have some darn good excuses, but the time for resting on that is done. Before Baby #1 I was 165, my heaviest to date. Before Baby #2 I had implemented a lot of life changes and was around 135lb (and dropping). I'm on this mitochondriac journey to get back there and inspire the health of my family and whoever else is receptive.

I was 120lb when I graduated high school. After college I was 130-135. I moved to SF, CA and walked almost everywhere and ate a ton of sushi. I went to law school in CT and went up to the 150s. I moved back to PA, my home state, and those subsequent stressful years took me to that 165.

I carried baby weight after #1 for about a year then suddenly started dropping it. I attributed it to my life changes (combined with breast feeding)-I was already avoiding fast foods and most processed foods but I began eating even more organic whole foods, transitioned from low fat dairy products to as fat and raw as I could, and discovered Weston A Price. We got a Berkey filter to get rid of fluoride. Added fermented foods to my diet. People started asking me what I was doing to drop weight so quickly when I chose to stop eating gluten for a month to see how it affected me after reading Wheat Belly. I also dropped booze from my diet as a year long personal challenge for the same reason. In retrospect, I see the bigger picture: I was eating paleo without labeling it as such, and I was outside a lot due to cycling. This included 150 miles of leisurely cycling for 5 days on the Great Allegheny Passage with our 1 year old--15 to 50 miles cycling daily then exploring small towns or nature, away from a lot of EMFs and blue light. My body feels better just _thinking_ about that vacation.

In stark contrast, after Baby #2 I became caregiver to two immediate family members navigating life-threatening illnesses. Stress eating hospital food (and not turning down any meals that people would generously give us) turned into a carb fest for almost a year now, and there was no cycling vacation. And honestly, I wasn't remotely interested in abstaining from alcohol this past year either.

All that said, I'm ready for the next phase of my life to begin.

**Details of note wrt a Mitochondriac Life**
Recognizing details of my life and preferences that elucidate my mitochondriac leanings-

-   Growing up, I was that girl that could eat ANYTHING and stay skinny. My dad called me a garbage disposal. I would actually pride myself on how big my appetite was. I must have been quite optimal then, because my body took a lot of years of dietary abuse--it was the 80s and 90s. McDonalds, Beefaroni, Velveeta Mac & Cheese were among my favorites. I was a self proscribed carnivore, though I would eat some veggies.
-   I disliked pizza as a kid, love it now (and I see why; hoping to reverse that preference again)
-   I love salty things. Love love love. I've never had much of a sweet tooth, even as a child (except for the phase where it was "cool" to just eat pixie stix and wash it down with mountain dew. It was actually a coach that encouraged us kids to do that... wtf)
-   I used to despise eggplant, but recently started adoring it. Somewhere around here I think I read we crave the things that are actually harming us...so I'm looking at the leaky gut protocol now.
-   I love seafood, especially crabs. My mother told me she craved crabs while she was pregnant with me and would go to all-you-can-eat seafood buffets. Oysters give me serious joy.
-   My body temp is generally low, 96 or 97 degrees, which I find interesting since I am clearly not optimized right now.
-   I've always had brittle nails
-   In the past I was told that sleeping next to me was like sleeping next to a furnace (but I am not now)
-   I always have high iron counts, which I discovered during pregnancy & confirmed with #2. I had to take prenatal vitamins without iron, otherwise I got wicked headaches.
-   I eschewed much technology. Wouldn't get a cell phone for the longest time, didn't want to convert to a smart phone. Got rid of my TV in college and haven't owned one since, except the one in SF that was given to me because my partner wanted to be able to watch TV while he was in my apartment. Tech and electrical things fry or goes haywire around me. Light bulbs pop, appliances randomly turn off or on, cell phones stop working, I sometimes get really creepy long and static-y voice mails etc.
-   My vision is different in each eye. I am nearsighted in my left eye. My right has astigmatism and is slightly nearsighted and farsighted. In my left I see more blues and in my right I see more yellows.

-   I stopped using sunscreen this year, despite being a pale person of Irish/Eastern European descent. I've gotten seriously sun burned in the past despite using the sunscreen. I avoided the sun, especially at 12-3. I covered up and wore sun glasses. Now I'm spending time with the sun rise and deliberately going out midday and I'm tanning with freckles.
-   Not sure how or if this is relevant, but I'm throwing it in here. I had a bad reaction to the non-hormonal, copper IUD. I bled for a week heavily every 3 weeks; I had serious emotional imbalances including rage (which was just dismissed as "normal" and PPD; I developed dry eye; I got a transient cyst by my ovary. I tried the copper IUD because I wanted to avoid hormones. So I stopped birth control altogether in 2014 and now practice Natural Family Planning. It has been amazing for my health on many levels and in many ways. It definitely helps move me back to attunement with cycles of my body and the natural world.

**Goals:**

-   Get back to a healthy body weight
-   Heal my vision. Currently reading Take Off Your Glasses and See.
-   Return to daily ashtanga practice
-   Continue educating myself on the science here, which is so in tune with the spiritual practices and realizations I've had over the last few years. I'm beyond stoked.

**One Particular Personal Challenge:
**
Meat. I know meat is considered a staple of being a mitochondriac and part of this tribe. I'm going to share these thoughts here anyway because it is part of my journey and perhaps others have been down the same path. After growing up a self-proscribed carnivore, my diet has shifted to near-vegetarianism. I have been on an intuitive eating journey, and I had been feeling called to eliminate meat from my diet. During the time I was closest to being meat-free, I was insanely hungry constantly. My partner convinced me to have bacon, and the hunger immediately ceased. I had been focused on protein (I'm embarrassed to admit, after all I've learned about protein in foods and in the body), but I realized then it was the fat I needed. I can eat avocados and nuts and olives all friggin day. But none of that hits it home like meat fat. I used to eat a pound of bacon myself every weekend (gotta love those grandparents "spoiling" you!). I am sure this is not surprising to anyone here.

I do my best to ethically source the meat I do eat. I aim for only organic (though it hasn't been that way this past year with all the exceptions I made due to time and stress). I noticed, however, that I now get sick when I eat red meat--the better quality, the more sick I am. I can only surmise that my gut flora is now an environment to support the veggies and carbs, rather than meat. Sorry for the TMI, but good quality red meat now goes right through me. Chicken and pork are not as bad, but still gives me stomach aches and loose stool.

That being said, I absolutely love organ meats, marrow, and bone broth, and I do not feel sick after eating those.

I'm struggling with this. I am still working on wading through all the conflicting information about whether we can get all the nutrients we need without eating meat. I had myself just about convinced that I could, including B12 from fermented foods. So I'll be doing a lot of homework on this site.

I have been eliminating meat from my diet as an intuitive journey. My body was giving me hints such as the only morning sickness I felt during my pregnancy was the 3 times I ate red meat, and the increasing stomach sickness. The concept of nonviolence (ahimsa) also resonates with me. I have legit concerns about the treatment of animals, and serious concerns about the toxins that can be found in meats. So how do I resolve this? Would I feel okay about eating meat if I knew the animal was treated well during its lifetime, and I could confidently know the flesh was as toxin-free as possible? I'm not sure yet, I'm still sitting with that one. I am seriously considering trying to meet a cow before it is slaughtered, then utilizing the whole thing. Or re-wilding myself and hunting some squirrel.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-23T22:53:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-219860>

One more thing I want to add--

**Goal:** Moving. I feel less well when I am in this geographic location. It happens to be my home town. After I moved away then moved back I developed "allergies". I have coughs/congestion that linger for weeks after a cold. I get colds, which I never used to. Once I moved home, my depression increased and reached critical mass. My libido disappeared. My weight skyrocketed, except during the time I was outside with cycling and camping all the time. This is the first time I'm living in a place with wi-fi (which is being turned off now). I also know we live near 5 cell towers and the effects cannot be shielded in the house. The smart meter went on our home a couple of years ago and as of yet there is no opt out available, I can only shield it best I can. When I travel I feel instantly better. It was easy to chalk that up to being "on vacation" or away from other life stressors. I now am confident in my intuition that I actually physically feel better because this house/area seems to be a nnEMF nightmare.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-24T01:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-219871>

Also forgot to add I recently realized I have diastasis recti, and way too much ab flab on top of that. So I'm working on realigning my core, and I know the information here will help with the ab flab. Even when I was heavy before it wasn't centered on my gut, it was evenly distributed. So this is a new thing.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-25T20:25:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-220057>

Have been waking up naturally before the sunrise. I used to get up at dawn all the time...then I had kids. I know the sleep is so important for my well-being so I stopped heeding the call and slept in as much as possible. Sleeping in for me means like 7 or 7:30, but I thought those extra minutes were important. Revisiting the sunrise has been, instead, great for my wellbeing. I'll catch the extra s after dusk instead. I was never a night owl anyway.

I've done a 180 and I'm making it a point to get into the sun, rather than stay out of it. Loving it! I'm so surprised at how I'm craving it and savoring the light. It never felt good like this--my skin burned and my eyes hurt. No more! I read Take Off Your Glasses and See while sunbathing and earthing. I got some nudist outdoors time in. I'm cycling again. I take breaks to simply go outside and suck up the sun (barefoot of course). I went swimming...and jumped right into that chilly water! So many things I had stopped doing. It's amazing how we can lose ourselves doing what we "should" do. Go to college, go to grad school, get a professional job, have the kids, volunteer the time--"How DO you do it all?" Ugh.

Prioritizing the de-EMF-ing of the home. Tech has always been my nemesis. Right now I can only get internet through wifi or smart phone data. I cannot figure out why the ethernet cable is not working. I will figure it out. In the interim, will shut off the wifi when not in use. Next stop, smart meter shielding and legislative action for an opt out.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-25T20:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-220058>

Have been joined by the kiddos sporadically for sunrise. I cannot yet bring myself to wake up sleeping children. Right now any alone time I can have is precious and restorative. Once the elder begins school this fall I shall add sunrise to our morning routine, because he definitely needs an optimal body to ensure no relapse.

Our first mitochondriac sunrise together, was actually a really special time. He was calmer and quieter than normal until the sun broke the horizon. He rarely is still or quiet. We talked and laughed together as he cuddled up with me. Interestingly though, he said his eyes didn't like the sun. I wasn't anticipating that because he's outside so frequently.

I have no idea how to rotate this image, but there he is, milk thistle tea with extra honey, and his legos. My little man.

WONDERFUL!!

My kids are trained now that when Mummy shouts "THE SUN IS OUTSIDE" in the morning it's time to run downstairs and get out naked  To be fair that isn't very often but the kids are now programmed (in a good way) that the sun is good and they should be completely naked in it first part of the day. I love instilling this in them, Their nursery is borderline insane about covering them up completely - even going as far as to put a long sleeved top on top of my boy's tshirt (hot!!!) so that he was completely protected from the sun.
I've had to correct the things he is saying about our need for sun cream etc.

That's such a beautiful pic of your boy

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-29T01:24:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-220340>

Have been upping my seafood, and I love it. I rarely eat it anymore, even though it has always been one of my favorites, even as a kid. Amazing how many things you can let slip to please or accommodate other people. Lesson learned. Today we discovered my toddler likes salmon. This kid has been eating like a bird because of his chemo but he ate 5 oz fish today! He was back and forth on the crabs I made last night. My weight is down a few pounds just by living the sphinx life and hacking sun-time even though I am just carving out the little time I can. I'm knocking out all the carbs and sugars I can in a manner that is realistic with the demands on my life, time, and sanity right now. It is mostly no carbs or sugar, unless someone offers food and I am not in a position to turn it down. It's already making a difference. I mentioned above I am struggling with the meat idea. I finished Epi-Paleo RX today and decided I'm just going to go at it, full hog, so to speak. I can see so clearly that my food preferences as a child were mitochondriac. I absolutely loved seafood and meat and fats and salt; I never was much for pasta or pizza or sweets. I wasn't in the sun as much, but the more I learn the more I realize how I must have been super leptin sensitive. I also had a crazy high threshold for pain. I have previously done my own kind of biohacking, usually elimination of a particular type of food/beverage to see how it affected my body, mind and mood. In the spirit of biohacking, I'm just going to put my ethical thoughts about meat aside for the moment and just according to the Epi-Paleo RX rules and assess my reaction. I suspect I may have some gut issues, since my flora has now adapted to less meats, but I'm seriously upping the MCT and coconut oil, and trying to be mindful of the leaky-gut info here and in the book. We shall see.

In other news, Momming is hard. It took me about 20 minutes and 5 interruptions to write this. This too shall pass.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-29T02:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-220341>

Nice journal.......love it.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-31T19:35:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-220541>

Cycled 30 miles yesterday with the family. Beautiful trail, flat but hella gravely. 10mph average. Not fantastic, but frankly I'm so out of shape and overweight that I was quite pleased with my performance. I surprised myself actually, because I actually enjoyed it. In the past, cycling was something I grudgingly did just to do something healthy, with the family together. If no one cried, it was a good ride. I got quite pissy actually when my Dude would tell me to go faster, go farther. I thought he should be happy I was on the damn bike to begin with, end of achievement. I'm relishing it in a new way lately. Was out in the mid-day sun, which I used to avoid like the plague. I can't believe how fast my sun sensitivity has flipped, just by living like the sphinx, avoiding sun screen, and tweaking the diet which has been awesome because I'm upping the foods I love and working to avoid the carb crap I never wanted to eat anyway. Got a little red because I was out in the intense sun longer than other times, but already fading today. Lost 5 lbs even with the sporadic light exercises here. I've learned trying to change everything all at once is not always the best plan, and is certainly not practical when you're caring for children and running a household. Face dunks are next on my list. I'm on a mission to find a good cold spring for swimming...... Looking forward to this trend continuing!

### ValerieBee — 2017-08-05T00:27:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-220942>

Something is definitely up! Today a random stranger told me I'm beautiful, in a noncreepy way; I got ogled by a dude in a passing car..he paced me; then I just got carded buying wine. (Ok not paleo...but it is a dry organic red!) I knew better than to think age defines beauty, but I guess thought my best days were behind me. Magnetism isn't about age, or apparently even attitude, because I didn't start the day in the best mood. I'm going to embrace the thought that the present is sweet, and the best is yet to come.

### ValerieBee — 2017-08-09T23:33:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-221288>

Ok on the flip side now. I feel quite yucky. Despondent this morning and fixated on the negative...and up a couple of pounds. What changed? This past weekend was my Little Man's 5th birthday. I let all the rules go as so many people offered cake I couldn't refuse, hoagies, or other refined carb-alicious "foods". Of course much less seafood and quality meats. (We went to an event for kids who have undergone serious medical treatments, and they knew it was his birthday, hence the feeling I couldn't refuse much, despite my tiny portions, then all the grandparents wanted to treat him and us, and we also went to another kid's birthday party...). After the year we all went through I didn't want to poop on anyone's party, so I just went with it all. I guess this was a slippery slope because I then decided last night it would be fine to have a couple of beers, and a cheeseburger with the soft white bun at a local joint. Seemed like a great idea... sand and open air style seating, in the middle of our local tiny metropolis, on the waterfront, as the sun was setting. Figured we might actually get to relax for a moment because the kiddos would play and we could be outside in the beautiful weather. We ended up chance meeting some awesome people traveling through who were also hippie-minded, so that is great. However, this morning I felt horrible. Not hungover, but angry, bloated, depressed. And I'm up a couple of pounds. I've definitely noticed as I've moved toward optimizing again with paleo and sunlight, I am way more sensitive to any dietary deviation. Or maybe I just notice it more because it is an island rather than a state of being. Nevertheless, the crappy feelings have kicked me back on track. It's a quick flip back to serious crappy carb craving and hunger.. so i'm simultaneously repulsed by and craving after I eat refined crap. Brain fog abounds, fatigue, bad moods. I've decided to be more mindful with a food and mood journal. I'll also work on having quick grab paleo snacks for any other upcoming events or other times people would offer me eats. I can't decline crappy food if I have no alternative. I'm not at intermittent fasting yet!

I need to start hanging out with people who will offer me oysters. Hell, isn't that one of the reasons I was seduced into the lawyer life? The free food and booze at all those gatherings ain't too shabby. Also dreaming of returning to the romance days.... when my dude was courting me, he'd grill me filets and veggies, and we'd drink really peat-y scotch and red wine on his porch. And then he'd just let me babble for a while until. Those were the days.

### drezy — 2017-08-10T01:01:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-221291>

Lots of people suggest lots of tests here. I have no issue with that, but guess what... I think you just gave yourself a redox test. Your subjective experience is worth quite a bit if you know it for what it is.

I've done the same in the past and can point you to it if interested.

In my heart I package up as much patience that I can and shipped it quantum express your direction. Bet you didn't know that I can do that. I even got free shipping. \[DISCLAIMER If I end up the person counseling moderation to you then you'd better check that you're not on fire.\]

On the romance front give it a little time. Us guys have thick myelinization and, relative to your female perspective, often need air horn volume to pick up on hints. I'm 13 years married. I'm extremely fortunate to have been able to find an excellent partner. Life happens and dopamine levels drop if not maintained. Keep at what you're doing. The fact that you are noticing that you're getting noticed is a good sign. Your partner will likely pick up on this change too.

Just putting this out there - could your cycle have anything to do with the switch? I know I'm my own n=1 but after ovulation I can tolerate a lot less physically... and emotionally

As for the romance, aren't oysters an aphrodisiac?  I think as me and my husband (but mostly me) have started to get more of the sun and follow the protocols we're finding each other way more interesting haha!

### drezy — 2017-08-10T22:46:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/valeries-radical-mitochondriac-adventure.19871/post-221383>

Lard or oil
Shrimp
\[stir fry for 2 min\]
[Red curry paste](https://www.amazon.com/Mae-Ploy-Red-Curry-Paste/dp/B0091UW4QS/ref=sr_1_4_s_it?s=grocery&ie=UTF8&qid=1502392938&sr=1-4&keywords=red+curry) (check Asian markets) add according to your taste and spice tolerance
\[stir fry for 1 min\]
1/4 or a little less crushed [Dried shrimp](https://www.amazon.com/Dried-Shrimp-Medium-Each-Pack/dp/B00TBSSVKI/ref=sr_1_29_s_it?s=grocery&ie=UTF8&qid=1502392826&sr=1-29&keywords=dried+shrimp) (cheaper at Asian markets) <--- total umami bomb secret ingredient
optional (2 t sugar)
splash of water for desired paste consistency
\[mix and let sit at medium-low heat for flavors to blend and shrimp dust to absorb the fluids\]

Make sure to crush dried shrimp into pebble/ dust size. Food processors or power blenders work great here.

My boy is not really a shrimp fan unless it's my "buffalo shrimp" but this always disappears. There's never any leftovers.
