### kvond — 2017-07-28T02:26:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220255>

Hi Folks,
Jack says in CT#7 that women who are post menopause need progesterone to keep their weight down and a host of other benefits such as Prolactin-autophagy cycle. Anyone try this?

I've recently lost 30lbs by adding CT via cold showers, and the Wim Hof breathing method. My thinking is I'll use progesterone cream and continue this additive process.

Thoughts?

### drezy — 2017-07-28T03:00:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220256>

Perhaps, I'm not the main audience but 30 lbs is a big deal. Bravo.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-28T08:25:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220269>

WOW! That's incredible! What's your CT protocol? Out of interest,

### caroline — 2017-07-28T12:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220283>

> [kvond said:](/goto/post?id=220255)
>
> Hi Folks,
> Jack says in CT#7 that women who are post menopause need progesterone to keep their weight down and a host of other benefits such as Prolactin-autophagy cycle. Anyone try this?
>
> I've recently lost 30lbs by adding CT via cold showers, and the Wim Hof breathing method. My thinking is I'll use progesterone cream and continue this additive process.
>
> Thoughts?

you have made me re think what I am doing - which is pretty much nothing right now. I had stopped my progesterone thinking I really didn't need it as I am getting so much outdoor time.

Lately - I really can't shift some weight I have gained and my sleep can be iffy at times. Obviously cold showers and lots of sun and grounding may not be quite enuf for a post menopausal woman.

I am also finding that too much fat in my diet is going directly to my tummy and my weight is going up.

I struggle with what to eat these days .... I don't want too much protein either. I definitely ate too much fat this summer - huge mistake.

what are you eating Kvond?

I have been thinking of trying a collagen shake with a bunch of good stuff in it ...like some greens and eggs and numeric etc.
I would have some protein around 10:00am and then dinner around 4:00pm which is when I naturally want to eat anyway.

I do think that IF is the way to go - for me.

It would be great to hear others thoughts....... Lots of ladies here have been struggling to get this right.

and the biggie ...... reduce nnEMF!!!!

### caroline — 2017-07-28T12:32:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220284>

I gotta re read C/T#7 .... it's been too long...

### kvond — 2017-07-28T16:08:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220297>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=220269)
>
> WOW! That's incredible! What's your CT protocol? Out of interest,

My CT is fairly lame - I step into a cold shower in the morning for about 2 min. It's warm here right now, so I hope to do better in the winter. I think what made the difference is adding the Wim How breathing while I'm out in the sunrise.

I do need to up my CT, I'm having significant cervical degeneration and facing another fusion - don't know what gains can make but I want to try.

### kvond — 2017-07-28T16:14:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220298>

As to food - if I get the sunlight - I notice I'm not as hungry and can get used to less pretty comfortably. Right now I have a bone broth protein shake with ketones around 8:30 in the morning, a small cup of coffee mid day to keep me alert - then try to hold off 'til dinner which is meat, greens and some fats. I want to IF but my adrenals are fairly shot still and I notice this begins to really tax me.

Recently I gained a few pounds back because I was comforting myself with carbs over the neck issue. Time to get back to mostly keto.

I have been able to hack my migraines - this is a huge jump for me - I attribute this to all of the above and Byronia 30c every day for about a week took care of my morning headaches that used to flair - plus the limited diet. I know a coupdl good homeopaths if you want to add that route for any reason.

I don't imagine any medicine of any kind can be as powerful as the whole body effects of CT, Sun and exercise. I do walk each day while at work and sometimes take work outside and sit in the sun.

Finally got my goggles for the work computer to combat the nEMF - working on that piece too.

### fitness@home — 2017-07-29T15:08:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220362>

" I have been thinking of trying a collagen shake with a bunch of good stuff in it ...like some greens and eggs and numeric etc. "

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) - I have been incorporating collagen daily and drinking a salad every morning. I find it easier to get down some veggies that way. Avocado, romaine lettuce, juice of a lime, mint, and a handful of spinach (ala Gundry). Sometimes I even add a TBL of olive oil.

Menopause sometimes sucks. No longer have flashes but the weight thing is harder to fight these days. Deal with the same 12-15 lbs over and over. Time for the annual Genova Complete Hormone test and visit to my BHRT doc soon. All things considered though, menopause is just another natural stage of life. I won't give up my BHRT anytime soon though

### Sheddie — 2017-07-29T20:39:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220384>

The minute I saw the drinkable salad ingredients I knew I could do that in my blender. Because of reducing lectins I'm limited in what to pick for my SLO-food deliveries (Fresh Harvest). Lots of lettuces for me right now. (I'm loving Inger's raw fish dish, here on Forum. Lots of Ceviche recipes online if I want to change up the idea but she'll always be remembered as my Initiation! You, too, for the idea of 'drinking' salad!!)

Everything I'm learning right now is new, really new, after 5 years of going blind from cataracts with cornea-scar complications, the last 2 being more than 'legally blind.' Hardly any computer use for learning during that period. It's like I've 'been away' for an extended time but no, I just dropped out! So glad to be back, despite UV-blocking IOLs. I picked up on Gundry and Dr. Jack Kruse only recently. Was grateful to Ray Peat's work for a long time, probably since the early 90s. Missed Haidut so will bone up -- if there's time over & above the present Kruse course of learning!

At first, any change seems to take enormous amounts of time, and then, it's on automatic pilot and I don't even think about Time... Later, too, for adding any more forums! (This is only my second online forum and I don't know a lot of how they can be used with sophistication... )

Thanx for the picture -- I like DMSO a lot for skin apps; short term, though, for the body odor side effect! Hope for you a compounding pharmacy isn't using synthetic 'compounds' or carriers in the formula? Like so many drug delivery forms, the additives/adjuvants are sometimes the culprits in unsatisfactory individual results.

### fitness@home — 2017-07-30T06:33:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220408>

I use a scoop of Great Lakes collagen in my morning cup of coffee. It dissolves well and I don't even know it is there.

My protein intake is low to moderate right now. The olive oil adds some good fat to my diet plus I love the taste. I found some lemon infused OO & orange infused OO that is amazing. My local store was clearing this item out of their stock at $2 a bottle (I bought ten).

^^ I am not 100% compliant but am slowly weeding out things that inflame and/or cause me discomfort otherwise.

P.s. I bet the farmed salmon was fed grains, etc. Gundry recommends wild caught seafood, pasture raised poultry and eggs, grass fed beef, etc. Hard to find meats and eggs that grains werent part of the process unless you raise them yourself or have access to a good farmers market.

### Penny — 2017-07-31T19:20:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220537>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=220283)
>
> you have made me re think what I am doing - which is pretty much nothing right now. I had stopped my progesterone thinking I really didn't need it as I am getting so much outdoor time.
>
> Lately - I really can't shift some weight I have gained and my sleep can be iffy at times. Obviously cold showers and lots of sun and grounding may not be quite enuf for a post menopausal woman.
>
> I am also finding that too much fat in my diet is going directly to my tummy and my weight is going up.
>
> I struggle with what to eat these days .... I don't want too much protein either. I definitely ate too much fat this summer - huge mistake.
>
> what are you eating Kvond?
>
> I have been thinking of trying a collagen shake with a bunch of good stuff in it ...like some greens and eggs and numeric etc.
> I would have some protein around 10:00am and then dinner around 4:00pm which is when I naturally want to eat anyway.
>
> I do think that IF is the way to go - for me.
>
> It would be great to hear others thoughts....... Lots of ladies here have been struggling to get this right.
>
> and the biggie ...... reduce nnEMF!!!!

Try Penny's morning coffee shake... this will last you 8 hours - wrong time of year for it - obviously - but if you want to intermittent fast, this is the way to go:
8-16 oz coffee
1/2 can coconut cream
5 Brazil nuts
10 almonds
10 walnuts
2 oz pumpkin seeds
1 oz sunflower seeds
1 tsp turmeric
some black pepper (to make the turmeric systemic - gut issues, you might not want to do this)
3 eggs (omega 3 flax fed ones are great)
cardamom (helps your P450 enzymes clear the coffee)
cinnamon (blood sugar)
1 tsp cocoa powder (polyphenols)
ginger powder
1 tsp glycine (helps your gut lining, helps make glutathione, helps if you've ingested lots of glyphosate and it tastes just like sugar)
some methylene blue - whatever dose you are currently doing
1 tray of ice cubes
Blast it all in a blender and down the hatch -
Then, start drinking ice water and doing face dunks and getting some morning sunshine - truly, you will have energy and not be hungry -

If you're close to the beach, jump in - best energizer there is - I've found though it is better to eat prior to jumping in - and drinking a lot of water - in fact, whenever I'm tired, I think "I'm dehydrated" it is amazing what water does for you - you can also try putting some baking soda/potassium bicarbonate (don't go nuts with this - 2 grams tops at one time) in the water - also, if you're doing fat, adding salt will help your mitochondria burn the fat as will the cold (even a face dunk)

some nettle tea would be great also for the histamine response and ensuing loss of sleep  If you did a bunch of glutamates, lemon balm is great for this too -

> P.s. I bet the farmed salmon was fed grains, etc. Gundry recommends wild caught seafood, pasture raised poultry and eggs, grass fed beef, etc. Hard to find meats and eggs that grains werent part of the process unless you raise them yourself or have access to a good farmers market.

Wonder if you could help me. @[fitness@home](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5474/)
Eating with a goal is a noble exercise.
But I like to test trying to figure out if the goal was reached.

I would like to figure out a way to test blood with the goal of finding protein status.

Not sure if
Total Protein results from
Complete Metabolic panel (14)
is the proper test?

### drezy — 2017-09-03T18:11:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-222802>

> [Debntx said:](/goto/post?id=222797)
>
> wow, this kind of explains something for me - the bag of chips things - a couple months ago i went all out on chips and noticed hip pains - i thought wthey- hard to go down stairs smoothly, get up from work chair etc... then stopped chips - miraculously pain went away not immediately but pretty dang quick - i know each person is individual but definitely chip (lays in particular ) have this affect on me

I agree with doc on the following two statements:

> It's not about food

> When you've got good redox you can eat shit on a shingle

The conclusion that I draw from this is that if I eat junk and subsequently inflame then my redox is faltering and it's like a test.

My current understanding is that when your redox is low the stomach lining (those cells that are typically most rapidly replaced) runs on senescent cells. This is what some call leaky gut.

Wild Part: When those lectins and bacteria and viruses slip on through your "leaky gut" all of your mitochondrial colonies assume that the environment is kicking your butt and that maybe they will borrow bits of exogenous viruses and bacteria (horizontal gene transfer) since they "evolve" to the environment faster. You mitochondria want to get the "answer" to the environment and will cheat so the you can pass these "answers" to the next generation. Not all "answers" passed on are considered desirable.

Related threads:
Huck and Jan are digging into Doc Gundry's work over here you might want to jump in: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-and-aortic-stenosis.7685/page-20#post-222245](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/huck-my-journey-bp-and-aortic-stenosis.7685/page-20#post-222245)

I started way back on a similar-ish blood type into Paleo into kept diet 12+ years ago. For the last 8 I've been almost eli-paleo since a mid to mid-high amount of my calories came from gulf seafood. I think that patched my gut and by dumb luck put me pretty close to epic-paleo mode.

Caveat:
I've only seen his interviews and have not read his book, but I suspect Gundry might have saved me some time on the way toward more optimal.

My N=1:
Potatoes,lentils,gluten = ouch when I'm not redoxing really well.

Bottom line:
It's not about food. All the above may help you frame it but if you stay fixated on food you run the risk of not realizing that your gut is a redox/environment indicator. If I hadn't made that realization I'd have been all \*almost\* eli-paleo and had maybe 35 extra lbs clinging to my saddlebag area. Even at 6'4" that starts to show up and I need to keep beach body going. That's my priority #1. A far-far second is making a difference in other's lives.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-03T20:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-222811>

Why are women from Venus and men from Mars with respect to light? Day and night offers changes in thickness and transparency for women. Your skin, tissues, blood plasma, collagen and cell water are all biologic semiconductors When light travels from a denser, higher refractive index medium to a less dense one, it similarly will be reflected if the the angle relative to vertical is greater than a critical angle. This phenomenon, known as total internal reflection, imposes significant restriction on the efficiency with which emitted LED's can transmit light into the air. This is why lady semiconductors are from Venus and not Mars.

Having sex and orgasim is the best way to boost your hormones naturally. Best naked on the beach in the sun. IF and increased mitophagy is essential for pretty much everything. I find methylene blue and nicotine help me for all the reasons they are known for. I block blue light, get cold exposure, exercise regularly. I’m 61, 5’5’ 112 lbs. I consume at least 1500 to 3000 calories a day and have difficulty keeping weight on. I brought myself back from a traumatic brain injury after being struck from behind while riding my road bike the van was traveling 70 miles an hour. >

> i have his book "minerals for the genetic code" and it is well worth a buy.

Josh, this fellow, Olree, is excellent. His book is on the way.

When I was researching in the 1980s, and far less information on environmental poisons was available, I recall Gallium, Thallium, Yttrium... were found in dentistry gold alloys (I had crowns); Antimony was in mascara and baby oil ; Sudan Black dye probably in the soles of my beloved flip-flops... Lanthanum and other rare earth metals were in photocopier inks (had one 20 feet from my work desk)... I'd hope today, somewhere, there's a central place to match which elements to what common, widespread products. It might make boycotting easier... Anyway, the accumulation of poisons in the body is assuredly a factor in many diseases, and their detoxification can be very hard work, including lifestyle modification, indeed. I think Ytterbium had been found in chewing tobacco... Olree takes me to another plane and his use of the Russels' material is beautiful. Only got my big toe in so far, but I feel there's much here to consider; bacterial dependence on metals for their food, and in what they produce from presence in the body. And, that a balance of consciously supplemented health-supporting minerals is taken much further as a result of Olree's genius... Of course, sunlight and photonic DNA have strong relationship, as I'm learning... Thank you.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-31T15:07:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minerals-electromagnetism-light-the-genetic-code-and-its-associated-minerals-and-polarities.19912/post-220506>

@[Sheddie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20386/) There's some more charts on [https://emineral.info/emineral/charts/](https://emineral.info/emineral/charts/) and some interesting articles.

I am really intrigued by
**_[https://emineral.info/emineral/is-obamacare-a-blessing-in-disguise-with-dr-richard-olree/](https://emineral.info/emineral/is-obamacare-a-blessing-in-disguise-with-dr-richard-olree/)
"Bill:_** _And protein for bacteria, the bifidos—they need yttrium as part of their protein sequencing, right?_
**Rich:** They need trace amounts of yttrium for their protein sequencing, from the way I… I’ll take the entire genome out of Pub Med and I had a computer program wrote that allows me to take and break down the individual amino acids, assign the mineral that we’ve got, in the book Minerals for the Genetic Code and then derive the amount of times it shows up. Well, the first time I did this I took everybody’s favorite bacteria, acidophilus—“Oh yes, I eat yogurt.” Taking antibiotics? “Yeah.” Are you taking probiotics? “Yes, I’m eating acidophilus” and I look at them. I go, “Well, that’s just one of hundreds of good bacteria your body needs.” The most important mineral needed for that, in terms of the whole genome aspect, was aluminum and I was quite disappointed because boron was quite far down the list.
And then I decided to figure out, “Why should I be so arrogant to think that life forms didn’t evolve using aluminum.” It is the third most abundant element in the crust of the Earth. And I did other bacteria but when I hit on the bifido species aluminum went from number one down to number 40 and boron went from the bottom of the chart to the top of the chart and so did yttrium. So I could look at protein sequences and say, “Well, based on the period needed to end the sentence so a protein is made, we’ve got a hydrogen based life form or we have a sulfur based life form or we have one that has high needs for yttrium to complete its life cycle.” So I look at the bifido bacteria as the species of bacteria in the immune system, found in your intestines, with the greatest need for trace amounts of yttrium from your food.
**Bill:** So practically, what happens to bacteria that don’t have any yttrium? And you can have that same problem that we’ve been discussing. You’re going to have messed up sequencing and you’re going to have a shutoff switch that doesn’t work, right?
**Rich:** Well, it just doesn’t work. They’re not able to multiple. And if they’re not able to multiply because they’re not getting the right food chain their numbers just continually drop. I took a look at a chart that was drawn up and it says when a person was born, through colostrum they had so many trillions and trillions of bifidobacterium longum and as they aged the bacteria content kept going down and down and down to when they were 80-85 they almost didn’t have any.
And there were two other linear graphs that absolutely followed that. One was HGH—human growth hormone—and the other one was pancreatic amylase. So I went in to Pub Med—homosapien—and found both pancreatic amylase and human growth hormone, took that protein sequence. He copied and pasted and did an output and those were needing high amounts of yttrium in the sequencing provided by the bacteria. So I started saying to myself, “Gosh, if these bacteria start sliding downhill then you’re going to have less and less of key hormones that keep you going” so essentially the faster we age is because we’re basically lacking good nutrition. We have food maybe passing through us or if we don’t have the right bacteria—maybe funguses, molds, yeast and things we might not even know about—growing in our gut.
**Bill:** So there is a relationship here between let’s say yttrium—as crazy as that is—and human growth hormone? That’s what you’re saying, as we age.
**Rich:** I drew that parallel."

There's traces in sea water, haven't yet found the concentrations in seafood, but its a seafood alert  and (from wiki)- human breast milk has 4 ppm.

A couple of points on this guy.......he says when you replace Mg with ioding in chlorophyll you get hemoglobin..........not true. He also says at 34 min in that Scandium molecular resonance emission occurs at 220 nm. Also not true and he followed that up with another bad error that D3 is made from 220 nm light. We know beyound a shadow of a doubt it is made by UVB and UVC light. 220nm light never falls to Earth because of the ozone layer.........If it did we'd be Mars in a few years.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-31T17:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minerals-electromagnetism-light-the-genetic-code-and-its-associated-minerals-and-polarities.19912/post-220530>

Yttrium is the element that came from the KT extinction as well. It is extremely rare on Earth except at the boundary present globally from 65 million years ago. It is very common on asteroids. This was how Alverez made his discovery in 1990.

### Sue-UK — 2017-08-01T11:03:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minerals-electromagnetism-light-the-genetic-code-and-its-associated-minerals-and-polarities.19912/post-220601>

With my mitochondriac hat on . I got an interesting chart from a preview page of Chaim T Horovitz book Biochemistry of Scandium and Yttrium. For scandium, in the human brain, in micrograms/kg, for an N=12 of normal microsomes, mitochondria and nuclei, the figures are 0.88, **7.05** and 1.8 respectively. In Alzheimer microsomes, mitochondria and nuclei the figures are 1.1, **3.9** and 1.9 (n=10). (reference Wenstrup et al 1990).

### Sheddie — 2017-08-01T16:32:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minerals-electromagnetism-light-the-genetic-code-and-its-associated-minerals-and-polarities.19912/post-220619>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=220530)
>
> Yttrium is the element that came from the KT extinction as well. It is extremely rare on Earth except at the boundary present globally from 65 million years ago. It is very common on asteroids. This was how Alverez made his discovery in 1990.

I'm going to have to be very discerning when I get the book! Thanks

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2017-08-02T13:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minerals-electromagnetism-light-the-genetic-code-and-its-associated-minerals-and-polarities.19912/post-220707>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=220529)
>
> A couple of points on this guy.......he says when you replace Mg with ioding in chlorophyll you get hemoglobin..........not true. He also says at 34 min in that Scandium molecular resonance emission occurs at 220 nm. Also not true and he followed that up with another bad error that D3 is made from 220 nm light. We know beyound a shadow of a doubt it is made by UVB and UVC light. 220nm light never falls to Earth because of the ozone layer.........If it did we'd be Mars in a few years.

i noticed that too...UVC range

### Sue-UK — 2017-08-02T14:56:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minerals-electromagnetism-light-the-genetic-code-and-its-associated-minerals-and-polarities.19912/post-220711>

I decided to try and find a spectral analysis for scandium  [https://www.nist.gov/sites/default/files/documents/srd/jpcrd352.pdf](https://www.nist.gov/sites/default/files/documents/srd/jpcrd352.pdf). The wide ranging scope of wavelengths on the wavelength and energy classification tables have caught my interest (just wish I understood the physics in the paper. ). In one of the Chaim T Horovitz tables it gives the scandium levels in hair in different parts of the world.(Collected from different studies). In micrograms/kg Czech Republic 10.4, Egypt 210. Japan 20. India (children)1.8-23.1. Nigeria 4-250. Taiwan 3.6. Another interesting one is teeth ... (Draskovic et al 1982). Dentine 18000-64000. Enamel 12300-51000, pulp 18000-52000.

# Thread: First Impressions after 24 hours of discovering Dr. Kruse & Questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-impressions-after-24-hours-of-discovering-dr-kruse-questions.19913/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### William Markham — 2017-07-28T06:40:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-impressions-after-24-hours-of-discovering-dr-kruse-questions.19913/post-220267>

I was just introduced to Dr. Kruse's ideas yesterday morning and subsequently watched 8hrs of podcasts and the Nourish Vermont talks of 2016/17

My first impression: This message needs to get out there and it was a blessing I found out about this.
While I instinctively get the importance of what is being said I am left feeling lost in the scientific lexicon. And from other feedback I have seen about people that feel the same way the reaction seems to be short and negative in nature such as "you are low dopa" or "you are just looking for a pill to take". Basically you're a lazy ass and not worth much consideration or compassion if you desire common language and a simple do's and dont's list. There are common obstacles that people need answers to that a guide would help with......without sorting thru a damn medical journal worth of info. I understand Dr Kruse needs to be impenetrable to his haters and critics. He cant leave an opening. But a "Blue light Dangers for Dummies" guide is a good....damn...idea.

Here are some scenarios and things Id like to see addressed in an overall guide. And who knows, maybe this guide is only like 10 pages:

What is the real world alternative for people with heavy eye prescriptions? Yes, we can go without corrective lenses in the morning to get our sun and that's easy enough but Im talking about after that. I myself wear contacts and hearing that I need to stop wearing them is a major blow. Your entire life and how you go about interacting with the world is very different than when wearing glasses. And if I understand this right...even glasses are a no go, really. So we need "permission" from Dr. Kruse on some alternatives. "All or nothing" will turn people away from this important information if you go purist on them. SO, what kind of compromise or alternative strategies are there? Any? Put it into a guide.

The Blue Blocker glasses confusion. I cant find a definitive specs on what we should have **written** anywhere. Ive also seen conflicting info on what amount of blue light should be blocked. "not all blue light is bad" type of thing from different sources. So when, where, and what, concerning this.... need to be definitively addressed and the subject ended.

Nutrition supplements: Supplements are useless or not? Dr Kruse has said they are a waste of money generally....but I see links for them on the website. So which is it? For example, do we need to take DHEA for adrenal/testosterone help.....or just rely on the sun? This is a financial issue and matters a lot to people on a budget. So a concrete, written answer on this is also needed.

Last, the latitude subject. Dr Kruse re-located his family because he thought it was important enough. So, a simple way to determine length of time in the sun and time of day based on your location and time of year. 90% of us arent going to move.

Those are my first impression conflicts. I am in NO WAY attacking Dr. Kruse. The guy is a gift to us all. But Im Thomas Dolby...I am Blinded by Science. We need a "Dummies guide" or people wont pursue, follow up, and apply on a large scale....and that's what is desired...yes?

Again, maybe this info is spread about the site somewhere in pieces and different threads or prescriptions. I'd just like to see it all in one place for the common person to quickly assimilate and form a plan of action right away.

P.S. I already know Im gonna be attacked by fanboys on this site but I dont care. My first impressions are real and I dont have an agenda or ego. I just wanna live this info...and maybe even be an advocate. But I cant be an advocate if I cant clearly communicate the information effectively if its full of ambiguity, full of overly scientific language, or if it's overwhelming to new minds with 100's of pages of info....packed with the aforementioned language.

Guide please.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-28T08:19:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-impressions-after-24-hours-of-discovering-dr-kruse-questions.19913/post-220268>

Hey William!

Welcome to the site. I, like you, discovered JK recently (about 2-3 months ago) and also, like you, simply devoured every fucking podcast and youtube video I could find.

I agree with some of your comments and confusion and actually I think that's really common when you come across any new health information or subject in general. I agree that a reply of low dopamine is a bit irritating. What I will say is that with time you'll get to understand more and more of it. When I went in to the raw vegan world I didn't have a fucking clue what they were all banging on about - I'm since out that world btw - but over time I got to understand it all. I realise there's way more science to this but let me expand.

Yesterday I listened to one of the podcasts with JK that actually is my least favourite. I think it may be the self-hacked one. It's my least favourite because the damn interviewer kept interrupting when JK was explaining something and it annoyed me so much I shouted at the podcast!! Here's my point: I listened to it for the third time yesterday because there's some science stuff in there and for the first time something clicked in my head. So I think repetition is the key!

I will answer some of your points based on my low dope status

My prescription is -4.75 in both eyes. I currently wear my glasses around my neck when I'm out and about and may need them. I wear them for driving and the rest of the time the world is blurry. It's my determination to fix my eyes and I know that wearing glasses won't do it so this is my work around. You may have a worse prescription - there is a thread on myopia reversed here with people with much worse prescriptions! Your idea about removing the lenses and using glasses is a good one. Steady improvements. You aren't going to hack everything 100% all at once! It's a work in progress IMO.

All artificial blue light is bad IMO. I wear the dark orange specs from Amazon every time I look at a screen. I have another daytime PRESCRIPTION set that only block worst part of spectrum but I prefer the dark orange ones every time!

Yeah, I'm confused with supplements too. I think I've seen a delineation between taking things for mito support like CoEQ10 and taking supplements that your body can make. But I agree there is a bit of confusion there.

The latitude thing for me depends on when your UVA shows up (or goes away altogether for Winter - Scotland). D Minder is useful for showing you when your UVA starts based on where you live. The main intention is to get the IR light in the AM before UV shows up then to access UV also through the day. That's my understanding. I think you'll agree the Vermont talk reiterates the need for AM sun above all else.

But yeah. Accept that you are brand new - like many of us - and will find out most of this by continuing to listen to podcasts (and relisten) and by showing up here on the forum as much as you can - you pick up SO MUCH that way!!

Hope that helps!

Hey Emma,

Thanks for the reply. I hear what you are saying. I just think a well rounded start guide would be a real asset to new people. And just as importantly, getting the practice out there and more people applying it. User friendly. I actually dont think it would be too much sweat off Dr. Kruse's back to make a guide like this. There are certain details that just need to be spelled out with a couple possible work arounds, compromises, and specific tools.

The heavy eye prescriptions I think may be the biggest issue of resistance for widespread adoption. I know for me Im just not sure how Im going to apply this practice outside the morning gaze. We need some sort of acceptable additional/alternative practice concerning that in particular. Maybe there is none I dont know.

All I know is a user friendly, adaptable PDF presentation to the masses could go a long way. Maybe it seems too commercial or unpalatable to Dr. Kruse to do this but all I know is there is over-load and an unclear path...at least for me.

### WalterNL — 2017-07-28T09:35:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-impressions-after-24-hours-of-discovering-dr-kruse-questions.19913/post-220277>

Fair points, but it's all about priorities and what is reasonably possible or necessary for every individual. That's why you'll read a lot about having to give something up to get your health back.

Most of the answers can be found on here. For instance, blue light is a normal part of sunlight, no problem there. But artificial blue is way too much around us so a problem.

Supplements can be useful but it's always N=1, requires testing etc. There is no answer that applies to everybody.

### caroline — 2017-07-28T11:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-impressions-after-24-hours-of-discovering-dr-kruse-questions.19913/post-220281>

There are easy start guides here for the newbies ....and all of us.

Dr.K.'s intention always was not to dumb down the information. If and when our health becomes a priority in our life we have to dig in and figure it out. That could mean reading things over and over and over until it becomes clear.

Lots of us have found that a blog could "click" for us a year later when we read it again. Sometimes I will swear I have never seen something before - but, of course, I have. I will be looking with "new" eyes.

This all needs to be a priority for us and we have to rise to the occasion - instead of Jack lowering his standards and catering to those who want to be spoon fed ....and given all the answers.

There have been many people over the years who have gotten really angry with Jack because he won't tell them exactly what to do and what pill to take and for how long. I have seen that many times.

When all is said and done ....to improve our health - it is all about the three legged stool.

Dr. K. will always answer questions and steer us in the right direction if we are willing to apply ourselves and learn and do......

Another thing ..... Please remember that Dr. Kruse is giving us all this for free ....... and no, it isn't easy! Generally nothing worthwhile is.

It is a pretty incredible opportunity if we are willing to put in the hard yards....

I don't have a scientific bone in my body .... but I do get the three legged stool - that is my compass for my life. Up until three years ago - my partner had never heard of Jack Kruse - but he gets all this intuitively. The morning after I met him - he was laying on the wet grass, naked, at sunrise.

### WalterNL — 2017-07-28T12:04:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-impressions-after-24-hours-of-discovering-dr-kruse-questions.19913/post-220282>

Exactly @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/), it takes effort. I've been reading and trying to understand for more than a year and I still haven't even scratched the surface.
Of course it helps if you slowly but steadily increase your overall health in the process, bringing into practice what you learn step by step.

> What is the real world alternative for people with heavy eye prescriptions? Yes, we can go without corrective lenses in the morning to get our sun and that's easy enough but Im talking about after that. I myself wear contacts and hearing that I need to stop wearing them is a major blow. Your entire life and how you go about interacting with the world is very different than when wearing glasses. And if I understand this right...even glasses are a no go, really. So we need "permission" from Dr. Kruse on some alternatives. "All or nothing" will turn people away from this important information if you go purist on them. SO, what kind of compromise or alternative strategies are there? Any? Put it into a guide.

About glasses; there is no answer that applies to everyone, a short guide doesn't exist. It's important to get natural sunlight on your eyes and skin without any unnatural filter blocking it, especially in the morning. The amount that you need depends on your individual situation. For instance, if you have a particular harmful job you might need to do more to offset the damage. Nobody is saying you must leave all behind and pursuit a life at the equator in the wild, but the point is that for some people, such a situation would probably be the only thing that will make them optimal. That's where your priorities come in, do you want to be optimal or are you okay (and can you cope) with less than optimal but staying in a sub-optimal environment. Most people will hang towards the latter (including myself for now), while biohacking their way through life.

Up until three years ago - my partner had never heard of Jack Kruse - but he gets all this intuitively. The morning after I met him - he was laying on the wet grass, naked, at sunrise.

I am not interested in psychological gymnastics when the work has already been done by Dr Kruse. If it cannot be broken down into a single, simple, specific and actionable document then something is wrong. I stated in my original post my questions and concerns about practical, real world application and another question about a flat out contradiction concerning supplements. Dr Kruse has put this information out there...not me. It is not incumbent upon me to go on an Easter egg hunt. If I were planning on teaching this material to students myself I would say ok....I really need to dig into the weeds here and get lost in it. But as someone interested in healing for my own purposes I dont really need to do that at this point. If a teacher wants to make things difficult on purpose so I can somehow prove my metal or commitment there is something else going on and Im not going to try and anyalyze that. Im not looking for a guru and Im not looking to join a cult or tribe.

I am recommending a simple, direct, specific PDF document that has all the facts and details I need to move ahead without sifting through hundreds of pages of info. Sure, as I would apply this information and benefit Im going to take the time to learn more about the how and why over time. But for now, I dont care. I understand and trust the basic premise and thats all that matters...but there are details that need better definition. Again, if I have to Easter egg this thing Im just gonna gaze in the morning for a few mintues and call this practice good.

### caroline — 2017-07-28T12:56:34+0300

# Thread: Sodium Bicarb & Dr Mark Sircus

### Limpei — 2017-07-28T09:53:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sodium-bicarb-dr-mark-sircus.19914/post-220278>

I was listening to Dr K's interviews with Luke Story. Another doc there, one Dr Mark Sircus advocates Sodium Bicarb (and other simple solutions) for optimal health.

He seems to be on a similar path, advocating Sun, good water and back-to-nature.

Anyone have experience with Sodium Bicarb?
Is what Dr Mark Sircus says legit?

[http://drsircus.com/general/wonderful-world-of-bicarbonate-and-co2/?utm\_source=Dr Sircus Newsletter&utm\_campaign=b8d374bf62-Article\_393\_8\_22\_2016&utm\_medium=email&utm\_term=0\_ea98c09673-b8d374bf62-10686005](http://drsircus.com/general/wonderful-world-of-bicarbonate-and-co2/?utm_source=Dr+Sircus+Newsletter&utm_campaign=b8d374bf62-Article_393_8_22_2016&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_ea98c09673-b8d374bf62-10686005)

(1)
"I and other doctors argue that **the reduction of bicarbonates in the blood is the cause of aging and diseases, not the result of aging**. As long as we can replenish bicarbonates in the blood, we don’t have to age so fast. Taking high dosages of magnesium bicarbonate is not as alkalizing as taking sodium bicarbonate but in terms of anti-aging effect magnesium bicarbonate would easily take the prize. **The most important function of alkaline water is to increase bicarbonates in the blood because we lose bicarbonates as we age**."
(2)
"Sodium bicarbonate is not only an excellent agent for natural chemotherapy, bringing as it does higher O2 levels through increased alkalinity to the cells, it is also one of the most basic medicines we have for kidney disease. **New research by British scientists at the Royal London Hospital shows that sodium bicarbonate can dramatically slow the progress of chronic kidney disease.**[\[1\]](http://drsircus.com/sodium-bicarbonate-baking-soda/healing-the-kidneys-with-sodium-bicarbonate/#_edn1) We don’t need a thousand years of tests to understand something as simple as water and it is quite the same with bicarbonate, which is always present in the best drinking waters."

_Bicarbonate acts to stimulate the ATPase by acting directly on it._[\[2\]](http://drsircus.com/sodium-bicarbonate-baking-soda/healing-the-kidneys-with-sodium-bicarbonate/#_edn2)

[\[1\]](http://drsircus.com/sodium-bicarbonate-baking-soda/healing-the-kidneys-with-sodium-bicarbonate/#_ednref1) [www.nelm.nhs.uk/en/NeLM-Area/News/2009—July/20/Bicarbonate-supplementation-may-slow-renal-decline-in-chronic-kidney-disease/](http://www.nelm.nhs.uk/en/NeLM-Area/News/2009---July/20/Bicarbonate-supplementation-may-slow-renal-decline-in-chronic-kidney-disease/)

[\[2\]](http://drsircus.com/sodium-bicarbonate-baking-soda/healing-the-kidneys-with-sodium-bicarbonate/#_ednref2) Origin of the Bicarbonate Stimulation of Torpedo Electric Organ Synaptic Vesicle ATPase. Joan E. Rothlein 1 Stanley M. Parsons. Department of Chemistry and the Marine Science Institute, University of California, Santa Barbara, Santa Barbara, California, U.S.A.

(3)
"bicarbonates medical profile, which includes rushing more Oxygen to cancer cells through radical pH shifts and increased CO2 levels."
[http://drsircus.com/cancer/vernon-j...ail&utm\_term=0\_ea98c09673-b8d374bf62-10686005](http://drsircus.com/cancer/vernon-johnston-cancer-cure/?utm_source=Dr%20Sircus%20Newsletter&utm_campaign=b8d374bf62-Article_393_8_22_2016&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_ea98c09673-b8d374bf62-10686005)

### Limpei — 2017-07-28T10:14:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sodium-bicarb-dr-mark-sircus.19914/post-220279>

I found this post by Dr K,
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-journal-of-a-madman.16871/page-2#post-178079](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/diary-i-mean-journal-of-a-madman.16871/page-2#post-178079)

"Psoriasis needs full spectrum UVB/UVA/IR and lots of spring water due to sodium bicarb for its power in the eye clock and on ocular melatonin generation.
Bicarbonate alters how we see and how the eye clock functions by modifying the visual signal generated by rod and [cone photoreceptors](http://phys.org/tags/cone+photoreceptors/) that detect light. The RPE controls the recycling and health of both rods and cones in different parts of the day.
Once lost, rods and cones are not replaced in humans, so an irreversible blindness is the outcome and a life time of circadian diseases that all tie back to epigenetic changes induced by cGMP mechanisms.
**Bicarb increases cGMP and this helps light transduction, especially UV light transduction."
**
In my layman understanding, Sodium Bicarb helps with absorption of UVA/UVB/IR (which by now, I know is a big positive)

I would just spray the mag chloride on your body - I mix epsom salts with some mag oil after I bathe, then I chase it with some sodium bicarbonate - my skin heats up, then I do a cold shower - after that, you will want to take a cold shower
Nuts have a lot of magnesium in them - you need magnesium for magnesium ATP-ase - the enzyme at the end of your mitochondrial respiratory chain that makes ATP - but really, more importantly is to not get dehydrated, because then the existing magnesium has nothing to kling to - so concentrate on drinking lots of ice water If you just drink Gerolsteiner or Peligrino water, they have mag already in there...

### Sun Disciple — 2017-09-26T18:54:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sodium-bicarb-dr-mark-sircus.19914/post-224326>

> [Limpei said:](/goto/post?id=224307)
>
> Hey Sun Disciple,
> Question about faraday cages, you seem to know more about it than me.
> If you slept in a faraday cage sleeping bag (mercola facepalm moments aside haha), shouldn't it shield you while you are inside the bag?
> (That's what it's supposed to do isn't it?)
>
> I've been toying with the idea of shielding the flat I'm in with aluminium foil/wire mesh in the walls. I read on facebook that Dr K did similar.
> Mainly for the family, since 5G is pretty much an eventuality. Some is better than none I reason.
>
> BUT, I am also thinking, it's impossible to have complete shielding (windows and all)... then wouldn't the nnEMF coming from the windows bounce around the house and zap us all?
> Kinda like wifi in a plane.
>
> What's your input on this?

Id just like to preface my response by saing im not an expert but from what ive learned ive got 2 issues with the sleeping bag idea.
The first being every faraday cage is an antenna for electric fields so If your using it in the woods it might not be an issue but most people would want to take them to hotels or when sleeping abroad. When you use a faraday cage in your sleep area, it is advised that you cut the breaker to your room to eliminate the fields coming from the walls ceillings and even cut the breakers to anything above or below you. In order to make sure you really have to measure body voltage or current running through you while inside.
The second issue is that im assuming the sleeping bag is not completley encassing you like a cacoon. If this is the case then any RF in the room is going to enter the bag and french fry you.
Same with any house shielding your gonna install you really need to hire a professional because any holes in your shielding might make things worse. You also want the expensive meters to get a really accurate reading. Also If your goal is 5g protection im not even sure there are products that are on the market that will protect 100% yet. Its just too early to know what the power density is gonna be till it gets here.

Look for MOM that has no flavoring and no ingredients other than purified water. If the bottle has listed under inactive ingredients sodium hypochlorite (bleach) then you don't want to use it. Under inactive ingredients it should only say purified water.

MOM comes in different strengths. Get the bottle that has 400 mg per teaspoon (5 ml) or it will read 1200 mg per 15 ml (1 tablespoon). The water listed as the "inactive ingredient" should be 41.7% by weight.

This will provide in 1 tablespoon (1 TBS = 15 ml), 1200 mg of Mg of magnesium. You will be using 45 ml or three tablespoons for a total of 3600 mg in the 1 liter bottle.

**1 Liter Carbonated Water.**

The magnesium bicarbonate water recipe requires unflavored carbonated water or club soda. Examples include Schweppes Club Soda and Canada Dry Seltzer. It will only have water and carbon dioxide. The club soda has a small amount of sodium.

The third thing we recommend is a glass 1 gallon jug.

**1 Gallon Glass Bottle**

The 1 liter bottle of carbonated water will serve to mix the concentrate. The concentrate needs to be diluted to drinking strength. Most people, following the Water Cures Protocol, drink their water at room temperature. To avoid any risk of plastic leaching, the glass bottle is the safest way to store water.

**Magnesium Bicarbonate Water Recipe**
Chill the carbonated water for at least 1 hour in the fridge.

Shake the MOM vigorously. In the soufflé cup that comes with the bottle, measure out 3 TBSP - 45 ml.

Take the refrigerated bottle of carbonated water and remove the cap. The less CO2 lost the better so do not stop to watch the tiny bubbles.

Pour the 3 TBSP - 45 ml of MOM into the 1 liter bottle. Replace the cap.

Shake the bottle well. Shake it for about 15 to 30 seconds. The bottle will be cloudy. This is part of the process. Return to the refrigerator. If you are a practical joker, then you can have some fun as you watch peoples eyes get big as you shake a bottle of seltzer water for a half minute. Or, you can see if you truly have the trust of a loved one as you ask them to shake it before putting it in the fridge.

After about 30 minutes, again shake the 1 liter bottle. Any MOM at the bottom will now be mixed. The bottle may become cloudy again. Shake for 15 to 30 seconds.

No need to worry about a soda explosion. The chemical reaction will make the bottle cave in slightly.

**The Magnesium Water Concentrate**

You now have dissolved (ionized) magnesium hydroxide and bicarbonate. No need to worry if there is any un-dissolved magnesium hydroxide on the bottom.

[http://www.watercures.org/magnesium-bicarbonate-water-recipe.html](http://www.watercures.org/magnesium-bicarbonate-water-recipe.html)
​

You can also buy magneisum carbonate light from a lab supplier, buy a soda charger (that you make soda water with) and then express the carbonated water into a bottle......put in 2 teas spoons of Magnesium carbonate light and put the id on and shut it up. Shake it a few time over 12 hours and you have a Magnesium Bi carbonate. Which is what you are drinking when drinking natural spring water (with other minerals of course). It is worth noting that it is only in a bi-carbonate form that all alkaline minerals are soluble.

### Jason Coates — 2018-05-16T11:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sodium-bicarb-dr-mark-sircus.19914/post-241327>

Well, I have been struggling with gout over the last year. Tried cider vinegar and the doctor insisted I go onto Allopurinol which I did for 3 months this year, begrudgingly. Did not do much for me. I have used Colchicine when most acute and once or twice when in much pain - an NSAID. I know. I have 1 cheat meal a week usually including alcohol which I have reduced. Over the last 3 weeks I have been using a propriety mixture of Sodium Bicarbonate, anhydrous Citric Acid and Sodium Carbonate (Eno Sparkling Antacid.) Goodbye gout symptoms. I looked up the Kreb's Cycle today and found this dude - coincidentally mentioned above. I do not know anything about Kreb's bicycle to understand whether Sircus is a clown or not.
I now liberally sprinkle lemon juice on my breakfast or make a very lemony Hollandaise as well.

### Billybats — 2017-10-07T18:18:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-blockers-for-ipad-computer-and-phone.19915/post-225204>

Hey.....

Just finished putting the blue blocker shield on my TV last night. My boyfriend offered to help put it on but he was working late and the rest of the weekend so I did it myself because I didnt want to have to wear my blue blocker ggoggles. . My boyfriend calls me goggles when I wear them. So he looks at the TV when he got home and I said your suppose to use it at sunset and he said why not leave it on instead of taking on and off. I said I guess so, it will help eye strain too. So is it okay to keep it on all the time day and night. Is it okay to leave it on 24/7. Another odd thing was, I was having gallstone pain and wasn't up to morning sun, so 2 minutes after thinking this not saying a word to him he texted me saying " go get your morning sun". He isn't home right now he is working. That is how connected we are and I guess what I have been doing and saying what Dr. Kruse says is resonating. Next thing on my list is change bulbs to anber and red. Then eventually move.

### BPS — 2017-07-29T10:07:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-with-skin-yeast-issues.19918/post-220352>

Hi

I’ve been struggling to fix the last 10% of my skin symptoms. I am looking deeply at leaky gut and healing it

Stool tests have shown high yeast.

So far I’m doing the following which ticks a lot of boxes on the LeakyGutRX

No grains or dairy
No nightshades or legumes
Lots of grass fed meat
Salmon daily (now adding in more seafood too)
Bone broth frequently
Bed by 10pm.
Morning walk at 7am
Lunchtime walk at 1pm (both in tshirt with no eye protection)

Desk job. Have setup desk to minimise emf, blue light filters, Iris on laptop but I do use phone quite a bit. Too much. Albeit with screen filters.

Current supplements:
Magnesium 400mg at night
500mg Ester C vit c
44mg Zinc
2000mg Turmeric

Then over the last few months I’ve been doing yeast specifics:

Antifungals rotates between CandidaStat (GSE, Garlic, Caprylic Acid, Oregon grape root), SF722 and GSE
25mil 12 strain probiotic x 2/day
Soil based probiotic x 2/day
Vitafibre prebiotic x 2/day
Goats colostrum.
x 2/day

I’ve been taking the above Candida supps for about 3 months but I’m suffering from bad symptoms. Swollen and crusty eye lids, jock+anal itching.

Digestion isn’t too bad, occasional constipation but mostly good stools.

I am well aware of die off but this doesn’t seem to be going.

As I’m very sensitive to foods, I know that grapefruit can be an issue for me but I’ve not had much other alternatives as Im resistant to oregano oil.

Since the start of the year I’ve focused on sunlight. Had 4 weeks of holidays near the equator and sun usually shifts thugs but this year I still had skin issues. In the past this has correlated to yeast issues.

So, what am I missing? Half of me says stop the yeast supps as you’re reacting to them the other half says keep going....?

I was thinking of buying a PEMF product. It's a small and portable device that you carry with you. The product's "blurb" says that it -

"generates clinically-proven, time-activated pulsed magnetic (PEMF) and light fields through the day and night that synchronise with the body's 24 hour biological clock."

Any thoughts about this please? Thanks a lot for any comments.

### OptimalSkr — 2017-07-30T01:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pulsed-electromagnetic-field-pemf-therapy-any-thoughts-on-its-benefits.19922/post-220397>

### drezy — 2017-07-30T01:33:40+0300

> [JKLearner123 said:](/goto/post?id=220383)
>
> Any thoughts about this please? Thanks a lot for any comments.

If you are a technical guy we can get more technical. But this might suffice.

Say someone placed a disruptive device(it's really just entropy) on my hormonal organs (hypothalamus, thyroid,...). The only way the device won't activate is if it is exposed to a symphony (the circadian and magnetic signaling of the earth).

Could I maybe just play an MP3 recording of the orchestra? Sure. If I was standing at a construction site near a jackhammer would it work as well? Nope.

Over time the more doodads, gizmos, and supplements I used turned out to be masking a change I needed to make.

### JKLearner123 — 2017-07-30T10:50:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pulsed-electromagnetic-field-pemf-therapy-any-thoughts-on-its-benefits.19922/post-220420>

Thanks a lot for all comments. You've saved me some money. I won't buy the PEMF device. I got up early today, and went for a cold swim in the sea. I'm learning.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-30T15:25:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pulsed-electromagnetic-field-pemf-therapy-any-thoughts-on-its-benefits.19922/post-220432>

### Sheddie — 2017-07-30T18:18:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pulsed-electromagnetic-field-pemf-therapy-any-thoughts-on-its-benefits.19922/post-220456>

Did that. Thanks!

Got enough to scrap my idea to pull out my far-from-portable Curatron clinical model with full size mat; that I was wondering if it would be a good idea (looks like, not) since it can be programmed to put out 7.8 Hz... Magnetico, up ahead...

### JKLearner123 — 2017-07-31T14:02:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pulsed-electromagnetic-field-pemf-therapy-any-thoughts-on-its-benefits.19922/post-220503>

Went for cold sea swim at 5:40AM, as the sun was rising over the horizon. Got head refreshingly cold. Remembered comments from JK - probably from the many YT interviews I've watched - as I went to get out of the water, I turned around to face the sun (is that the idea?) and "make like a sphinx" and got all four limbs earthed, and also star gazed at the sun at what I thought/guessed was about a 15 degree angle away from looking directly at it. After the swim, a variety of seafood for breakfast.

"Full Spectrum: 42 red LEDs (**630nm, 660nm**), 18 blue LEDs (**440nm, 460nm**), 6 IR LEDs, 6 UV LEDs, 6 White Light LEDs."

Does this sound like a good idea to experiment with? Thanks a lot for any comments/thoughts.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-a-grow-light-provide-any-health-benefits.19923/post-220442>

# Thread: Embarking from NY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/embarking-from-ny.19925/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### karenr — 2017-07-29T22:18:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/embarking-from-ny.19925/post-220391>

Hi folks,
I'm seriously considering becoming a gold member soon,.. when I've made a little more headway with some of the basic principles. Although I feel more than ready in terms of willingness to do what needs to be done. Not sure if I should I post an intro here, or wait until I can start digging in with membership..

So briefly.. I'm in a very debilitated condition with a multitude of health issues, rarely going out of the house for the past 25 years, because it's such an ordeal to push through overwhelming exhaustion. So the challenges are huge on a lot of levels.

When I was less than a year old, I was beaten by a neighbor, and I think that trauma had a major impact on my developing nervous and immune system. I functioned more or less up until my 30's.

The more unusual part is that I haven't been able to do much with diet because for most of my adult life I've been unable to digest most foods, and only very small quantities before feeling way too full. Last year I was down to 75 pounds and couldn't even tolerate feedings by gastric feeding tube. This means that I live on anything I can get down, which is mainly starches. Even tiny amounts of better quality foods sit heavily in my gut, and then I can't eat sometimes for many hours afterward, feeling very sick and weak. So I'm not even near being able to implement dietary hacks in the usual way, although I've been studying Weston Price and native diet for almost 20 years.. but I know that workarounds will evolve as I embark on this work in a new way..

(Also I'm of the quantum mindset, having been a homeopathic practitioner, having read Robert Becker back in the 80s, etc., and am very plugged in to nonduality spiritual awareness that consciousness is primary, not as a belief, but actual visceral experience)..

My frustration and fear of not being able to eat enough has gone on too long.. This is why I'm excited to lean about biohacks for EMF exposure, which is one thing I CAN leverage at this point, and hopefully improving things on that front could be a way to get at where I'm stuck with the diet/appetite issue. There has to be an elegant solution on another level than the level the problem appears. I gave 30 years to struggling with the problem on its own level, believing in all the gory details, and it's time to free myself from that.

So what I'm looking for here will be some direct guidance, for my own n=1 which I have always been doing, as I have been crafting my own protocols based on research and intuitive understanding of my own body. I'm looking for guidance to facilitate my own experiments, not looking to be spoon fed, no pun intended!

However, given my severe chronic exhaustion, (and financial limitations due to living on disability insurance), its often necessary for me to make practical compromises.. I see part of the work as learning to discern where compromises are necessary due to limitations, and where the perceived limits can be stretched more than expected. Because everything is a function of perception, and what is doable is never as fixed as it appears.

So I want to look at what seems to be a ridiculously complicated situation, by seeing through to the essence of what needs to be shifted. Leaving no stone unturned, willing to look at dark and scary underpinnings of what may be holding me back, willing to do what must be done, although there probably needs to be a very gradual protocol starting from a severely decinditioned state, so I don't blow myself up in the process.

Thanks for this opportunity, I'm very hopeful. Any suggestions as to how to proceed with membership and involvement in the forum? I'm in debt up to my eyeballs, so I was thinking gold membership for a few months to get some momentum going and then re-evaluate.

### Allin — 2017-07-30T05:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/embarking-from-ny.19925/post-220405>

> [karenr said:](/goto/post?id=220391)
>
> Hi folks,
> I'm seriously considering becoming a gold member soon,.. when I've made a little more headway with some of the basic principles. Although I feel more than ready in terms of willingness to do what needs to be done. Not sure if I should I post an intro here, or wait until I can start digging in with membership..
>
> So briefly.. I'm in a very debilitated condition with a multitude of health issues, rarely going out of the house for the past 25 years, because it's such an ordeal to push through overwhelming exhaustion. So the challenges are huge on a lot of levels.
>
> When I was less than a year old, I was beaten by a neighbor, and I think that trauma had a major impact on my developing nervous and immune system. I functioned more or less up until my 30's.
>
> The more unusual part is that I haven't been able to do much with diet because for most of my adult life I've been unable to digest most foods, and only very small quantities before feeling way too full. Last year I was down to 75 pounds and couldn't even tolerate feedings by gastric feeding tube. This means that I live on anything I can get down, which is mainly starches. Even tiny amounts of better quality foods sit heavily in my gut, and then I can't eat sometimes for many hours afterward, feeling very sick and weak. So I'm not even near being able to implement dietary hacks in the usual way, although I've been studying Weston Price and native diet for almost 20 years.. but I know that workarounds will evolve as I embark on this work in a new way..
>
> (Also I'm of the quantum mindset, having been a homeopathic practitioner, having read Robert Becker back in the 80s, etc., and am very plugged in to nonduality spiritual awareness that consciousness is primary, not as a belief, but actual visceral experience)..
>
> My frustration and fear of not being able to eat enough has gone on too long.. This is why I'm excited to lean about biohacks for EMF exposure, which is one thing I CAN leverage at this point, and hopefully improving things on that front could be a way to get at where I'm stuck with the diet/appetite issue. There has to be an elegant solution on another level than the level the problem appears. I gave 30 years to struggling with the problem on its own level, believing in all the gory details, and it's time to free myself from that.
>
> So what I'm looking for here will be some direct guidance, for my own n=1 which I have always been doing, as I have been crafting my own protocols based on research and intuitive understanding of my own body. I'm looking for guidance to facilitate my own experiments, not looking to be spoon fed, no pun intended!
>
> However, given my severe chronic exhaustion, (and financial limitations due to living on disability insurance), its often necessary for me to make practical compromises.. I see part of the work as learning to discern where compromises are necessary due to limitations, and where the perceived limits can be stretched more than expected. Because everything is a function of perception, and what is doable is never as fixed as it appears.
>
> So I want to look at what seems to be a ridiculously complicated situation, by seeing through to the essence of what needs to be shifted. Leaving no stone unturned, willing to look at dark and scary underpinnings of what may be holding me back, willing to do what must be done, although there probably needs to be a very gradual protocol starting from a severely decinditioned state, so I don't blow myself up in the process.
>
> Thanks for this opportunity, I'm very hopeful. Any suggestions as to how to proceed with membership and involvement in the forum? I'm in debt up to my eyeballs, so I was thinking gold membership for a few months to get some momentum going and then re-evaluate.

_Get out into the sun with bare arms and calves, barefeet on the grass from 6-8 AM with no glasses on. Do this everyday even if overcast. Drink a little spring water when you are doing this. The sun and the earth will feed you some of the electrons you need to power and fix your body. Buy a UVa bulb and have it on when you are inside during the day. Buy a pair of Blue blocking glasses and wear them if you are watching TV, looking at a cell phone or looking at a computer screen. Also you need to put a scarf on around you neck if you are looking at a blue lit screen. Buy a half dozen candles at the 99 cent store. Turn off your lights and use a candle or two after 8 PM for light. Your investment in yourself will cost about $35-$40. Remember step by step, keep it simple and taking action everyday. Each day I mark on a calendar what I have done. Welcome to the journey._
